Local source PDFs IUT I IUT II IUT III IUT IV Formalization report Scholze-Stix note
Inter-universal Teichmuller theory

IUT as a map of alien arithmetic worlds.

Inter-universal Teichmuller theory is Mochizuki's proposed arithmetic deformation theory for number fields equipped with elliptic-curve data. It replaces direct comparison of ring/scheme structures by a network of Hodge theaters, theta links, log links, indeterminacies, and log-volume estimates.

Enter the wiki Open source library Collas category view Lean efforts
Global route source objects to Diophantine bounds

Orientation

IUT is not best approached as a single calculation. It is a theory of controlled comparison between arithmetic structures that are deliberately not kept inside one global ring or scheme history. The vocabulary is unusual because the comparison method is unusual.

Objects

The starting objects are initial theta data, hyperbolic orbicurves, Frobenioid-type structures, log-shells, and Hodge theaters.

Motion

The central motion is through theta links and log links, arranged in the log-theta lattice. These links compare weakened data rather than ordinary scheme identities.

Category Layer

Collas' account is useful as an orientation layer for IUT's categorical replacement of unavailable global scheme-theoretic objects. Use it as a guide, then check every technical object against IUT I-IV.

Output

The proof-critical output is a log-volume comparison in IUT III, Corollary 3.12. IUT IV then applies this comparison to Diophantine inequalities.

Wiki convention. This page explains the source theory and its formalization interfaces. It does not assert that the disputed proof is settled. When a statement is a conditional interface, the page says so.
Related: reading map Collas category view source library glossary

Reading Map

The best first pass is thematic, not chronological. Read the overview texts to understand why alien copies and separated structures are introduced; then read the four IUT papers with the route map below in mind.

PassThemePrimary local sourcesGoal
1 Global picture Panoramic Overview, Mutually Alien Copies, Collas overview Know why IUT uses alien copies, non-scheme-theoretic deformation, and anabelian reconstruction.
2 Source machinery IUT I, IUT II Track initial theta data, Hodge theaters, theta values, Kummer theory, and theta links.
3 Proof-critical corridor IUT III, Essential Logical Structure Locate Theorem 3.11, Step (x), Step (xi), and the Corollary 3.12 comparison.
4 Consequences IUT IV, Explicit Estimates Understand how log-volume estimates are converted into Szpiro, abc, and Vojta-type bounds.
5 Verification and critique Scholze-Stix, 2018 discussion report, 2026 Lean report Separate source claims, objections, responses, and formalization targets.
Four-paper layer map what each paper contributes to the route

Official Mochizuki IUT Corpus

The local library includes the ten papers listed under the Inter-universal Teichmuller Theory heading on Mochizuki's official papers page. The wiki treats these as the primary source spine; auxiliary papers are used for orientation, critique, formalization, and independent checks.

No.PaperWiki roleLocal source
1IUT I: Construction of Hodge TheatersInitial theta data, Frobenioids, theta-Hodge theaters, D-prime-strips.PDF
2IUT II: Hodge-Arakelov-theoretic EvaluationTheta evaluation, mono-theta environments, Gaussian monoids, theta links.PDF
3IUT III: Canonical Splittings of the Log-Theta-LatticeLog-theta lattice, Theorem 3.11, indeterminacies, Corollary 3.12.PDF
4IUT IV: Log-Volume Computations and Set-Theoretic FoundationsExplicit log-volume estimates, Diophantine consequences, species/mutations.PDF
5Panoramic Overview of IUTConceptual orientation for alien copies and non-scheme-theoretic deformation.PDF
6Bogomolov's Szpiro Proof from the IUT ViewpointComparison with the geometric Szpiro proof and the role of theta/Gaussian data.PDF
7Mathematics of Mutually Alien CopiesExtended explanation of alien copies, Gaussian analogies, and comparison discipline.PDF
8Explicit Estimates in IUTNumerical effective estimates attached to the IUT IV output layer.PDF
9Essential Logical Structure of IUTLogical decomposition of the proof and the AND/OR structure around the dispute.PDF
10IUT as an Anabelian GatewayShort report linking IUT to anabelian geometry and analytic number theory.PDF
Coverage checkpoint: all ten official IUT-category Mochizuki PDFs are present locally and linked from this wiki. The next expansion pass can therefore focus on concept pages instead of source acquisition for this core corpus.
Related: full source library preparatory corpus formalization route paper atlases

Preparatory Corpus

The 2021 IUT Summit page lists four preparatory papers next to IUT I-IV: Frobenioids I, Frobenioids II, the etale theta function paper, and Topics in Absolute Anabelian Geometry III. These sources supply infrastructure that the four IUT papers repeatedly invoke, so the offline wiki mirrors them locally.

Frobenioids I

Develops the general theory of Frobenioids as categorical abstractions of divisor and line-bundle arithmetic, including Frobenius degree, divisor monoids, model Frobenioids, perfection, realification, and birationalization.

FrdIgeneral theory

Frobenioids II

Continues the Frobenioid framework in the poly-Frobenioid direction, supplying categorical infrastructure for later prime-strip and Frobenius-like constructions.

FrdIIpoly-Frobenioids

Etale theta function

Provides the etale theta function and its Frobenioid-theoretic manifestations. This is the source behind theta evaluation and the theta-function rigidity that IUT II and later effective-estimate pages use.

EtThtheta evaluation

Absolute Anabelian Topics III

Supplies absolute anabelian reconstruction input used by the theta-approach and by IUT's recurring distinction between group-theoretic containers and reconstructed ring/scheme data.

AbsTopIIIreconstruction
Summit labelLocal sourceWiki dependency
FrdI The Geometry of Frobenioids I IUT I Frobenioid package, bad Frobenioids
FrdII The Geometry of Frobenioids II Frobenioid prime-strips, global packet Frobenioids
EtTh The Etale Theta Function and its Frobenioid-theoretic Manifestations theta evaluation, μ6 evaluation points
AbsTopIII Topics in Absolute Anabelian Geometry III theta approach to absolute anabelian algorithms, Frobenioid reconstruction
Audit rule: when an IUT page says that a Frobenioid, theta function, or reconstruction algorithm is available by earlier theory, the relevant local preparatory paper should be cited here rather than silently treated as a black box.
Source: RIMS IUT Summit 2021 source list Related: official corpus source library anabelian background

Frobenioid Standard Model

Frobenioids I begins with the elementary model F_M = M x N_{>=1}. The product law (a_1,n_1)(a_2,n_2)=(a_1+n_1 a_2,n_1 n_2) is the prototype for treating divisor data and tensor-power/Frobenius-degree data inside one category-theoretic object.

Elementary Frobenioid ingredients Frobenioids I, §I1 and Definition 1.1
IngredientFrobenioids I contentIUT wiki use
M Commutative monoid, read arithmetically as divisor or line-bundle data. Explains why later IUT Frobenioids track divisor monoids separately from fields.
N_{>=1} Acts on M by multiplication; records Frobenius degree. Source of the tensor-power/Frobenius-like direction used in later theta data.
C -> F_Phi A Frobenioid is a category substantially reflected in a functor to an elementary Frobenioid. Prevents treating a Frobenioid as just a label or ordinary category.
Source: Frobenioids I, §I1 and Definition 1.1 Related: preparatory corpus IUT I Frobenioid package model Frobenioid construction

Frobenioid Reconstruction Program

The main Frobenioids I program is reconstruction of the functor C -> F_Phi from the category C itself, under technical hypotheses. Sections 3 and 4 split the task into reconstruction of the base category and Frobenius degree, then reconstruction of the divisor monoid.

Category C

Start from the abstract category, not from its original scheme-theoretic construction history.

Base and degree

Theorem 3.4 reconstructs the base category and Frobenius-degree portion.

Divisor monoid

Theorem 4.9 reconstructs the divisor monoid under non-dilation and related conditions.

Frobenioid structure

Corollary-level reconstruction recovers the functor that makes C a Frobenioid.

Source componentFunctionAudit consequence
Irreducible morphisms Proposition 1.14 characterizes prime-Frobenius morphisms, steps, and base irreducibles. Later IUT code/wiki pages should keep morphism types separated.
Slim exponentiation Technique used in the category-theoretic recovery of base and degree data. Signals that Frobenioid reconstruction is not a mere projection field.
Technical hypotheses Examples show that portions of the structure need not be preserved without hypotheses. Do not turn reconstruction availability into an unconditional black box.
This preparatory page is directly relevant to formalization: a Lean record that only stores “there exists a Frobenioid” misses the reconstruction theorem's content. The source distinguishes base data, Frobenius degree, divisor monoid, and morphism types.
Source: Frobenioids I, §§1-4 Related: standard model IUT I Frobenioid reconstruction IUT II Frobenioid route

Frobenioid Operations

Frobenioids I uses three operation types repeatedly: perfection, realification, and birationalization. In the elementary model they correspond to replacing Z_{>=0} by Q_{>=0}, R_{>=0}, or Z. IUT later uses these operation types whenever divisor monoids are rationalized, realified, or passed to rational functions.

OperationElementary effectGeometric readingIUT use
Perfection Z_{>=0} -> Q_{>=0} Allow rational divisor scaling. Rationalized Frobenius/divisor data.
Realification Z_{>=0} -> R_{>=0} Real-valued divisor/degree container. Log-volume and “currency exchange” among local divisor monoids.
Birationalization Z_{>=0} -> Z Category-theoretic analogue of rational functions. Frobenioid-theoretic theta functions and Kummer comparison.
The operations are preparatory infrastructure. A later IUT page that mentions a realified or birational Frobenioid should link back here when the operation, rather than the specific IUT object, is the point.
Source: Frobenioids I, §I1 Related: global Cmod realification theta-value birationalization D-side realifications

Etale Theta Mono-Environment

The etale-theta paper introduces a mono-theta environment as a Kummer-theoretic version of the classical theta trivialization on a Tate curve. It is designed as a common bridge between the tempered etale fundamental-group route and the tempered Frobenioid route to the etale theta function.

Common core for two theta routes Etale Theta, Introduction and Definition 2.13
Source: Etale Theta, Introduction and Definition 2.13 Related: IUT II standard type mono-theta environments Frobenioid standard model

Etale Theta Rigidity Triad

The mono-theta environment is important because it satisfies three rigidity properties: cyclotomic rigidity, discrete rigidity, and constant-multiple rigidity. Mochizuki stresses that the cyclotomic rigidity depends on the first-power theta class and fails in the same form for higher powers.

RigiditySource readingWhy it matters later
Cyclotomic rigidity Identifies the relevant cyclotome through the theta class and a commutator computation. Controls cyclotomic identifications in theta/Kummer transport.
Discrete rigidity Keeps the integral/discrete structure visible in the mono-theta package. Prevents replacing the package by a purely profinite flat object.
Constant-multiple rigidity Controls multiplication of theta data by constants without arbitrary choices. Feeds IUT II constant-multiple rigidity and theta-monoid comparison.
Source warning: the paper says the cyclotomic rigidity mechanism fails in the same form for an M-th power of the l-th root of theta when M > 1. The wiki should therefore keep “first-power theta class” visible whenever this rigidity is cited.
Source: Etale Theta, Corollary 2.19 and Remark 2.19.2 Related: mono-theta environment constant-multiple rigidity theta monoid multiradiality

Bi-Kummer Theory

Section 4 of the etale-theta paper develops bi-Kummer theory: a category-theoretic generalization of the Kummer class of a rational function to Kummer data attached to a pair of sections, interpreted as numerator and denominator of a rational function. Section 5 then specializes this to the etale theta function.

Rational function pattern

Ordinary Kummer theory attaches a class to a rational function.

Pair of sections

Bi-Kummer data uses numerator and denominator sections of a line bundle.

Tempered Frobenioid

The construction is formulated for the Frobenioid-theoretic setting.

Etale theta specialization

The theta function becomes accessible through this categorical Kummer route.

This page is a preparatory dependency page. The later IUT II pages record the specific theta and Gaussian monoids; this page records the underlying Kummer-theoretic construction principle.
Source: Etale Theta, §§4-5 Related: Frobenioid theta monoids Kummer evaluation square etale theta rigidity

Absolute Anabelian Reconstruction Algorithms

Topics in Absolute Anabelian Geometry III uses “group-theoretic algorithm” in a strong sense: the procedure is phrased in the language of a single topological fundamental group, not by comparison with a second reference scheme. Theorem 1.9 reconstructs the NF-portion of a function field by Belyi cuspidalization; Corollary 1.10 extends this to the function field over mixed local fields.

Input group

A single extension or group such as Π_X.

Belyi cuspidalization

Construct cusps, decomposition groups, and cyclotomic identifications.

Kummer image

Recover multiplicative function-field data inside cohomology.

Additive structure

Recover K_X^× ∪ {0} as a field-like object.

ResultSource readingWiki consequence
Theorem 1.9 Reconstructs the NF-portion of the function field of a strictly Belyi-type hyperbolic orbicurve over a sub-p-adic field. Source for “algorithm” pages: construction steps must be explicit, not only invariant under isomorphism.
Corollary 1.10 Reconstructs function-field and Kummer data for hyperbolic orbicurves over mixed local fields. Connects local reconstruction to the mono-anabelian route used by log-Frobenius compatibility.
Remark 1.9.8 Contrasts mono-anabelian “software” with ordinary bi-anabelian preservation under isomorphism. Prevents the wiki from treating model-comparison data as equivalent to source-free reconstruction.
Source: Absolute Anabelian Topics III, Theorem 1.9 and Corollary 1.10 Related: theta approach to absolute anabelian algorithms Collas algorithmic anabelian log-Frobenius compatibility

Cores and Telecores

Definition 3.5 introduces diagrams of categories, families of homotopies, observables, cores, and telecores. A core records a portion of the diagram that remains constant under the operations in question. A telecore is a delayed or partial core structure: compatibility is recovered only after returning through the core vertex.

Constant core and delayed telecore compatibility Absolute Anabelian Topics III, Definition 3.5 and Remark 3.5.1
TermSource readingIUT relevance
Observable Projects operations between different data types into morphisms inside a single category. Needed when a functor is categorically trivial but operationally nontrivial.
Core A constant portion of a diagram, invariant up to the specified homotopies. Models the group-theoretic part that survives log-Frobenius operations.
Telecore A partial section to the core whose compatibility takes effect after a time lag. Explains how temporary incompatibilities can be routed through the mono-anabelian core.
Source: Absolute Anabelian Topics III, Definition 3.5 Related: log-Frobenius compatibility RIMS four-triangle route Collas category view

Mono-Anabelian Log-Frobenius Compatibility

Corollary 3.6 formulates a mono-anabelian log-Frobenius compatibility: Galois-theoretic data can remain constant through operations that obliterate ring structure. Corollary 3.7 gives the corresponding bi-anabelian incompatibility, where a fixed reference model cannot simultaneously be core, comparable to log-subject copies, and consistently observable.

RouteWhat survives logWarning
Mono-anabelian The single-group reconstruction core remains available for re-running the algorithm after log. The ring structure is still obliterated; compatibility is through the group-theoretic core.
Bi-anabelian A reference-model comparison can be made in ordinary anabelian fashion. The model-core, comparability, and log-observability requirements are not simultaneously compatible.
IUT reading The later Hodge-theater route must use the source-free reconstruction logic, not an implicit reference scheme. This is preparatory support, not by itself the Corollary 3.12 proof.
The source explicitly says the log-Frobenius functor obliterates field or ring structure. The positive statement is therefore not “log preserves rings”; it is that the mono-anabelian group-theoretic core supplies a log-invariant framework.
Source: Absolute Anabelian Topics III, Corollaries 3.6 and 3.7 Related: cores and telecores Scholze-Stix critique Corollary 3.12

Fundamental Properties of Log-Shells

Corollary 5.10 is the main log-shell result of Topics III. It records four properties: finite log-volume, compatibility of log-volumes with the log-Frobenius functor, compatibility with panalocalization, and compatibility with mono-analyticization. These properties explain why log-shells function as rigid local integral structures after the ring structure has been disabled.

PropertySource readingLater use
Finite log-volume Log-shells are compact enough to carry finite log-volume data. Feeds the signed log-volume bookkeeping used by IUT III-IV.
Log-Frobenius compatibility Log-volume at one lattice index is compatible with the log-volume at the next index. Prepares the vertical log-link side of the log-theta lattice.
Panalocalization Log-shell construction remains compatible after restricting to disjoint local prime data. Allows local prime data to be treated without reintroducing global field history.
Mono-analyticization Log-shells remain meaningful after disabling one combinatorial dimension of a ring. Supports movement between arithmetic holomorphic structures and a common mono-analytic core.
Source: Absolute Anabelian Topics III, Corollary 5.10 Related: log-shells log-theta lattice Collas height universes

2010 Dilation Program

Mochizuki's 2010 progress report presents IUT first as an arithmetic analogue of Teichmuller deformation. A ring is viewed as a one-dimensional arithmetic holomorphic structure with two underlying combinatorial dimensions: addition and multiplication. The intended deformation dilates the valuation-group direction by theta-function data while leaving unit data undilated.

Arithmetic Teichmuller analogy one arithmetic holomorphic structure, two underlying dimensions
Classical model

One holomorphic dimension has two real directions; one is dilated and one is fixed.

Arithmetic model

A ring is read as addition plus multiplication, two combinatorial directions inside one arithmetic holomorphic structure.

IUT deformation

The value-group direction is theta-dilated while the unit direction remains the fixed core.

Progress-report itemMeaning in 2010Later wiki target
Earlier Teichmuller theories Classical and p-adic Teichmuller theories motivate deformation with one direction dilated and another fixed. classical roots route, p-adic analogy
Two arithmetic dimensions Addition and multiplication are treated as separate combinatorial directions of ring structure. two ring dimensions, theta/log links
Units and value group The local-field picture separates O_k^* from the value group. log-shells, theta evaluation sections
Log-theta lattice Horizontal theta links and vertical log links organize the deformation. log-theta lattice, IUT III tensor packets
Source: IUT Progress Report 2010, §§1-3 Related: 2010 expected results Invitation route Collas category view

2010 Expected Results

The 2010 report states the then-expected main theorem as an explicit algorithmic description, up to mild indeterminacies, of the left side of the theta link relative to the alien ring structure on the right side. It then states the expected corollary: a volume computation of the algorithmic output should yield the Szpiro, hence abc, inequality.

Theta-link input

The left-hand theta values indexed by q^(j^2) are read relative to the alien ring structure on the right.

Anabelian algorithm

Only abstract topological groups survive direct passage through log and theta links; anabelian reconstruction supplies the comparison data.

Volume computation

The algorithmic output is subjected to a log-volume calculation expected to imply Szpiro/abc.

Abstract groups after links

The report already stresses that log and theta links are incompatible with ring structures and scheme-theoretic basepoints. Across links, one can only compare local fundamental groups and Galois groups as abstract topological groups before applying anabelian geometry.

§4inter-universality

Coric Galois action

The stated key point for the expected main theorem is the coric nature of the action of the local Galois group on unit data. This anticipates later language about coricity, mono-anabelian reconstruction, and multiradial invariance.

§5expected theorem

Volume corollary

The expected corollary is not stated as a direct scheme-theoretic inequality. It depends on performing a volume computation on the output of the algorithms described by the expected main theorem.

§5Szpiro/abc
3.12 relevance. The later Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12 corridor is the mature form of this early expected-theorem-to-volume-corollary pattern: first obtain a multiradial, source-faithful description of theta-side data; only then pass to a log-volume comparison.
Source: IUT Progress Report 2010, §§4-5 Related: Theorem 3.11 Corollary 3.12 2026 four-triangle route

Invitation Route

The expanded Invitation notes give a compact route into IUT before the four main papers: Hodge-Arakelov motivation, Teichmuller-style deformation, log-theta-lattice structure, and the need for anabelian reconstruction across non-ring-compatible links.

Four-section route from Hodge-Arakelov motivation to inter-universality

Hodge-Arakelov motivation

The notes start from a hypothetical global multiplicative subspace and generators for an elliptic curve. The Hodge-Arakelov calculation suggests the assignment {q^{j^2}} -> q and motivates the desired height estimate.

Invitation §1height

Teichmuller-style deformation

Mochizuki compares IUT to Teichmuller deformation: one keeps a common underlying structure while dilating one dimension. In the arithmetic setting, value groups are dilated by theta-function data while unit data is left undilated.

Invitation §2dilation

Log-theta lattice sketch

Section 3 presents the log-theta lattice as a noncommutative two-dimensional diagram of Hodge theaters. Vertical log links and horizontal theta links correspond to the two combinatorial dimensions of local arithmetic structure.

Invitation §3lattice

Inter-universality and anabelian geometry

The log and theta links are not compatible with ring structures. On the opposite side of such a link, local fundamental groups and Galois groups make sense as abstract topological groups, so anabelian reconstruction is required to compare the alien ring structures.

Invitation §4anabelian
Sources: Invitation to IUT, expanded Related: classical roots route Hodge theaters theta/log links log-theta lattice anabelian background

Invitation Hodge-Arakelov Obstruction

Invitation §1 starts with a conditional calculation. Locally, a Tate curve has a canonical multiplicative subspace in its l-torsion and canonical generators on the quotient. If analogous global data existed for the elliptic curve over the number field, Hodge-Arakelov theory would produce a height estimate by ordinary arithmetic line-bundle methods. The obstruction is precisely that these global objects generally do not exist.

Local Tate input

The Tate-curve torsion sequence supplies local multiplicative subspaces and quotient generators at bad multiplicative places.

Hypothetical global input

Assume, only for motivation, compatible global multiplicative subspace and generator data over the number field.

Height computation

Hodge-Arakelov evaluation and projection toward q^(j^2) factors suggest a bound for the elliptic height.

Invitation ingredientRoleDo not confuse with
0 -> mu_l -> E[l] -> Z/lZ -> 0 Local Tate-curve model for the multiplicative subspace and generator picture. A globally available splitting for the actual number-field curve.
Hodge-Arakelov evaluation Produces a direct-sum side weighted by theta-type factors q^(j^2). The later IUT theta-link itself; this is the motivational scheme-theoretic precursor.
Arithmetic Kodaira-Spencer morphism Projection to the weighted factors suggests the height bound in the hypothetical setting. A proof in the genuine global situation without further IUT machinery.
Formalization warning. A Lean model should not encode the hypothetical global multiplicative subspace as data available in the real IUT situation. It is a motivating obstruction that later Hodge theaters simulate.
Source: Invitation to IUT, expanded, §1 Related: Invitation distortion Collas GMS categorification global multiplicative subspaces

Invitation Distortion

After the hypothetical Hodge-Arakelov argument fails globally, Invitation §1 replaces it by a deformation problem. The assignment from the theta-value family to the q-parameter is treated as if it were a quasiconformal comparison between distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures, not as an automorphism of one number field. The task of IUT is then to compute the resulting distortion.

Impossible automorphism

The notes first describe an impossible shortcut: if the assignment from the theta-value family to q came from a number-field automorphism, degree preservation would give the desired estimate.

Distinct copies

Since no such automorphism exists, the two sides are placed in distinct copies of conventional ring/scheme theory, i.e. distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures.

Measured distortion

The later computation bounds the distortion attached to the j-indexed portion by log-differential data and aims at the Szpiro/abc inequality.

3.12 relevance. This is the informal ancestor of the dispute corridor: one may not collapse the two sides into one ring-theoretic copy before the permitted multiradial comparison and log-volume bound have been constructed.
Source: Invitation to IUT, expanded, §1 Related: 2010 expected results Corollary 3.12 Scholze-Stix critique

Invitation Hodge-Theater Bookkeeping

Invitation §3 describes a Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater as a model of the usual scheme-theoretic arithmetic geometry around an elliptic curve over a number field, but rebuilt from abstract monoids, Galois groups, and arithmetic fundamental groups. Its stated purpose is bookkeeping for l-torsion labels, so that IUT can simulate the global multiplicative subspace and generators that were unavailable in §1.

Source curve

An elliptic curve over a number field and its localizations provide the ordinary arithmetic-geometric input.

Abstract package

The theater stores monoids, Galois groups, arithmetic fundamental groups, and finite-label symmetries.

Simulation role

The package simulates the missing global multiplicative subspace/generator data without asserting that such data exist globally.

Label symmetryInvitation roleLater source route
F_l^* / {+/-1} Multiplicative label symmetry for the half-indexed nonzero labels. multiplicative versus additive symmetry
F_l rtimes {+/-1} Additive signed label symmetry around the ordered torsion-label array. F_l± groups and torsors
Basepoint rearrangement The notes compare the construction to a p-adic-Hodge-theoretic tensor rearrangement of basepoints. basepoints and inter-universality
Source: Invitation to IUT, expanded, §3 Related: Hodge theaters theta-Hodge theaters HA obstruction

Invitation Kummer Bridge

Invitation §§3-4 identifies Kummer theory as the bridge between Frobenius-like monoid data and etale-like fundamental-group/Galois data. This bridge is needed because log and theta links form walls from the viewpoint of ring structure, while etale-like group data can pass through as abstract topological groups. Cyclotomic rigidity protects the cyclotome inside the monoids where theta values and realified number-field data appear.

Frobenius-like side

Abstract monoids contain unit data, theta values at torsion labels, and realified number-field information.

Etale-like side

Arithmetic fundamental groups and Galois groups provide the group-theoretic data that can cross link walls.

Cyclotomic rigidity

The theory must protect the cyclotome from unit indeterminacy when theta-value and realified data are compared.

The Invitation compares this passage to moving from Cartesian to polar coordinates in the Gaussian integral. The analogy is only structural: it marks a coordinate change from directly visible monoid data to a group-theoretic algorithmic description.
Source: Invitation to IUT, expanded, §§3-4 Related: Kummer theory cyclotomic rigidity Gaussian log-theta lattice

Panoramic Route

The 2012 panoramic overview presents IUT as a response to a precise obstacle: scheme-theoretic Hodge-Arakelov theory suggests the needed height estimate, but its useful "global multiplicative subspace" picture is not generally available. IUT replaces that unavailable scheme-theoretic situation by non-scheme-theoretic deformations and then measures the resulting distortion.

Four-part route from Hodge-Arakelov motivation to Diophantine output

Hodge-Arakelov obstruction

Section 1 reviews an idealized Hodge-Arakelov theorem whose codomain contains factors weighted by q^{j^2}. If global multiplicative subspaces and canonical generators existed generally, a height bound would follow by conventional arithmetic line-bundle arguments.

Pano §1motivation

Key assignment

The overview isolates the assignment from the family {q^{j^2}} to q as the central computational problem. Since it is not a ring homomorphism inside one conventional scheme theory, IUT treats the two sides as living in distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures.

Pano §1KEY

Coricity

Coricity is the invariance of selected structures under the deformation. The overview compares it to the underlying real-analytic surface in classical Teichmuller theory: it is the part that remains usable while holomorphic structure is changed.

Pano §1core

Prime-strips as sections

Section 3 explains prime-strips as valuation-indexed sections of Spec(K) → Spec(F). This simulates the missing global multiplicative subspace and global generator data, while forcing explicit bookkeeping of arithmetic line-bundle and Frobenioid structures.

Pano §3prime-strips

Theta-link non-ring map

In the overview, the theta-link at bad places is an isomorphism of abstract monoids sending the theta-value family to the q-parameter side. Its compatibility with local Galois groups is by an unspecified abstract isomorphism, not by an identity map of one ambient ring.

Pano §3non-ring

Jacobi/Gaussian analogy

The overview relates multiradiality of the etale theta function to the invariance of the Gaussian under Fourier transform and to Jacobi's identity. The analogy marks the role of quadratic theta data and logarithmic unit geometry in the final log-volume calculation.

Pano §4theta analogy
This panoramic route is a navigation layer. The detailed source objects are still the Hodge-theater, prime-strip, theta-link, log-link, multiradial, and log-volume constructions in the four IUT papers.
Sources: Panoramic Overview Related: mutually alien copies Hodge theaters prime-strips theta/log links log-theta lattice

Bogomolov-Szpiro Analogy

Mochizuki's 2016 paper compares IUT with Bogomolov's proof of the geometric Szpiro conjecture. The analogy is not a claim that the proofs are the same: it isolates a common pattern in which a precise degree computation must be combined with nontrivial estimates made in a larger geometric container.

Shared comparison pattern precise identity plus displacement/log-volume estimates

Upper-half-plane geometry

The paper formulates Bogomolov's proof through the geometry of the upper half-plane, universal coverings of SL2(R), and displacement estimates for lifted unipotent elements.

Bogomolov §1-3geometry

Degree plus estimate

The geometric proof combines a precise degree relation with nontrivial displacement estimates to bound orders of q-parameters. Mochizuki compares this with IUT's combination of a precise theta-link degree relation and multiradial log-volume estimates.

Bogomolov §3-4Szpiro

IUT similarities

The analogy identifies upper-half-plane boundary geometry with the additive symmetry part of Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters, and compares Bogomolov's displacement containers with IUT's mono-analytic log-shell containers.

Bogomolov §4containers

Essential difference

Section 5 stresses the absence, in Bogomolov's proof, of Gaussian distributions and theta functions. These are precisely central in IUT: theta values, Galois evaluation, and processions are not optional decorations.

Bogomolov §5theta functions

Frobenius/etale analogy

Mochizuki compares holomorphic line-bundle and discriminant-modular-form data in Bogomolov's proof with Frobenius-like objects, and local systems plus fundamental groups with etale-like objects.

Bogomolov §4Frobenius/etale

Formalization use

For the wiki and Lean work, the paper is best used as an analogy for proof shape: precise correspondences alone do not replace estimates, and estimates alone do not provide the exact source comparison.

audit useanalogy only
Sources: Bogomolov-Szpiro viewpoint Related: panoramic route mutually alien copies log-theta lattice IUT IV consequences

Classical Roots Route

Mochizuki's Classical Roots lecture notes give four classical analogies for reading IUT: isogeny invariance of elliptic-curve heights, crystals and Hodge filtrations, complex Teichmuller dilations, and the Jacobi theta identity. The page uses them as orientation maps into the source papers, not as replacements for the formal IUT constructions.

Four classical roots source analogies pointing into IUT machinery

Isogeny-height GMS model

In the hypothetical presence of a global multiplicative subspace, quotienting an elliptic curve by it gives a relation like q_E^l = q_E*, hence l log(q_E) = log(q_E*). The lecture presents Hodge theaters as the apparatus for generalizing this key point when such global objects do not exist.

Classical Roots §1GMS

Hodge-filtration container

The crystals/Hodge-filtration section uses p1*E ~= p2*E as a common container large enough to house two Hodge subbundles but small enough to yield a nontrivial height estimate. Mochizuki compares this to IUT's multiradial common container for incompatible ring structures across the theta link.

Classical Roots §2container

Complex Teichmuller dilation

Classical Teichmuller deformation keeps a common real-analytic structure while one real direction is dilated and the other is fixed. The lecture uses this to motivate the log-theta lattice's infinite-H portion, where log links act like rotations of arithmetic holomorphic structure and theta links perform the dilation-like move.

Classical Roots §3dilation

Jacobi theta identity

The identity theta(t)=t^-1/2 theta(t^-1) is read as descent to a quotient up to a factor. The lecture compares the identity with IUT's multiradial representation, the factor with indeterminacy, and the involution with the log-link rotation of holomorphic structure.

Classical Roots §4theta
Use in the wiki. These analogies are particularly useful for readers before the technical Frobenioid and Hodge-theater pages. They explain why IUT repeatedly asks for a common container that is broad enough for alien structures but narrow enough to preserve a numerical estimate.
Sources: Classical Roots of IUT Mutually Alien Copies Panoramic Overview Related: GMS/GCG motivation Hodge theaters log-theta lattice multiradiality theta/log links

Core Concept Index

This index is the working expansion map for the wiki. Each entry is a source concept that appears in the four IUT papers or in the supporting IUT corpus. Short entries will be promoted to full pages as the source reading proceeds.

Mutually Alien Copies Expository model two-copy method, Gaussian analogy, GMS/GCG motivation, q-power model, mono-anabelian transport, closed-loop prime-strip, AND/OR alien model, Gaussian overview 1.1, naive coordinate change 1.2, two-copy method 1.3, plane integral 1.4, Jacobian/infinitesimals 1.5, polar decoupling 1.6, error factor 1.7, heights/abc 2.1, arithmetic degrees 2.2, GMS 2.3, Frobenius/NF 2.4, Frobenius lifting 2.5, positive-characteristic transport 2.6, mono-anabelian transport 2.7 Mutually Alien Copies, Sections 1-3

Hyperbolic orbicurve

The quotient of a once-punctured elliptic curve by its order-two involution is a basic orbicurve input. It is the geometric object behind many fundamental-group and cusp reconstructions.

Tempered fundamental group

Tempered fundamental groups encode covering data used in IUT I's anabelian reconstruction layer. They are distinct from the later mono-analytic comparison interfaces.

Decomposition subgroup

Decomposition subgroups recover local valuation and cusp information from group-theoretic data. They are part of the source machinery for local prime-strip objects.

Model base-NF bridge

The model base-NF bridge is the number-field side of the bridge formalism in IUT I. It transports capsule data while preserving the appropriate multiplicative labels.

Model base-Theta bridge

The model base-Theta bridge is the theta side of the same bridge discipline. It is tied to evaluation sections and label-class transport at bad places.

D-ThetaNF-Hodge theater

A D-ThetaNF-Hodge theater combines compatible D-NF and D-Theta bridges over a shared capsule of D-prime-strips.

Holomorphic procession

A procession organizes bridge and prime-strip data over label-indexed capsules. Later procession-normalized log-volumes inherit this label discipline.

ThetaNF-Hodge theater

A ThetaNF-Hodge theater glues theta and number-field bridge structures into a combined Hodge-theater interface. It prepares the later additive constructions.

Theta cyclotome

Definition 5.2 uses theta-side cyclotomes to compare Kummer classes of coric rational functions with roots of unity attached to the Frobenioid side.

Theta bridge

Theta bridges carry theta-side structure in the Hodge-theater taxonomy. They must be kept distinct from NF bridges and from theta links.

NF bridge

NF bridges carry number-field-side bridge structure. They participate in gluing operations but do not by themselves constitute theta evaluation.

Theta-plus-minus bridge

The additive theory introduces plus/minus theta bridges to manage the additive symmetry layer associated to labels and cusps.

Theta-ell bridge

Theta-ell bridges encode the elliptic/torsion side of the additive combinatorial Teichmuller theory in IUT I, Section 6.

Theta-plus-minus-ellNF theater

This theater combines additive theta-plus-minus, theta-ell, and NF data. It is the source object for the theta and log-theta constructions in IUT II and III.

Theta evaluation

Galois-theoretic theta evaluation reconstructs theta-value data from mono-theta and group-theoretic information. It is a source for the horizontal theta links.

Tempered Gaussian Frobenioids

Tempered Gaussian Frobenioids encode Gaussian theta-value distributions in a Frobenioid-theoretic setting before the global Gaussian layer.

Global Gaussian Frobenioids

Global Gaussian Frobenioids synchronize local Gaussian data and supply the realified Frobenioid objects used in theta-link comparisons.

Theta×μ link

The Theta×μ link is the full poly-isomorphism F×μ_env -> F×μ_△ of IUT II Corollary 4.10(iii). It is obtained by functorially upgrading a nonempty set of isomorphisms of F-prime-strips to isomorphisms of F×μ-prime-strips.

Theta×μ-gau link

The Gaussian version is the full poly-isomorphism F×μ_gau -> F×μ_△. Corollary 4.10 treats it parallel to the Theta×μ link, while Remark 4.10.2 explains why the later papers mainly use its enhanced descendants.

Coric F-prime-strip

Coric F⊢×μ-prime-strip data is invariant under both theta-link variants. Corollary 4.10(iv) identifies F⊢×μ_△ across theaters and then induces the corresponding D⊢_△ poly-isomorphism.

Multiradial theta monoids

IUT III Section 2 develops theta monoids whose constructions remain meaningful across the relevant alien holomorphic viewpoints.

Local holomorphic tensor packet

IUT III, Proposition 3.1 starts with an n-capsule of F-prime-strips. Over v_Q, the one-packet is the direct sum of the local logarithmic modules over all v | v_Q; the n-packet is their tensor product, equivalently a direct sum over all choices of one place in each tensor factor.

Local mono-analytic tensor packet

IUT III, Proposition 3.2 repeats the tensor-packet construction for D⊢-prime-strips, and when these arise from F⊢×μ-prime-strips it records the poly-isomorphisms to the holomorphic packets. The integral structures now come from mono-analytic log-shells.

Global tensor packet

IUT III, Proposition 3.3 defines (†M_mod)_A as a tensor product over α∈A of the labeled fields (†M_mod)_α for a subset of Hodge-theater labels and embeds it into the product of the local holomorphic tensor packets by localization composed with the given log-links.

Different estimates

IUT IV begins with estimates for differents in tensor products of local fields. These estimates feed the explicit log-volume bounds.

Normalized log-volume estimates

Nonarchimedean normalized log-volume estimates are the local numerical estimates that later combine into the theta-pilot log-volume theorem.

Prime number theorem input

IUT IV uses a prime-number-theorem estimate as part of the elementary analytic input for effective global inequalities.

Arithmetic divisors

Arithmetic divisors in IUT IV package the global arithmetic data whose degrees and heights enter the final Diophantine estimates.

Mutation history

A mutation history records a sequence of species-level transformations. It is the formal language for tracking inter-universal movement without collapsing implementations.

Sources: IUT I IUT II IUT III IUT IV Related: source library glossary

Anabelian Background

IUT is built on anabelian reconstruction: information about arithmetic curves is recovered from fundamental groups and related Galois-theoretic structures. Collas describes IUT as a continuation of Grothendieck's anabelian program through a new geometry of monoids; Mochizuki's gateway report frames IUT as a route from Diophantine geometry toward analytic number theory. Recent Collas papers also place this background in the active France-Japan anabelian and Galois-Teichmuller program.

Anabelian route from curves and covers to group-theoretic reconstruction
Source layer Anabelian object Role in this wiki
IUT I, Introduction and §1 Hyperbolic orbicurves, finite etale coverings, cusps, inertia groups, and tempered/etale fundamental groups. Explains why initial theta data includes orbicurve and covering data before Hodge theaters are built.
IUT I, Theorem B Group-theoretic recognition of decomposition and inertia data in tempered fundamental groups of p-adic hyperbolic curves. Source for reconstructing local valuation-indexed structure from group-theoretic data.
IUT I, etale-picture discussion Absolute anabelian recovery of ring/scheme structures from etale-like data, then deliberate use of that recovery inside alien copies. Prevents treating Hodge theaters as arbitrary records; their etale side is supposed to carry reconstruction algorithms.
IUT IV §3 and 2026 Lean report Species, mutations, and functorial algorithms as a language for reconstruction independent of one set-theoretic realization. Connects anabelian reconstruction to formalization interfaces and the LANA/RIMS Lean routes.
Collas overview, §2.2 Fukugen, decomposition/inertia reconstruction, Kummer reconstruction of multiplicative monoids, and additive recovery from valuations. Gives a reader-facing route from classical anabelian geometry to the monoid-transport mechanisms that IUT uses.
Collas-Murotani-Yamaguchi 2025 Reconstruction from symmetries, from Galois theory and absolute Galois groups to etale fundamental groups and contemporary anabelian trends. Provides a compact non-IUT-specific entry point for why group-theoretic reconstruction is plausible and mathematically structured.

Classical anabelian input

Hyperbolic curves, their coverings, and their arithmetic fundamental groups are the base objects. IUT I uses this input when reconstructing local decomposition data, cusps, and valuation-indexed structures.

Combinatorial and mono-anabelian methods

Hoshi and Mochizuki's background work turns reconstruction into functorial algorithms. The wiki treats these algorithms as a prerequisite layer for Hodge theaters and prime-strips.

Geometry of monoids

Collas emphasizes IUT's monoid geometry as a way to move beyond direct ring/scheme comparison. This is exactly why the wiki keeps separate pages for Frobenioids, log-shells, and prime-strip interfaces.

Fukugen reconstruction

Collas presents anabelian geometry as reconstruction, or fukugen: recover a space from its etale fundamental group and the functorial symmetries encoded by covers. The IUT relevance is that reconstruction is algorithmic input, not merely a philosophical analogy.

Collas §2.2reconstruction

Two-step monoid recovery

In Collas's summary of absolute mono-anabelian reconstruction, one first reconstructs multiplicative data through Kummer-theoretic containers, then reconstructs additive structure by using valuation quotients of function fields. This separates the two monoid structures before recovering a field.

Collas §2.2.2Kummer

Mono-anabelian transport

Collas describes mono-anabelian transport as moving Frobenius-like monoid data through etale-like containers, subject to indeterminacies. This is the expository bridge from p-adic anabelian reconstruction to IUT's theta-link and log-link transport.

Collas §2.2.3transport

Second regeometrization

Collas frames IUT as a second regeometrization of arithmetic geometry: after anabelian geometry unified arithmetic and geometry through fundamental groups, IUT tries to include Diophantine estimates by dismantling and comparing additive and multiplicative monoid structures.

Collas §3IUT framing

Hodge-theater synchronization

Collas explains Hodge theaters as synchronizing geometric symmetries of the pointed elliptic curve side with arithmetic symmetries of the number-field side. This is the expository version of the theater as a typed environment for the later theta/log link operations.

Collas §3.1.1synchronization

Indeterminacies in the Collas map

Collas locates Ind1 in mono-analytic etale-like comparison, Ind2 in the etale-like versus Frobenius-like comparison, and Ind3 in the noncommutativity of log-Kummer correspondence. This matches the wiki's separation of indeterminacy pages from ordinary quotient-invariance language.

Collas §3.1.2Ind1/2/3

Reconstruction from symmetries

Collas-Murotani-Yamaguchi introduce anabelian geometry through the question whether numbers and geometric spaces can be reconstructed from their symmetries. They start from Galois theory and then pass to absolute Galois groups and fundamental groups.

CMY 2025 §1symmetries

Homotopic arithmetic geometry

The same survey frames etale fundamental groups as the arithmetic-geometric analogue of topological loop groups. This is the broad background behind IUT's reliance on tempered and etale fundamental groups of hyperbolic curves and orbicurves.

CMY 2025 §2etale pi1

GT as anabelian container

Collas's 2026 Galois-Teichmuller paper places braid groups, mapping-class groups, and Grothendieck-Teichmuller symmetries inside an anabelian framework. The point for this wiki is not that GT proves IUT, but that it supplies a parallel model in which group-theoretic containers retain arithmetic information after geometric presentation data is removed.

Collas GT §3anabelian container

BGT models and G_Q

In Collas's account, Belyi-Grothendieck-Teichmuller models involve multiple ring structures connected by non-algebraic identifications. This is useful background for IUT because it normalizes the distinction between a shared abstract group-theoretic container and the particular arithmetic-holomorphic structures carried by its realizations.

Collas GT §4.2G_Q

Deligne-Mumford curve inertia

Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi develop anabelian reconstruction for Deligne-Mumford curves. Their 2026 results treat stack inertia as information visible in etale fundamental groups, not as decoration added after reconstruction. This supports the wiki's treatment of orbicurves, cusps, and inertia as part of the input geometry.

CPY 2026 §1orbicurves

Three-step detection

The Deligne-Mumford curve paper proves detection results for hyperbolicity, affineness, and related inertia phenomena from low-level solvable quotients in its stated setting. The relevance here is methodological: some geometric predicates entering IUT-style orbicurve data can be formalized as group-theoretic recognizers.

CPY 2026 §2.4recognition

Affine stacky reconstruction

Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi formulate a five-step anabelian reconstruction theorem for rigidifying affine stacky curves in their framework. This belongs in the IUT wiki as current evidence that the orbicurve side of the theory is an active formal reconstruction problem, separate from the later theta/log-volume comparison.

CPY 2026 §3rigidification

Gateway framing

Mochizuki's anabelian gateway report presents IUT as a bridge from Diophantine geometry to analytic number theory. The practical link inside the main series is through log-volume estimates.

N-th power monoid map

The Gateway report uses the N-th power map as an elementary model: it is an isomorphism of multiplicative monoids but not a ring homomorphism. This is the basic reason the common multiplicative interface cannot be read as equality of ring structures.

Gateway §2monoid not ring

Projective-line gluing analogy

The same report compares IUT gluing to constructing P1 by gluing two labeled affine lines along Gm using inversion. Deleting the labels turns a meaningful gluing into a contradiction unless one passes to a quotient or collection of possibilities.

Open concept page

Gateway §3.1labels

ZFCG and data types

Gateway §3.3 stresses that IUT is formulated in ZFC with Grothendieck universes and works with diagrams and species-like packages of data, not only with selected underlying sets. This supports the wiki's insistence on typed records and closed-loop comparisons.

Gateway §3.3species

Galois-orbit IUT

Section 4 describes a Galois-orbit version of IUT as work in progress, with claimed applications to local-global section-conjecture conditions, Siegel zeroes for imaginary-quadratic Dirichlet L-functions, and stronger abc/Szpiro inequalities.

Gateway §4work in progress

Formalization implication

Lean development should not treat anabelian reconstruction as a black box forever. It can start as typed interfaces, but the long-term project must replace those interfaces by constructions from anabelian source results.

Sources: Collas overview Collas 2026 CMY 2025 Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi 2026 Hoshi introduction Anabelian Gateway Anabelian Gateway 2025 Related: prime strips Hodge theaters LANA formalization

Collas Algorithmic Anabelian View

In the 2026 Galois-Teichmuller perspective, Collas describes a shift from explicit braid-theoretic computation toward functorial anabelian algorithms. The algorithmic viewpoint reconstructs discrete arithmetic or geometric data from fundamental groups without relying on arbitrary noncanonical choices.

From reconstruction to algorithms Collas, Anabelian Perspectives §1.3
Collas termSource readingWiki use
Algorithmic anabelian geometry Functorial group-theoretic algorithms produce discrete arithmetic/geometric data from fundamental groups. Explains why IUT pages should state reconstruction procedures, not just object labels.
Combinatorial anabelian geometry A group-theoretic version of Galois action and Belyi/GT phenomena. Provides context for BGT and Galois-Teichmuller background pages.
MA versus AH Mono-analytic data are tied to multiplicative monoids; arithmetic-holomorphic data are global anabelian objects. Matches the wiki's separation of mono-analytic containers from arithmetic holomorphic ring histories.
Source: Collas, Anabelian Perspectives §1.3 Related: Collas category view mono-analytic log-shells arithmetic holomorphic spokes

Collas Height Universes

Collas emphasizes a foundational point that matters directly for the 3.12 corridor: local heights and realified Frobenioids attached to different Hodge theaters should not be treated as living in one fixed copy of the real numbers before the permitted indeterminacies and multiradial construction have supplied a common comparison setting.

Heights before and after multiradial comparison Collas overview, Universes/Species and abc inequality sections
PointCollas reading3.12 relevance
Different copies of R Distinct Hodge theaters carry incompatible realified Frobenioid data before comparison. Prevents silently comparing log-volumes in one ordinary ordered-real space too early.
Species and mutations They manage the common portion independent of one fixed ZFC model. Connects Collas's exposition to IUT IV §3 and Mochizuki's 2026 LeanForm report.
Common universe The multiradial algorithm supplies structures compatible with the distinct ring histories. Explains why the q-pilot/Theta-pilot comparison is a constructed comparison, not a default equality.
Source: Collas overview, pp. 17-19 Related: Collas universes/species IUT IV ZFCG universes LeanForm common container

Collas Stack Inertia

Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi's 2026 paper develops anabelian reconstruction for Deligne-Mumford curves by tracking stack and orbifold inertia inside etale fundamental groups. For this wiki, the point is methodological: orbicurve and inertia data should be treated as reconstructible structure, not as an auxiliary label added after a curve has already been recovered.

Stacky curve

Rigidification removes generic inertia; the coarse curve records the underlying smooth curve.

Fundamental group

Closed-point stack inertia and related orbifold data are detected group-theoretically in the stated settings.

Anabelian output

Weak and finite-step reconstruction results recover stacky curve data under precise hypotheses.

Source itemMeaningIUT-wiki use
Rigidification/coarsification A Deligne-Mumford curve has stacky and coarse curve layers. Useful background for orbicurve packages in IUT I.
Stack inertia Finite inertia subgroups at stacky points are part of the reconstructible data. Supports keeping inertia and cusp data typed in formalization pages.
Five-step affine stacky result The paper proves a finite-step reconstruction theorem for affine hyperbolic stacky curves under its hypotheses. Current anabelian work informs the long-term library needed before IUT is fully source-formalized.
Source: Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi 2026 Related: Collas current anabelian work IUT I orbicurve covers covering symmetries

2021 Combinatorial Anabelian Overview

Mochizuki's July 2021 overview organizes combinatorial anabelian geometry around semi-graphs of anabelioids, finite etale and tempered coverings, group-theoretic recognition of vertices and edges, and cuspidalization results. For the IUT wiki, the page is background: it explains the group/covering technology that later supports algorithmic reconstruction, but it is not itself an IUT proof of the Corollary 3.12 inequality.

Anabelioids

Finite etale coverings can be discussed without fixing geometric basepoints.

Semi-graphs

PSC-type semi-graphs of anabelioids encode vertex and edge behavior through covering categories.

Tempered output

For p-adic hyperbolic curves, tempered fundamental groups recover special-fiber semi-graph data.

Overview topicSource contentWiki use
T0 Semi-graphs of anabelioids, finite etale and tempered coverings, and Zariski-main-type statements. sub-semi-graph arithmetic subgroups
T2-T3 Combinatorial Grothendieck-conjecture-type results, cuspidalization, and FC/F comparison results. combinatorial tripodal example
T4 Arithmetic applications over number fields and mixed-characteristic local fields. GT decomposition route
Source: Overview of Combinatorial Anabelian Geometry, T0-T4 Related: Collas current anabelian work Hoshi Kummer transport

Tripod Synchronization

The later part of the 2021 overview summarizes synchronization of cyclotomes, profinite Dehn twists, geometric monodromy, and tripods. The key route is the tripod homomorphism: under the stated hypotheses, central tripods in configuration-space fundamental groups lead to maps toward the Grothendieck-Teichmuller group, with synchronization expressed by compatible diagrams of such maps.

Cyclotome synchronization

Profinite orientation and cyclotomic data must be synchronized before Dehn-twist and monodromy statements can be compared.

Tripod homomorphism

Central tripods yield natural maps from suitable outer-automorphism groups toward GT.

GT structure

The overview ends with structure results and arithmetic subgroup statements for the Grothendieck-Teichmuller group.

This is adjacent background for IUT and LANA: it clarifies the combinatorial anabelian style of algorithmic reconstruction, but it should remain separate from IUT's log-theta lattice and Corollary 3.12 log-volume route.
Source: Overview of Combinatorial Anabelian Geometry, T5-T7 Related: GT genus-zero quotient GT species perspective combinatorial tripodal example

Collas Category View

Benjamin Collas presents IUT as a Grothendieck-style categorification of a Diophantine problem: the classical ring/scheme framework is not discarded, but it is reorganized through functorial anabelian reconstruction, monoid geometry, Frobenioids, Hodge theaters, species, and logical structure. This is an interpretive map for reading the source papers. Its key mathematical checkpoint is that IUT simulates a global multiplicative subspace that exists locally but typically not globally, while keeping track of operations that are not compatible with one fixed ring structure.

From schemes to category-theoretic containers Collas's expository route into IUT
Grothendieck setting

Schemes, rings, and etale fundamental groups supply the reconstruction language.

Mono-anabelian recovery

Group-theoretic algorithms recover arithmetic data without fixing a second comparison scheme.

Monoid geometry

Additive and multiplicative sides are separated so non-ring-theoretic transport is visible.

IUT containers

Hodge theaters, Frobenioids, and log-shells hold data across alien ring histories.

Log-theta wandering

Multiradial algorithms compare Frobenius-like data through the log-theta lattice.

Species/universes

Logical and set-theoretic bookkeeping records which comparisons are legitimate.

Functorial unification

Grothendieckian reconstruction as a common language for arithmetic discreteness and geometric continuity.

Collas §1standalone page

Geometry of monoids

The shift from ring/scheme geometry to separated additive and multiplicative monoid structures.

Collas §2.2standalone page

Categorifying a Diophantine problem

The height problem is reframed through categorical containers rather than a single global scheme.

Collas §3.1standalone page

Local/global GMS obstruction

Local multiplicative subspaces do not automatically assemble into the required global object.

Collas §3.1standalone page

Non-ring dilation

The theta-side dilation behaves Frobenius-like but is not compatible with one fixed ring structure.

Collas §3.1standalone page

Basepoints and log-shells

Multiple basepoints and log-shell containers are structural consequences of changing ring histories.

Collas §3.1.1standalone page
Collas checkpointCategory-theoretic readingWiki consequence
§3.1 IUT starts from a local/global obstruction: local ell-multiplicative subspaces exist, but the needed global multiplicative subspace generally does not. GMS/GCG motivation
§3.1 The quotient/dilation operation behind the height estimate is not a ring homomorphism, so the comparison cannot live inside one unchanged ring/scheme history. theta/log links
§3.1 The Diophantine problem is categorified because local multiplicative subspaces need not assemble globally, and the relevant ell-power operation is not a ring homomorphism. GMS/GCG motivation
§3.1.1 Hodge theaters synchronize geometric symmetries of the pointed curve with arithmetic symmetries of the base field. Hodge theaters
§3.1.1 The ThetaNF and Theta±ell parts separate multiplicative arithmetic and additive geometry; the theta link respects one side but not the full ring structure. theta/log links
§3.1.1 Log-shells act as common containers for handling multiplicative and additive monoid structures, while multiple basepoints are unavoidable. basepoints
§3.1.2 The multiradial algorithm constructs a common container for Frobenius-like data of theta-linked theaters; Collas identifies Ind3 as the essential indeterminacy. multiradiality
§3.1.3 Collas separates external logical articulation from the internal category-theoretic/species layer where objects, morphisms, functors, and universe changes are controlled. species and mutations
§3.2.1 The theta-pilot and q-pilot comparison is presented as a comparison of regions in log-shell tensor-product containers, after indeterminacies make the alien ring-structure data comparable. Theorem 1.10 route
Collas sourceCategory-theoretic contributionUse in this wiki
Anabelian arithmetic geometry and IUT Presents IUT as categorification of a Diophantine problem: Hodge theaters simulate the missing global multiplicative subspace, while theta/log links separate incompatible ring structures. Orientation for GMS/GCG, Hodge theaters, and log-theta lattice.
Anabelian perspectives in GT theory Develops the GT side as a passage from braid computations to functorial and algorithmic anabelian reconstruction, including BGT models of Qbar from Belyi diagrams. Context for GT species, anabelian background, and the wiki's insistence on reconstruction algorithms rather than labels.
Symmetries and anabelian geometry Gives a short route from symmetry groups to reconstruction of arithmetic and geometric spaces, with contemporary emphasis on canonicity and categorification. Background for anabelian reconstruction and for explaining why IUT depends on canonical, source-independent recovery procedures.
Anabelian geometry for Deligne-Mumford curves Extends reconstruction to stacky curves by combining stack inertia, rigidification, and low-step solvable quotients of fundamental groups. Current anabelian context for IUT's orbicurve/Hodge-theater background; not a replacement for the IUT proof corridor.
Scope. Collas's text is highly useful for orientation and for designing human-auditable Lean interfaces, but it does not replace the exact definitions in IUT I-IV. In the wiki, Collas pages should point back to the corresponding Mochizuki source objects whenever a construction becomes formal.
Wiki design consequence. Collas's category-theoretic reading supports treating IUT concepts as separate typed pages rather than as one narrative scroll. Anabelian reconstruction, monoid geometry, Hodge theaters, and species/logical structure are linked as containers and transitions; their source definitions remain on the Mochizuki pages where the formal data is introduced.
Sources: Collas overview Collas GT perspectives Collas-Murotani-Yamaguchi Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi 2026 Related: anabelian background Frobenioids Hodge theaters GMS/GCG motivation Collas audit guide projective-line gluing logical structure

Collas: Functorial Unification

Collas reads IUT against a Grothendieckian background: arithmetic discreteness and geometric continuity are compared by functorial reconstruction rather than by ad hoc coordinate choices. This is an orientation page, not an independent IUT definition.

Anabelian reconstruction

The starting model is reconstruction of a space from its etale fundamental group. Collas emphasizes the classical bi-anabelian form, the mono-anabelian form, and the absolute form that detaches the object from a fixed base scheme.

IUT consequence

In IUT this reconstruction style is used to transport weakened arithmetic data between contexts where the original ring/scheme history is deliberately not identified.

Source: Collas overview, §§1-2.2 Related: anabelian background absolute anabelian algorithms monoid geometry

Collas: Geometry of Monoids

Collas identifies a central IUT shift: instead of treating the ring as an indivisible object, IUT works with the multiplicative and additive monoid structures separately, then tracks the cost of comparison.

Ring data split into monoid data orientation for IUT theta/log links
Ring structure

The ordinary object packages addition and multiplication together.

Multiplicative monoid

The Frobenius-like side retains multiplicative transport data.

Additive monoid

The logarithmic side carries additive comparison data.

IUT comparison

Indeterminacies and containers control the comparison without pretending one ring history survived unchanged.

Collas' point matches the IUT source route through Frobenioids, Kummer theory, theta links, log links, and indeterminacies. It should not be read as saying that IUT forgets ring structures without replacement.
Source: Collas overview, §2.2 Related: Frobenioid standard model theta/log links non-ring dilation

Collas: Diophantine Categorification

Collas describes IUT as categorifying a Diophantine height problem. The desired comparison is not made by constructing one ordinary global multiplicative subspace, but by replacing that missing object with Hodge theater and Frobenioid containers.

LayerCollas readingIUT wiki target
Diophantine Height estimates for elliptic-curve data motivate the construction. IUT IV Diophantine output
Categorical Hodge theaters simulate the unavailable global multiplicative subspace. Hodge theaters
Comparison The log-theta lattice supplies the route to theta/q-pilot comparison. Corollary 3.12
Source: Collas overview, §3.1 Related: local/global GMS Hodge synchronization IUT III A/B pipeline

Collas: Local/Global GMS Obstruction

In Collas' account, a key obstruction is that the relevant ell-torsion multiplicative subspaces exist locally at suitable places, but not as a single global multiplicative subspace over the number field. IUT replaces this unavailable object by synchronized Hodge-theater data.

Local data

At selected local places, multiplicative subspaces attached to elliptic data are available.

Global obstruction

These local pieces do not generally glue into the needed global subspace.

IUT replacement

Theater containers and links encode the comparison without postulating the missing global object.

Source: Collas overview, §3.1 Related: GMS/GCG motivation theta-Hodge theater Diophantine categorification

Collas: Non-Ring Dilation

Collas presents the theta-side dilation as Frobenius-like on the multiplicative side, for example through the Tate parameter pattern q_E -> q_E^(j^2). The operation is not compatible with a single fixed additive ring structure.

What survives

The multiplicative/Frobenius-like reading of the theta link is retained and becomes the useful transport route.

What fails

The same move is not a ring homomorphism for one fixed ring history, so additive information must be handled through log-shell and link machinery.

Source: Collas overview, §3.1.1 Related: theta links 3.7 theta values q^j^2 monoid geometry

Collas: Basepoints and Log-Shells

Collas stresses that multiple basepoints and log-shell containers are not cosmetic. They record the fact that theta and log links compare structures across distinct ring histories while retaining enough common mono-analytic information for controlled comparison.

Theater labels

Labels such as source and target theaters remember that isomorphic copies are not identified as one ringed object.

Log-shell container

The log-shell supplies a common place to compare multiplicative and additive readings after logarithmic transport.

Formalization cue

Lean records should keep labels, source histories, and allowed indeterminacies visible instead of quotienting them away too early.

Source: Collas overview, §3.1.1 Related: basepoints log-shell properties log-theta wandering

Collas Audit Guide

Collas' category-theoretic account is best used as an audit guide for the wiki and for Lean formalization. It explains why IUT repeatedly replaces one ambient ring/scheme picture by typed reconstruction containers, but it is not itself the source of the formal definitions. The formal source remains Mochizuki's IUT I-IV and the specific later papers cited on each page.

How Collas should influence the wiki orientation layer, source definitions, formalization guardrails
Collas themeAudit readingFormal source to check
Geometry of monoids The additive and multiplicative structures should be carried separately until the relevant IUT construction supplies a comparison. Frobenioids, Theta±ell bridges
Categorified GMS The missing global multiplicative subspace must appear as a categorical replacement process, not as an assumed global object. GMS categorification, GMS/GCG motivation
Hodge-theater synchronization Basepoints, labels, and side information are structural data. Treating the theater as an undifferentiated copy loses the Collas/Mochizuki point. Hodge theaters, basepoints
Log-theta wandering Movement through theta and log links should preserve the type of the data being compared and should expose Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3 explicitly. Theorem 3.11, Corollary 3.12
Universes and species A comparison target is not automatically shared by all inputs. It must be produced by the species, mutation, and common-container machinery. IUT IV species, mutation histories
Boundary. Collas helps identify what must not be collapsed: local/global distinctions, additive/multiplicative sides, basepoint histories, and universe/species context. A page or Lean record still needs a direct Mochizuki source link before it is treated as formal IUT content.
Sources: Collas overview IUT I IUT III IUT IV Related: Collas category view GMS categorification log-theta wandering RIMS four-triangle route

Collas Source Alignment

Collas is most useful in this wiki as a category-theoretic alignment layer: his exposition explains why IUT moves from a missing global multiplicative subspace to Hodge-theater containers, log-theta wandering, and species. The formal definitions still have to be checked against Mochizuki's source papers, especially IUT I for Hodge theaters, IUT III for multiradial algorithms, and IUT IV §3 for species and mutations.

Category-theoretic reading, source-definition targets Collas overview §§2.2-3.1 aligned with IUT I, III, IV
Collas checkpointReadingMochizuki source to verifyWiki action
Missing GMS The desired global multiplicative subspace exists locally but not globally in the required form; this motivates categorification. IUT I construction of Hodge theaters; Essential Logical Structure Example 3.2.1. GMS categorification
Monoid geometry The additive and multiplicative sides of a ring are separated so that non-ring-theoretic transport can be controlled. IUT I Frobenioid/Hodge-theater packages; IUT III log-Kummer constructions. Frobenioids/log-shells
Log-theta wandering The category-theoretic picture must be read as theta/log movement through labeled theaters, not as an equality inside one ambient ring category. IUT III Theorem 3.11 and Introduction Theorem A. IUT III Theorem A
Species and universes Object types are handled as formula-defined species, so outputs in different universe levels can be compared without violating foundation. IUT IV §3, especially Definition 3.3 and Remarks 3.1.3-3.3.2. species vs categories
Height comparison The q-pilot/Theta-pilot comparison depends on the preceding machinery; it is not a default comparison in one pre-existing real line. IUT III Theorem B; IUT IV §1, Theorem 1.10 and §2 applications. IUT III Theorem B
Use rule. Collas may supply the orientation question, but every concept page should still name the Mochizuki construction that carries the mathematical load. This prevents the wiki from replacing source definitions by high-level category-theory paraphrase.
Sources: Collas overview §§2.2-3.1 Collas GT perspective IUT IV §3 Related: Collas category view Collas audit guide IUT IV mutations IUT III A/B pipeline

Collas GMS Categorification

Collas's IUT overview presents the first entrance to IUT as a Diophantine categorification problem. The motivating object is a global ell-torsion multiplicative subspace for an elliptic curve. Local versions exist at suitable places, but the global subspace needed for the desired height argument is not available in general. IUT replaces that absent global object by Hodge-theater and Frobenioid containers.

Local multiplicative data, categorical global simulation Collas §3.1 as a navigation layer for IUT I-IV
Collas pointTechnical readingWiki target
Local existence At suitable local fields, elliptic curves admit the relevant ell-torsion multiplicative subspaces. GMS/GCG motivation
Global failure The desired global multiplicative subspace is generally absent, so a direct scheme-theoretic proof route is unavailable. Hodge theaters
Categorification IUT simulates the missing global object by category-theoretic containers, especially Frobenioids and Hodge theaters. IUT I Frobenioid package
Formalization guardrail A Lean object should not merely name a global multiplicative subspace; it must distinguish local availability from the categorical substitute. four-triangle route
Use in this wiki. This page is interpretive. The exact objects to formalize remain Mochizuki's Hodge-theater, Frobenioid, prime-strip, and theta/log-link constructions, not Collas's expository shorthand.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1 IUT I IUT III Related: Collas category view Hodge synchronization log-theta wandering

Collas Hodge Synchronization

Collas describes a Hodge theater as a synchronization device. It combines the arithmetic symmetries of the base field with the geometric symmetries of a pointed genus-one curve. The result is not one ordinary scheme copied many times, but a typed container in which multiplicative and additive information can be separated before later comparison.

Theater as synchronized container arithmetic and geometric symmetries remain typed

ThetaNF part

In Collas's account, this part is arithmetic and multiplicative. It points back to the number-field and Frobenioid side of the Hodge theater.

Theta±ell part

This part carries the geometric and additive side, tied to the curve and to the covering/symmetry data used to synchronize conjugates.

Multiple basepoints

Collas stresses that IUT does not operate with one unified basepoint. The labels and basepoint choices record genuinely different ring histories.

Formalization consequence. The theater interface should carry synchronization data, labels, and side information. A record that only stores an undifferentiated "copy of a curve" misses the point of this layer.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1.1 IUT I Related: Hodge theaters Theta±ell bridges basepoints

Collas Log-Theta Wandering

Collas reads the log-theta lattice as the setting in which IUT relates distinct Frobenius-like objects by multiradial algorithms. The important point for this wiki is not the picture alone, but the audit discipline it suggests: movement through theta links, log links, Kummer correspondences, and indeterminacies must preserve the exact type of the data being compared.

From alien theaters to a common container Collas §3.1.2, aligned with IUT III Theorem 3.11
LayerRoleSource target
Theta link Moves between distinct Hodge-theater labels and is compatible with the multiplicative side, not with one fixed full ring structure. IUT I Corollary 3.7
Log link Relates multiplicative source data to additive target data through log-shell containers. IUT III 1.1
Kummer route Provides the etale-like access path between Frobenius-like data and the common container, subject to indeterminacies. Log-Kummer correspondences
Ind3 Collas identifies this as the essential indeterminacy for the main theorem and the height comparison application. Indeterminacies
3.12 relevance. This is the Collas-side explanation of why the wiki treats the Theorem 3.11 output as a typed multiradial construction, not as a bare equality between objects in one ambient scheme category.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1.2 IUT III Related: Theorem 3.11 algorithms Corollary 3.12 corridor multiradiality

Collas Universes and Species

Collas separates an external logical layer from an internal category-theoretic layer. The external layer concerns how statements are connected by AND/OR structure. The internal layer concerns categories, functors, objects, universes, species, and mutations. This distinction is useful for the wiki and for Lean work because the height comparison is not meant to be read inside one unchanged universe with one unchanged real line attached to all local data.

Logical structure and species discipline Collas §3.1.3 points back to IUT IV §3

External layer

Collas points to Mochizuki's logical analyses for the sequence of statements. The wiki uses this only as proof-route orientation.

Internal layer

Species and mutations are the IUT IV mechanism for controlling typed objects and transformations without identifying all outputs in one raw set.

Height comparison

Local heights may be attached to different reconstruction contexts. The common comparison setting is an output of the IUT apparatus, not a default.

Formalization consequence. This page supports keeping species, mutations, and comparison universes explicit. It also explains why the wiki links Collas's interpretation directly to IUT IV §3 rather than treating it as independent formal source material.
Sources: Collas overview §3.1.3 IUT IV §3 Related: species vs categories mutation histories/cores same-species parity

Collas Current Anabelian Work

Collas' recent papers are useful context for the wiki because they explain the anabelian and category-theoretic environment in which IUT and LANA sit. They should be read as orientation and adjacent reconstruction work: source definitions for IUT still come from Mochizuki's IUT I-IV and related official notes.

Current anabelian route from symmetry reconstruction to IUT context
PaperMain contentUse in this wiki
Symmetries of Spaces and Numbers Collas, Murotani, and Yamaguchi introduce anabelian geometry through the question of reconstructing numbers and spaces from their symmetries, moving from Galois theory to etale fundamental groups and current trends. Use as the lowest-barrier entry to the anabelian reconstruction idea behind IUT's source machinery.
