(1Fr/et) Early philosophical contrast: Frobenius-like as order-conscious, etale-like as indifferent to order. Useful as intuition only; do not formalize from this slogan.
(2Fr/et) Log-theta-lattice notation: Frobenius-like data uses coordinates such as (n,m); etale-like data uses vertically coric coordinates such as (n,*) or (*,*). This is the technically sufficient reading for IUT I-IV and for our corridor pages.
(3Fr/et) Abstract link-theoretic terminology: Frobenius-like and etale-like are defined relative to a specific link. Use for general orientation, but route back to concrete theta/log links before making proof claims.

This page is an audit guard: an internal link named "Frobenius-like" or "etale-like" must say which link or lattice coordinate system is in force. Collas's categorical perspective is compatible with this guard, since his exposition treats the categories as containers whose meaning depends on the surrounding functorial apparatus.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §2.8 Related: mono-anabelian transport Frobenius/etale versions Collas category view

Alien Copies 2.9: Kodaira-Spencer Analogy

Section 2.9 identifies a central IUT task: compute the discrepancy between two Kummer theories after a link, while one side is held fixed. Mochizuki compares this with the classical Kodaira-Spencer construction for a family of elliptic curves, where a positive-mass Hodge line is compared through a zero-mass local system.

Kodaira-Spencer as transport pattern positive-mass Hodge line through a zero-mass container
Classical objectIUT reading in §2.9Bound
omega_E subset E Positive-mass Frobenius-like object on one side of a deformation. 2 deg(omega_E) <= deg(omega_Slog/C)
(E, nabla_E) Zero-mass etale-like container used to relate the alien Hodge-line copies.
omega_Slog/C The tensor factor that measures the indeterminacy admitted in the comparison.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §2.9 Related: positive-characteristic model Kummer detachment Hodge-Arakelov theory

Alien Copies 2.10: Inter-Universality

Section 2.10 gives the literal reason for "inter-universal": theta and log links do not preserve the ring or scheme structures that normally label Galois groups. After transport, the group must be treated as an abstract topological group; anabelian reconstruction supplies controlled ways to recover scheme-theoretic structure without identifying the old labels with the new ones.

Change of universe as coordinate change only the abstract object is shared across incompatible labels

Mochizuki's elementary string 010 example is the local model: the middle symbol has a left-coordinate description in 01 and a right-coordinate description in 10, but only the abstract symbol is common to both. Likewise, a Frobenius-induced Galois-group isomorphism can be the identity from ordinary ring theory while remaining indeterminate relative to class field theory or Kummer labels.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §2.10 Related: absolute anabelian mutation fiber functors/universes Collas universes/species

Alien Copies 2.11: Two Ring Dimensions

Section 2.11 isolates the two combinatorial dimensions of a ring: addition and multiplication. Mochizuki expresses the pair through semidirect products such as Z_l rtimes Z_l^x and F_l rtimes F_l^x, then relates the same two-fold structure to local and global Galois cohomological dimension.

Arithmetic objectTwo-dimensional readingIUT use
Z_l rtimes Z_l^x, F_l rtimes F_l^x Additive group acted on by multiplicative units. Finite and profinite prototypes for separating additive/multiplicative information.
Mixed-characteristic local field k The two cohomological dimensions of G_k. Moves the ring-dimension split into Galois-theoretic language.
1 -> O_k^x -> k^x -> k^x/O_k^x -> 1 Unit-group and value-group components; not functorially split in the required sense. Source of the unit/value separation later handled by log-shells.
C^x ~= S^1 x R_>0 Classical split analogue. Explains the Gaussian/polar-coordinate analogy without replacing the arithmetic case.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §2.11 Related: polar decoupling MLF transport log-shells

Alien Copies 2.12: Mixed-Characteristic Local Fields

Section 2.12 is the local-field transport laboratory. Local class field theory and Kummer theory reconstruct multiplicative monoids from abstract Galois data; the p-adic logarithm converts multiplication into addition; log-shells give regions large enough for upper semi-commutativity and compatible log-volume comparison.

MLF log-link model noncommuting element transport controlled by regions
ComponentSource roleWhy it matters
Local CFT and Kummer Recover monoids and Kummer images from Galois data. Makes etale-like transport meaningful after ring labels are dropped.
p-adic log Maps box-multiplication to plus-addition. Models the log-link as a rotation of the two ring dimensions.
Log-shell I A compact region containing possible images of noncommuting transports. Provides upper semi-commutativity rather than pointwise commutativity.
Log-volume Normalized so vol_log(O_k)=0 and vol_log(pO_k)=-log(p). Supplies the region-based numerical comparison used later.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §2.12 Related: two ring dimensions log-shells upper semi-commutativity

Alien Copies 2.13: Rational-Function Monoids

Section 2.13 adds the geometric dimension missing from the local-field monoids of §2.12: nonconstant rational functions on a hyperbolic curve and evaluation at special points. This is the setting in which Kummer theory is essential, because it is functorial for evaluation of functions.

Rational functions through Kummer theory geometric functions reconstructed from abstract fundamental groups
StepContentPrecision guard
Cyclotome M_X = Hom(H^2(Delta_X^cp, Zhat), Zhat) is constructed group-theoretically from Pi_X. Do not treat it as an externally labelled copy of roots of unity.
Chern class A degree-one line bundle gives lambda : mu_k^Zhat ~= M_X. The relevant cyclotomic rigidity is only controlled up to the stated orbit.
Kummer map K_X^x -> H^1(G_X, mu_k^Zhat), then through lambda. This is the bridge between Frobenius-like functions and etale-like cohomology.
Evaluation Kummer theory is functorial for evaluating rational functions at special points. This is why class-field-theory analogues are insufficient here.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §2.13 Related: kappa-coric functions IUT I kappa-coric reconstruction Collas category view

Alien Copies 2.14: Finite Harmonic Approximation

Section 2.14 explains why derivatives give way to Galois groups and etale fundamental groups. Mochizuki presents finite discrete approximations of differential calculus, Fourier analysis on the unit circle, and Hodge-Arakelov harmonic analysis on elliptic curves.

Discrete approximation ladder from finite differences to Hodge-Arakelov evaluation
ExampleFinite approximationIUT relevance
2.14.1 Difference operators replace derivatives; degree < d polynomials are evaluated at 0,...,d-1. Prototype for Hodge-Arakelov discrete function theory.
2.14.2 Functions on G_m are evaluated at mu_d; torsion points approximate S^1. Motivates Galois/fundamental groups as arithmetic tangent data and Kummer classes as logarithmic derivatives.
2.14.3 Sections on the universal extension of an elliptic curve are evaluated at l-torsion points. Gives the Hodge-Arakelov evaluation theorem behind theta-function comparison.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §2.14 Related: Hodge-Arakelov theory mono-theta evaluation Kodaira-Spencer analogy

Alien Copies §3: Multiradiality

Section 3 shifts from general arithmetic coordinate changes to the particular IUT coordinate changes. The guiding analogy is the passage from Cartesian to polar coordinates in the Gaussian integral; the technical abstraction is multiradiality, a fullness condition that lets two radial objects be compared after they are expressed through common coric data.

Source subsectionConceptWiki route
§3.1 Radial environments, multiradiality, switching, and the parallel-transport analogy. Alien multiradiality 3.1
§3.2 Complex holomorphic structures and arithmetic fundamental groups as uniradial/multiradial prototypes. Alien multiradial examples 3.2
§3.3 onward Log-theta-lattice, Kummer decouplings, Galois evaluation, log-shells, and log-volume estimates. Next pages in this source route.

The important discipline for the wiki is that "multiradial" is not just "available in many copies". It is a concrete fullness or lifting property of a radial functor, sometimes obtained by packaging orbit data. That distinction matters for later IUT III statements where the allowed indeterminacies do real work.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3 Related: general multiradiality page tautological multiradialization Collas log-theta wandering

Alien Copies 3.1: Notion of Multiradiality

Section 3.1 gives the abstract format. A radial environment has radial data, coric data, and a functorial radial algorithm from radial to coric data. It is multiradial precisely when the associated radial functor is full; it is uniradial when fullness fails.

Fullness gives switching radial data can be compared through common coric data
Definition itemSource contentFormal reading
Type/species A type of mathematical object determines a category in a universe, but the abstract category may forget data; rings isomorphic to Z/pZ illustrate this loss. Do not replace a species by the abstract category it happens to generate.
Radial environment Radial data, coric data, and a functorial algorithm from radial to coric. The algorithm is part of the structure, not an afterthought.
Multiradiality The radial functor is full; equivalently, the switching construction preserves isomorphism classes, or objects over common coric data are diagonal up to isomorphism. Later "license to confuse" claims must cite a real fullness/lifting result.
Connection analogy Fullness acts like parallel transport between two fibers over a shared base path; lifting choices behave like monodromy. Explains why indeterminacy is expected, not accidental.

The Kodaira-Spencer review in §3.1 recasts the classical argument as a degenerate multiradial situation: a line bundle is compared across two infinitesimally close alien factors by means of a common rank-two container. When the two inclusions fail to coincide, the resulting discrepancy is the Kodaira-Spencer morphism.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.1 Related: multiradial fullness Kodaira-Spencer analogy category species example

Alien Copies 3.2: Multiradial Examples

Section 3.2 gives two prototype pairs. Forgetting a single complex structure on a real two-space is uniradial, while remembering the entire GL(V)-orbit is tautologically multiradial. Similarly, a bare quotient Pi -> G is uniradial, while adding an Aut(G)-orbit of identifications makes the local arithmetic holomorphic structure multiradial.

Uniradial data becomes multiradial after orbit packaging complex and arithmetic prototypes in §3.2
ExampleUniradial formMultiradial form
Complex holomorphic structures One-dimensional C-vector space mapped to its underlying two-dimensional R-space. A real two-space equipped with a GL(V)-orbit of complex structures.
Teichmuller geometry signal A single holomorphic structure is too rigid for switching. The moduli of complex structures identifies with upper/lower half-planes and recalls dilation parameters.
Local arithmetic holomorphic structures A topological group Pi with its constructible quotient Pi -> G. A triple (Pi,G,alpha), where alpha is an Aut(G)-orbit of identifications.

These are deliberately "tautological" examples. They teach the mechanism: adding orbit data can restore fullness, but the resulting multiradiality must be accounted for as structure. This warning carries forward to IUT's log-theta-lattice pages.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.2 Related: tautological multiradialization inter-universality Collas category view

Alien Copies 3.3: Log-Theta-Lattice

Section 3.3 introduces the stage on which IUT operates: the log-theta-lattice. It is a noncommutative two-dimensional diagram of Hodge theaters, vertical log-links, and horizontal theta-links. The whole lattice is translation-symmetric, while the proof ultimately uses an "infinite H" shaped portion.

Two-dimensional lattice vertical log-links, horizontal theta-links, Hodge theaters at nodes
ObjectMeaning in §3.3(ii)Formal caution
NodeA Theta^{+-ellNF}-Hodge theater.Not just a copy; it packages a miniature arithmetic geometry around the initial theta data.
Vertical arrowA log-link, modeled on the local logarithm and log-shell construction.It rotates/juggles additive and multiplicative dimensions and is not ring-compatible.
Horizontal arrowA theta-link, modeled on q -> {q^{j^2}}.It uses multiplicative structure and requires distinct vertical coordinates.
CoricityHorizontal, vertical, or bi-coric invariance under translations of the full lattice.Finite vertical segments do not have the same symmetry as entire vertical lines.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.3(ii) Related: log-theta lattice log-links theta-links

Alien Copies 3.3(i): Initial Theta Data

The log-theta-lattice is determined, up to isomorphism, by initial theta data. Mochizuki's summary lists an elliptic curve over a number field, an algebraic closure, a prime l >= 5, selected valuations, and bad multiplicative reduction data satisfying the technical conditions of IUT I Definition 3.1.

DataSource roleDownstream use
E_F/F, Fbar, l >= 5 Elliptic curve, ambient algebraic closure, and torsion prime. Provides the l-torsion combinatorics and theta values.
F_mod, K F_mod is generated by the j-invariant; K adjoins fields of definition of l-torsion. Base for the prime-strip and Galois-action bookkeeping.
X_F, C_F Once-punctured elliptic curve and its quotient by {+/-1}. Supplies the hyperbolic orbicurve used in anabelian reconstruction.
V, V_bad Chosen valuation set and nonempty bad multiplicative reduction subset of odd residue characteristic. Index the prime-strips and the local theta-value data.
C_K -> C_K A finite etale cover of degree l from a rank-one quotient of E_K[l]. Feeds the cusp-label and symmetry apparatus of the Hodge theater.

This page intentionally points back to IUT I for the exact definition. The wiki summary is not a replacement for the initial theta data record; it is a route map for the pieces used in the later Mutually Alien Copies explanation.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.3(i) Related: initial theta data ThetaNF Hodge theaters theater symmetries

Alien Copies 3.3(iii-iv): Frobenioids and Hodge Theaters

§3.3(iii)-(iv) describes the categorical packaging. Frobenioids encode divisor, line-bundle, rational-function, and base-category data. A Theta^{+-ellNF}-Hodge theater is a system of such Frobenioids and related fundamental-group data, arranged to give a tautological solution to a global multiplicative-subspace problem.

Hodge theater as multiplicative packaging prime-strips ignore additive structure to reassemble selected primes
LayerSource descriptionWhy it is used
FrobenioidAbstract category encoding divisors and line bundles on coverings of a normal integral scheme.Captures multiplicative portions while forgetting additive structure when needed.
Model FrobenioidsThe only non-negligible Frobenioids in IUT are essentially model Frobenioids over arithmetic fundamental-group-like base categories.Keeps the category tied to anabelian reconstruction and prime strips.
Prime-stripsCollections of local data indexed by the selected valuation set V.Monoid/Galois-theoretic analogue of adeles/ideles, but with selected primes.
Global Gaussian analogueBuild a global version of the local {q^{j^2}} distribution.Motivates the entire Hodge-theater packaging.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.3(iii)-(iv) Related: Frobenioids/log-shells prime strips rational-function monoids

Alien Copies 3.3(v): Hodge-Theater Symmetries

Once the Hodge theater supplies the tautological combinatorial solution, the quotient of E_K[l] is interpreted through cusp labels. The resulting additive and multiplicative symmetry groups are F_l rtimes {+/-1} and F_l^x/{+/-1}.

SymmetrySet acted onGeometric reading
F_l rtimes {+/-1} F_l = {-l*,...,0,...,l*} Geometric automorphisms of X_K; additive/unipotent side.
F_l^x/{+/-1} F_l^* = {1,...,l*} Quotient of arithmetic automorphisms of C_K; multiplicative/toral side.
Gluing F_l superset F_l^x -> F_l^* Connects individual prime-strips, entire label sets, and symmetry actions.

Mochizuki stresses that these symmetries are not decorative. They are used in the Kummer-theoretic arguments of §3.6, and they mirror additive and toral symmetries from the upper half-plane and p-adic Hecke correspondence analogies.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.3(v) Related: F_l± groups/torsors F_l multiradial combinatorics theta-links

Alien Copies 3.3(vi): Log-Links

A log-link is a vertical arrow of the log-theta-lattice. It relates copies of the local data Pi_X acts O_k^triangleright by the logarithmic operation of §2.12, but the underlying copies of Pi_X must be regarded as distinct abstract topological groups related only by an indeterminate isomorphism.

Log-link pointContentConsequence
D-Hodge theatersThe log-link induces an indeterminate isomorphism between the associated D-Hodge theaters.D-Hodge theaters are vertically coric.
Arithmetic holomorphic structuresEach Pi_X can reconstruct base-field ring structure by mono-anabelian algorithms.The group is an abstract representative of arithmetic holomorphic structure.
Arithmetic analytic continuationLocalizations and gluings in a theater or log-link act like analytic continuation between number fields along prime-strips.Implemented through mono-anabelian reconstruction and Belyi cuspidalization.
CuspidalizationDense opens of P^1 minus {0,1,infty} can be recovered via suitable Belyi maps and open subgroups.Relates function theory on a curve and on dense opens, formally resembling analytic continuation.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.3(vi) Related: MLF transport absolute anabelian mutation full log-link

Alien Copies 3.3(vii): Theta-Links

The theta-link is the horizontal gluing, roughly modeled on q -> {q^{j^2}} for j=1,...,l*. Technically, it glues domain and codomain Hodge-theater data through unit-group portions, local value-group portions, and global realified Frobenioids.

PortionDomain sideCodomain side
Unit group (a_Theta): local data G_k acts O_k^{x,mu} with integral structures for open subgroups. (a_q): analogous local unit data in the codomain theater.
Local value group (b_Theta): formal monoids generated by {q_v^{j^2}}. (b_q): formal monoids generated by q_v.
Global value group (c_Theta): global realified Frobenioid with product-formula relations. (c_q): corresponding global realified Frobenioid.
Coricity Unit data is horizontally coric; the topological group portion is bi-coric.

The theta-link is ring-incompatible just like the log-link. Its Frobenius-like portions are initially confined to a fixed Hodge theater, while the etale-like topological group portions are only meaningful up to indeterminate isomorphism. The associated global realified Frobenioid objects are the theta-pilot and q-pilot objects used in the later log-volume estimates.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.3(vii) Related: theta-link definition theta-pilot object Hodge-theater symmetries

Alien Copies 3.4: Kummer Decoupling

Section 3.4 begins the route to a multiradial representation of the theta-pilot objects. The radial algorithm forgets from the theta-link domain data (a_Theta,b_Theta,c_Theta) to the horizontally coric unit-group portion (a_Theta). The first step is to construct multiradial cyclotomic-rigidity and decoupling algorithms for special functions.

StepSource roleLater target
§3.4 Decouple Kummer theory for special rational functions and theta functions from unit-group data. Multiradial cyclotomic rigidity for value-group data.
§3.6 Evaluate these functions at special points via Galois sections. Special values acting on processions of log-shells.
§3.7 Use the multiradial representation for log-volume estimates. Theta-pilot comparison feeding the 3.12 corridor.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.4 Related: theta-links theta/q pilot objects theta monoid multiradiality

Alien Copies 3.4(i): Essential Kummer Role

The theta-link exists because its monoids and Frobenioids are treated as abstract Frobenius-like structures without their construction histories. But to compare these objects across links, IUT uses etale-like Galois data and Kummer theory to compute the indeterminacies needed for simultaneous execution.

IssueSource statementConsequence
Frobenius-like abstraction If the monoids remembered how they were built, domain and codomain data would generally not be isomorphic. The link is not a ring/scheme morphism; it is an abstract gluing.
Etale-like computation Galois groups and fundamental groups compute opposite-side ring/scheme information via mono-anabelian transport. Indeterminacy is measured through Kummer compatibility, not ignored.
Cyclotomic rigidity Kummer classes require a group of torsion elements to be identified with an etale-like cyclotome. This is where naive functorial rigidity can conflict with multiradiality.
Naive local-field route The natural Zhat^x-action on coric unit data is incompatible with the functorial cyclotomic rigidity isomorphism. Value-group data must be decoupled from (a_Theta).
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.4(i) Related: MLF transport mono-theta vs MLF rigidity rigidity taxonomy

Alien Copies 3.4(ii): κ-Coric Decoupling

The global value-group data (c_Theta) is controlled through special rational functions on the orbicurve. Mochizuki uses the pseudo-monoid of κ-coric rational functions: functions whose values at strictly critical points are roots of unity and whose elementary closure properties suffice for Kummer theory.

FeatureContentUse
Global data (c_Theta) is interpreted through realifications of categories of arithmetic line bundles indexed by j=1,...,l*. Reduces the problem to copies of F_mod.
κ-coric functions Special rational functions on |C_Fmod| restricting to roots of unity at strictly critical points. Evaluation can be performed etale-theoretically by restricting Kummer classes to decomposition groups.
Pseudo-monoid κ-coric functions are not closed under product, but positive powers and roots of unity behave well enough. Kummer theory can still be conducted on the restricted class.
Cyclotomic rigidity Uses the Q_>0 cap Zhat^x = {1} argument from rational-function Kummer theory. Multiradial, but not compatible with profinite topology.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.4(ii) Related: κ-coric functions κ-coric definition rational-function monoids

Alien Copies 3.4(iii): Theta-Function Rigidity

At bad valuations, the local value-group data (b_Theta) is controlled through normalized reciprocals of l-th roots of theta functions on a tempered cover of a Tate curve. Constant-multiple rigidity comes from evaluation at zero-labeled points, where the value is a 2l-th root of unity.

ObjectSource roleRigidity effect
Tempered cover Y_v -> X_v Universal cover of the loop dual graph of the Tate curve special fiber; Galois group noncanonically Z. Provides discrete component/cusp labels.
Theta function Zeros at cusps with multiplicity one; poles on irreducible components with orders j^2. The zero order one is essential for mod N Kummer rigidity without {+/-1} ambiguity.
Zero-labeled evaluation points Points over order-four torsion meeting the zero-labeled component. Evaluation decouples the theta function from unit-group data.
Discrete rigidity obstruction Poles are not compatible with the full Z-symmetry; coherent basepoints would require profinite completion. Explains why discrete rigidity is needed to avoid Zhat-powers of divisors/functions.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.4(iii) Related: theta-value indeterminacy etale theta standard type discrete rigidity

Alien Copies 3.4(iv): Mono-Theta Environments

Mono-theta environments repair the incompatibility of the naive theta-function approach with Z-symmetries. Instead of using the ratio of algebraic and theta sections directly, they package the G_m-torsor, a symmetry-generated automorphism group, and the theta section in a form compatible up to isomorphism with the desired symmetries.

Triple componentMeaningWhy it works
(a_mu-Theta) The G_m-torsor L_v^x. Compatible with the Z-symmetries.
(b_mu-Theta) Automorphisms generated by Z-symmetries and multiplication by constants. Stabilized under the meromorphic multiplications induced by translating the theta section.
(c_mu-Theta) The theta section on the pulled-back torsor. Admits the needed compatibility after allowing the automorphism group.

Passing to mod N tempered fundamental-group versions of l-th roots of this triple gives mono-theta environments. Their rigidity properties are decoupled from unit data, compatible with tempered topology, compatible with F_l rtimes {+/-1} symmetries, and formally tied to the quadratic commutator/curvature structure of theta groups.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.4(iv) Related: mono-theta environments mono-theta rigidity Hodge-theater symmetries

Alien Copies 3.4(v): Cyclotomic Rigidity Taxonomy

§3.4(v) summarizes three rigidity routes. The important distinction is not whether a cyclotomic rigidity statement exists, but whether it is multiradial and whether it is compatible with the relevant profinite or tempered topology.

ApproachApplied toMultiradial?Topology-compatible?
Local class field theory / Brauer-group route (a_Theta) No: it remains tied to unit data. Yes.
Mono-theta environments (b_Theta) Yes: decoupled from unit data. Yes: works through mod N tempered fundamental groups.
κ-coric rational functions (c_Theta) Yes: decoupled from unit data. No, but this is not needed for the global data in the same way.

This taxonomy is a useful audit page for later 3.11-3.12 claims: a proof step cannot cite "cyclotomic rigidity" generically. It must specify which route is used, what data it applies to, and which compatibility properties are available.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.4(v) Related: mono-theta vs MLF rigidity theta monoid multiradiality Kummer decoupling overview

Alien Copies 3.5: Use of Frobenioids

Section 3.5 narrows the role of Frobenioids in IUT. Mochizuki says the Frobenioid papers were not written as an optimally efficient extraction of just the pieces later used in IUT. For the wiki, this means Frobenioids should be routed to the specific IUT packages they support, not treated as an unlimited prerequisite swamp.

Frobenioid classIUT use in §3.5Audit consequence
Archimedean Frobenioids Essentially equivalent to O_C^triangleright, the monoid of nonzero complex numbers of norm <=1. Can be mostly ignored at the level of this route.
Nonarchimedean model Frobenioids Equivalent to data such as G_k acts O_k^triangleright, Pi_X acts O_k^triangleright, or subquotients. Track the underlying ind-topological monoid/action rather than invoking general Frobenioid theory everywhere.
Global Frobenioids Categories of arithmetic line bundles on number fields, possibly realified. Important because Picard groups have non-torsion elements and arithmetic degrees can be nonzero.
Tempered Frobenioids Used through theta monoids and mono-theta environments. The needed pieces are the data (a_{t-F}) and (b_{t-F}), not all of EtTh §3-§5.

The key practical conclusion is explicit: for IUT study, one may either accept the construction of theta monoids and mono-theta environments as a supplied construction or reconstruct them from the scheme-theoretic objects of Etale Theta Functions §1-§2. This should prevent the wiki from over-indexing on general Frobenioid theory where IUT only uses a smaller interface.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.5 Related: Frobenioid category packaging mono-theta environments theta monoid multiradiality

Alien Copies 3.6: Galois Evaluation

Section 3.6 studies Galois evaluation of the κ-coric rational functions and theta functions from §3.4. The basic route is abstract functions -> values: pass from Frobenius-like functions to etale-like functions by Kummer theory, restrict to decomposition subgroups of closed points, then use base-field Kummer theory to recover Frobenius-like values.

Galois evaluation pipeline Kummer, decomposition groups, inverse Kummer

Mochizuki stresses that the suitable evaluation subgroups are the decomposition subgroups coming from closed points; for theta functions one also needs a combinatorial Section-Conjecture-type input. The full §3.6 analysis then has to track labels, symmetries, and log-shell containers.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.6 Related: Kummer decoupling log-shell actions processions

Alien Copies 3.6(i): Etale-Picture Symmetries

Passage to the etale-picture makes D-Hodge theaters from distinct vertical lines permutable: the radial data is a D-Hodge theater up to indeterminate isomorphism, the coric data is the etale-like G_v portion of (a_Theta) or (a_q). This is tautologically multiradial, but still nontrivial because the log-theta-lattice itself does not permute vertical lines before this passage.

SymmetrySource behaviorInterpretation
F_l^* Independent actions glued at the common symbol 0. Combinatorial prototype of multiradiality; tied to global value-group data (c_Theta).
F_l rtimes {+/-1} Mutually interfering actions glued at 0. Combinatorial prototype of uniradiality; tied to unit data and local value-group data.
Formal averages [0], [|j|], [succ], [>] are compatible with the gluing 0,succ -> >. Prepares weighted log-volume averages.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.6(i) Related: Hodge-theater symmetries F_l multiradial combinatorics D-theta etale pictures

Alien Copies 3.6(ii): Theta Values and Local Diagonals

At bad valuations, the F_l rtimes {+/-1}-symmetry gives symmetrizing isomorphisms between labelled copies (G_v)_t. These isomorphisms are free of inner automorphism indeterminacy and form local diagonals that contain the theta values q^{j^2} obtained by Galois evaluation.

ComponentSource contentUse
Tempered groups Pi_v subset Pi_v^+- subset Pi_v^cor, reconstructible from Pi_v. Carry cusp-label and evaluation-point structure at bad places.
Conjugate synchronization F_l rtimes {+/-1} acts by conjugation in Pi_v^cor, producing inner-indeterminacy-free symmetrizing maps. Synchronizes labels and signs across the left-hand Hodge-theater side.
Evaluation route Restrict theta Kummer classes first to line-segment decomposition groups, then to evaluation-point decomposition groups. Produces q^{j^2} up to 2l-th roots of unity.
Topology compatibility The Kummer theory here only involves local data (a_Theta), (b_Theta). It remains compatible with tempered/profinite topology, hence with log-link power-series comparisons.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.6(ii) Related: theta-function rigidity theta evaluation orbits theta exponent compatibility

Alien Copies 3.6(iii): NF Values and Global Diagonals

The F_l^*-symmetry handles number-field values. By applying linear disjointness involving the κ-coric solvable extension and the Galois closure of C_K, elements of F_mod or F_sol are obtained by Galois evaluation of κ-coric rational functions.

PointTheta caseNF case
Symmetry F_l rtimes {+/-1}. F_l^*, isolated from the additive symmetry.
Values Label-dependent theta values q^{j^2}. Total multiplicative monoids F_mod^x or F_sol^x; label dependence appears later as mono-analytic weights j^2.
Topology Topology-compatible and needed for log-link comparison. Not topology-compatible, but no natural-log power-series comparison is needed at this stage.
Weighted copy warning Weights are attached to theta values. A holomorphic copy (F_mod^x)^{j^2} is not additive-structure compatible; it only makes sense mono-analytically.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.6(iii) Related: κ-coric decoupling κ-coric functions rigidity taxonomy

Alien Copies 3.6(iv): Actions on Log-Shells

Galois-evaluation outputs are not naturally multiradial and are not generally local units. The solution is to use the log-link and the four log-shell variants to build multiradial containers for theta values and number-field copies. This is also where the separation of Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3 starts to appear.

Log-shell typeIndispensable role in §3.6(iv)
Holomorphic Frobenius-like The log-link can be applied to it, and it remains tied to a fixed vertical position meaningful for the theta-link.
Holomorphic etale-like Relates holomorphic Frobenius-like log-shells at different vertical positions while accounting for the log-link.
Mono-analytic Frobenius-like Constructed directly from the horizontally coric unit data (a_Theta), (a_q) and fixed at a vertical position.
Mono-analytic etale-like Constructed from the bicoric G_k data and links mono-analytic Kummer theory to holomorphic Kummer theory.

The key decoupling is to compare through vertical position zero. Without mono-analytic Frobenius-like log-shells, log-link and theta-link indeterminacies act simultaneously. With them, the theta-link side carries (Ind1,Ind2), while the vertical log-link comparison carries Ind3.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.6(iv) Related: log-shell variants upper semi log-Kummer Collas log-theta wandering

Alien Copies 3.6(v): Processions and Tensor Packets

Processions provide a mono-analytic substitute for labels in F_l^*, |F_l|, or F_l, since those labels are not horizontally coric and have no immediate multiradial formulation. The replacement is a nested diagram of finite container sets with permutation indeterminacy.

ObjectSource meaningUse
S_{j+1}^{+-}={0,...,j} A container set for labels 0,...,j, subject to arbitrary permutation automorphisms. Replaces direct label access by controlled container indeterminacy.
Capsule A collection of etale-like mono-analytic prime-strips indexed by a container set. Produces log-shells at each valuation.
Tensor packet Tensor products of log-shells indexed by one container set. Natural target for log-volume computations.
Actions Theta values q^{j^2} and copies of F_mod^x act on these packets up to the listed indeterminacies. Feeds directly into the §3.7 multiradial representation.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.6(v) Related: processions/capsules tensor packets log-shell actions

Alien Copies 3.7: Log-Volume Estimates

Section 3.7 summarizes the chain from IUT III Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12 and onward to IUT IV. The preceding §3.4-§3.6 machinery produces a multiradial representation of the theta-pilot object, and log-volume estimates then compare this representation with the q-pilot object.

Source itemRoleWiki page
§3.7(i)Theorem 3.11-style multiradial representation up to Ind1/2/3.3.11 representation
§3.7(ii)Corollary 3.12-style log-volume upper-bound comparison.3.12 estimate
§3.7(iii)Stewart-Yu comparison and vertical-shift interpretation.Stewart-Yu comparison
§3.7(iv)IUT IV leading-term computation and Szpiro/ABC consequences.leading term
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.7 Related: IUT III Theorem 3.11 route Corollary 3.12 single theta-link IUT IV Theorem 1.10

Alien Copies 3.7(i): 3.11 Representation

The §3.7(i) summary of IUT III Theorem 3.11 says that the theta-link domain data (a_Theta,b_Theta,c_Theta) can be expressed multiradially, up to Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3, relative to the radial algorithm (a_Theta,b_Theta,c_Theta) -> (a_Theta).

IndeterminacySource descriptionAudit reading
Ind1 Etale-transport indeterminacies from automorphisms of the G_k data in (a_Theta). Controls transport of etale-like structures; not a log-shell containment.
Ind2 Kummer-detachment indeterminacies from identifying mono-analytic Frobenius-like and etale-like log-shells; concretely isometries of O_k^{x,mu} preserving lattices. Acts on the mono-analytic theta-link side.
Ind3 Kummer-detachment indeterminacies from upper semi-commutativity of the log-Kummer correspondence along one vertical line. The one-sided upper-bound source in the 3.12 corridor.
Invisible Root-of-unity and +/-1 ambiguities that are absorbed by stability of the output data. Do not count these as new independent proof resources.

Mochizuki interprets this as stronger than merely linking the domain to the codomain: any data isomorphic to the domain data can be represented through the conventional scheme-like representation of the domain, compatibly with its own scheme-like representation, after allowing the specified indeterminacies.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.7(i) Related: Theorem 3.11 core log-shell actions processions

Alien Copies 3.7(ii): 3.12 Log-Volume Estimate

The §3.7(ii) estimate begins on the fixed RHS arithmetic holomorphic structure of the theta-link. The q-pilot object is sent through the theta-link to the theta-pilot isomorphism class, then the multiradial representation and log-shell log-volume comparison are applied.

Step groupContent3.12 relevance
1-3 Start with RHS q-pilot, transport its isomorphism class to the LHS theta-pilot, then apply the multiradial representation. Fixes the RHS holomorphic structure for the computation.
4-5 Log-volume is invariant under Ind1/Ind2 and invisible indeterminacies; Ind3 converts the theta-pilot log-volume into an upper bound. This is the formal one-sided part of the dispute corridor.
6-8 Comparison is with the q-pilot isomorphism class, not fixed local trivializations; output is converted to O_k-modules via the holomorphic hull. Prevents reading the comparison as equality of concrete local sections.
9-12 Log-volume becomes arithmetic degree; log-Kummer compatibility preserves the inequality; final result bounds the q-pilot by the theta-pilot hull. Gives the Corollary 3.12-style inequality.

In the summary formula, the log-volume of the theta-pilot object with mild indeterminacies and holomorphic hull is greater than or equal to the log-volume of the q-pilot object, whose value is negative and negligible relative to the main left-hand terms.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.7(ii) Related: single theta-link rule theta/q pilot objects tensor/log-volume corridor

Alien Copies 3.7(iii): Stewart-Yu Comparison

Mochizuki compares the vertical-shift feature of IUT with the weak ABC-type inequality of Stewart-Yu, where an undesired exponential appears in the upper bound. In IUT, the relevant deviation is tied to the single log-link iterate separating the horizontally coric data from the data of ultimate interest.

Comparison pointMeaning
Stewart-YuWeak ABC-type bound with an extra exponential operation.
IUT vertical shiftThe horizontally coric portion differs from the target data by one log-link iterate.
Ind3This same vertical shift gives the upper semi-commutativity indeterminacy used in the estimate.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.7(iii) Related: log-shell actions 3.12 estimate

Alien Copies 3.7(iv): Leading-Term Computation

§3.7(iv) points to IUT IV Theorem 1.10 for the detailed computation of the left-hand side of the 3.12 inequality. Mochizuki records the simple leading term calculation that motivates the framework.

TermLeading contribution
Tensor packet at label j(j+1) log(d_K)
Multiplication by theta value q^{j^2}- j^2/(2l) log(q)
Summed leading terml^2/48 * (6 log(d_K) - log(q))
Conjectural output routeStrong Szpiro inequality for suitable initial theta data, then Vojta/Szpiro/ABC via noncritical Belyi maps.

The point is not that the leading-term computation alone proves ABC. Rather, the computation identifies the quantity that must be bounded below; IUT is presented here as the framework in which this Hodge-Arakelov-inspired computation can be performed after the multiradial/log-volume machinery.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.7(iv) Related: IUT IV theorem 1.10 suitable initial theta data Szpiro to ABC

Alien Copies 3.8: Gaussian Comparison

§3.8 compares the arithmetic coordinate changes and multiradial representations of §2-§3 with the classical computation of the Gaussian integral. The comparison is not a proof step by itself; it is Mochizuki's structural guide for why alien copies, coordinate changes, radial/angular decoupling, and error factors appear in the IUT architecture.

Gaussian computation as structural analogy Mutually Alien Copies §3.8
Source clusterContentPage
(1gau)-(4gau)Naive coordinate change, two alien copies, two dimensions, and infinitesimal/net-mass interpretation.theta-link comparison
(5gau)-(8gau)Local coordinate changes, MLF examples, passage to polar coordinates, and Kummer decoupling.transport comparison
(9gau)-(10gau)Radial evaluation by quadraticity and angular evaluation by logarithms on units.radial/angular comparison
(11gau)The final Gaussian error factor is compared with the 3.7 log-different error factor from indeterminacies on log-shells.error factor
Final paragraphThe left side of {q^(j^2)} -> q is rigid in a way the right side is not.left/right rigidity
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.8 Related: Gaussian error-factor review 3.12 estimate Collas category view

Alien Copies 3.8(1-4): Gaussian and Theta-Link Setup

The first four Gaussian comparison points align the elementary-looking substitution e^(-x^2) -> u with the theta-link assignment {q^(j^2)} -> q. They also match the doubled Gaussian integral, the two dimensions of the Euclidean plane, and the net-mass view of integration with the basic alien-copy architecture.

Gaussian stepIUT-side comparisonReading
(1gau) naive substitution The theta-link assignment from theta values q^(j^2) to q. The analogy starts with an apparently illegitimate coordinate change that later receives a controlled correction.
(2gau) two alien copies The two theta-Hodge theaters in the domain and codomain of the theta-link. One does not identify two copies inside one fixed conventional scheme theory.
(3gau) two plane dimensions The two dimensions of the log-theta-lattice and the two combinatorial dimensions of a ring. This is where the MLF/NF/ring-dimension discussion of §2.11 re-enters.
(4gau) net mass from zero-mass pieces Prime-strips, heights as log-volumes, and the Frobenius-like/etale-like dichotomy. Local summation and infinitesimal/limit behavior are separated conceptually.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.8(1gau)-(4gau) Related: theta-links ring dimensions heights/log-volumes

Alien Copies 3.8(5-8): Transport and Decoupling

The middle comparison points treat coordinate change as the main mechanism. Local Euclidean coordinate changes correspond to mono-anabelian transport; linear changes in the plane correspond to arithmetic coordinate changes for MLFs; and passage to polar coordinates corresponds to multiradial transport from the log-theta lattice to the etale-picture.

Gaussian featureIUT featureDependency
Effect of local coordinate changes on integrals Computing arithmetic coordinate-change effects by mono-anabelian transport. Uses the §2.7-§2.11 transport viewpoint.
Unipotent maps, toral dilations, rotations MLF examples of arithmetic coordinate changes. Connects §2.12 with the theta-link context.
Cartesian-to-polar passage Parallel transport between radial data collections, especially log-theta lattice to etale-picture. Feeds the §3.7 multiradial representation.
Radial/angular decoupling Kummer theory for functions via multiradial decoupling and cyclotomic rigidity. Uses §3.4 rigidity before log-volume estimates are applied.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.8(5gau)-(8gau) Related: mono-anabelian transport MLF transport Kummer decoupling

Alien Copies 3.8(9-10): Radial and Angular Evaluation

In the Gaussian computation, the radial integral is tractable because of the quadratic exponent, while the angular integral is evaluated over complex units using the logarithm. Mochizuki compares these two facts to theta-group commutator quadraticity and to log-shells as containers for Galois-evaluation output.

Gaussian sideIUT-side comparison3.12 corridor relevance
Radial integral Formal rigidity of mono-theta environments arising from quadratic commutator structure in theta groups. Controls the value-group side of the theta-link.
Angular integral over units Galois evaluation and log-shells, including iterated log-link actions on local units. Places the unit-group side into the log-shell containers where indeterminacies act.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.8(9gau)-(10gau) Related: theta rigidity Galois evaluation log-shell actions

Alien Copies 3.8(11): Error Factor Interpretation

The final Gaussian step says the naive substitution can be justified after paying an error factor from the angular integral. Mochizuki compares this to the §3.7 computation: a naive Frobenius-style height bound over number fields is justified only after allowing the log-different error factor arising from Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3 acting on log-shells.

ComponentGaussian roleIUT comparison
Naive change e^(-x^2) -> u is not accepted without correction. Naive Gaussian-Frobenius height bounding is not accepted without indeterminacy control.
Error factor Square root of the angular integral. Log-different term produced by the log-shell indeterminacies.
Traceability warning One should not expect explicit portions of the original integral to match radial/angular pieces. Mochizuki likewise warns that tracing the original value-group portions through tensor packets of log-shells is not readily computable.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.8(11gau) Related: 3.12 log-volume estimate 3.11 representation Scholze-Stix critique page

Alien Copies 3.8: Left/Right Rigidity of the Theta-Link

The closing observation of §3.8 separates the two sides of the theta-link assignment. The left side {q^(j^2)} cannot be freely replaced by q^lambda or {q^(N*j^2)}, while the right side q can be replaced by a rational power without materially improving the estimates.

Attempted replacementSource reason it is not allowed or not useful
Replace left side by q^lambda No corresponding theory of multiradial decoupling/cyclotomic rigidity of the kind available for theta functions and mono-theta environments.
Replace left side by q^(N*j^2), N >= 2 The first power of reciprocal theta functions and the Galois-evaluation passage from abstract functions to values play a special role.
Replace right side by q^lambda Mochizuki says this has no substantive effect and gives no substantive improvement in the §3.7 estimates.

For the wiki and Lean project, this page marks a constraint on any simplified model: the two sides of the theta-link cannot be treated as formally symmetric parameter slots.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.8 final discussion Related: theta-function rigidity Galois evaluation IUT IV estimates

Alien Copies 3.9: Relation to Hodge-Arakelov Theory

§3.9 rereads the §3 multiradial representation through scheme-theoretic Hodge-Arakelov theory. Mochizuki presents the older Hodge-Arakelov calculation as a precursor of the IUT log-volume computation, not as a substitute for the theta-link and log-shell machinery.

From Hodge-Arakelov computation to IUT log-volume computation Mutually Alien Copies §3.9
Source itemMain contentPage
§3.9(i)Hodge filtration, theta trivialization, and the rough weighted package q^(j^2)*(Omega_log)^j.Hodge filtration
§3.9(ii)Log-shells as localized arithmetic analogues of logarithmic differentials, and the Hodge-Arakelov computation as precursor of the IUT leading term.HA/IUT comparison
§3.9(iii)Analytic torsion, mono-analytic transport, and multiradial containers.analytic torsion
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.9 Related: Hodge-Arakelov motivation 3.7 leading term Gaussian comparison

Alien Copies 3.9(i): Hodge Filtration and Theta Trivialization

Mochizuki starts from the fundamental theorem of scheme-theoretic Hodge-Arakelov theory: sections of a line bundle restricted to a torsion subscheme are compared with torsion-value data. After theta-group irreducibility removes redundant dimensions, the source and target may be read roughly as l-dimensional vector spaces.

SideSource structureRough arithmetic line-bundle reading
Left side Natural Hodge filtration by relative degree. Adjacent pieces behave like tensor powers of the cotangent-line dual; with Omega_log = omega_E^2, the relevant label j contributes ((Omega_log)^j)^vee.
Right side Theta trivialization by torsion labels. At potentially multiplicative places, the label contributes a theta factor of the form q^(j^2).
Combined rough package Dual of the left contribution tensored with the right contribution. Weighted average over labels of q^(j^2)*(Omega_log)^j.

The resulting global degree calculation has the same leading-term profile as a positive tensor power of the discriminant modular form: the geometric prototype behind the later log-volume leading term.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.9(i) Related: theta values q^(j^2) leading-term computation

Alien Copies 3.9(ii): Hodge-Arakelov/IUT Comparison

The comparison point is explicit: log-shells are treated as localized absolute arithmetic analogues of logarithmic differentials. Under this reading, the Hodge-Arakelov package q^(j^2)*(Omega_log)^j becomes a scheme-theoretic precursor of the j-labeled portion of the §3.7 multiradial representation.

Hodge-Arakelov itemIUT analogue in §3.9(ii)
Logarithmic differentials on the compactified elliptic-moduli side. Log-shells, locally identified in tame p-adic cases with the dual fractional ideal to the different.
Label-wise line bundle q^(j^2)*(Omega_log)^j. The j-labeled component of the multiradial representation.
Global degree leading-term computation. The leading term of the log-volume on the left side of the §3.7/3.12 inequality.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.9(ii) Related: log-shell background 3.11 multiradial representation 3.12 estimate

Alien Copies 3.9(iii): Analytic Torsion Interpretation

Mochizuki then compares IUT with analytic torsion. In Arakelov theory, analytic torsion measures how holomorphic global sections sit inside a larger L^2-type Hilbert space that is topological rather than holomorphic in nature.

Classical analytic-torsion patternIUT-side reading
A holomorphic section space varies with holomorphic moduli. Arithmetic holomorphic structures vary across Hodge-theater contexts.
The ambient Hilbert space is topological and insensitive to those deformations. Log-shells serve as mono-analytic multiradial containers for the varying holomorphic structures.
Scheme-theoretic Hodge-Arakelov theory discretizes this picture over number fields. IUT is presented as a global Galois-theoretic version over number fields.

This page is a conceptual bridge only. The formal corridor still has to be supplied by the log-shell, theta-link, multiradiality, and log-volume constructions rather than by the analogy with analytic torsion.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.9(iii) Related: mono-anabelian transport mono-analytic log-shells Collas category view

Alien Copies 3.10: Tripodal Transport

§3.10 presents tripodal transport as a recurring proof pattern: reduce a hard property for a given arithmetic holomorphic structure to an alternative property that can be transported to, and checked on, a tripod. Mochizuki then reads IUT, p-adic Teichmuller theory, scheme-theoretic Hodge-Arakelov theory, combinatorial anabelian geometry, and Bogomolov's proof through this template.

Tripodal transport pattern Mutually Alien Copies §3.10
SubsectionRolePage
§3.10(i)General five-step template and the need to sacrifice fixed scheme/ring compatibility.template
§3.10(ii)IUT's 3.12 estimate as tripodal transport through the multiradial representation.IUT
§3.10(iii)-(iv)Earlier p-adic Teichmuller and Hodge-Arakelov examples.p-adic / HA
§3.10(v)-(vi)Combinatorial anabelian geometry and Bogomolov-Szpiro analogy.anabelian / Bogomolov
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.10 Related: IUT tripodal reading Bogomolov-Szpiro analogy Collas category view

Alien Copies 3.10(i): Tripodal Template

The template has five steps: start with a nontrivial property for a given arithmetic holomorphic structure; split it into a relatively trivial local part plus an alternative property; use a non-holomorphic transport mechanism to move the alternative property to a tripod; verify it there; and return to the original property.

Step clusterMeaningWarning
(1trp)-(2trp) Identify the hard property and separate a comparatively simple piece from an alternative property. The simple piece remains tied to the original holomorphic structure.
(3trp)-(4trp) Transport the alternative property to the tripod and prove it there. The transport is typically not compatible with the fixed scheme/ring structure.
(5trp) Recombine the simple part and transported alternative property. The method depends on rigidity strong enough to compensate for discarded structure.

Mochizuki ties the need to abandon fixed scheme/ring compatibility to the Frobenius-like versus etale-like distinction: one gains transport only by working with structures whose intrinsic rigidity survives outside the original scheme-theoretic interpretation.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.10(i) Related: Frobenius-like/etale-like terminology inter-universality

Alien Copies 3.10(ii): IUT via Tripodal Transport

Mochizuki recasts the 3.7 log-volume estimate as a tripodal-transport argument. The relatively simple part is carried out on the RHS arithmetic holomorphic structure of the theta-link; the nontrivial transport is supplied by the multiradial representation, whose input-side rigidity is built from the Tate-curve theta function and mono-anabelian reconstruction.

Tripodal roleIUT reading
Desired property The fundamental log-volume estimate, identified with the §3.7(12est) target.
Relatively simple RHS part Operations such as forming the holomorphic hull inside the fixed RHS arithmetic holomorphic structure.
Parallel transport mechanism The multiradial representation of Theorem 3.11, with the IPL/SHE/APT/HIS properties cited in the source.
Tripod-side rigidity The theta function on the Tate curve, understood as function-theoretic p-adic arithmetic geometry of a tripod with the 0 and infinity cusps interchanged by symmetry.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.10(ii) Related: 3.11 multiradial representation 3.12 estimate theta-function rigidity

Alien Copies 3.10(iii): p-adic Teichmuller Example

The p-adic Teichmuller example transports canonical objects from the tautological elliptic family over the tripod to general p-adic hyperbolic curves. The source emphasizes canonical indigenous bundles, canonical Frobenius liftings, and associated Galois representations.

ToolTripodal-transport role
Ordinary Frobenius lifting A p-adic generalization of the basic map T -> T^p on the tripod.
Nilcurve moduli morphism Finite, flat, local complete intersection structure supplies a transportable moduli mechanism.
Ordinary-locus rigidity Strong deformation rigidity supplies the transport potency.
p-curvature and differentials These replace anabelian rigidity as the technical source of control in this example.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.10(iii) Related: tripodal template GT species perspective

Alien Copies 3.10(iv): Hodge-Arakelov via Tripodal Transport

In the Hodge-Arakelov example, the evaluation morphism for sections at torsion points is proved to be an isomorphism by checking Tate curves through explicit theta-derivative computations and then transporting the result over the compactified moduli stack by degree calculations.

ComponentSource reading
Tripod check Tate-curve theta-function computations represent the tripod-side verification.
Transport over moduli Degree comparison for vector bundles on the compactified moduli stack transports the isomorphism.
Alternative transport Mochizuki also points to an approach using Frobenius and Verschiebung in positive characteristic.
IUT relation This theory is not logically anabelian, but is presented as a key motivation for the later anabelian reformulation.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.10(iv) Related: Hodge-Arakelov relation Hodge filtration/theta trivialization

Alien Copies 3.10(v): Combinatorial Anabelian Example

The combinatorial anabelian example is Mochizuki's most transparent prototype. It concerns injectivity of an outer Galois representation attached to etale fundamental groups of configuration spaces, reduced to tripods and totally degenerate pointed stable curves.

MoveMeaning
Factor through configuration-space levels The original representation factors through higher configuration-space automorphism groups.
Reduce injectivity of forgetful maps Injectivity depends only on type (g,r) and can be checked on totally degenerate curves glued from tripods.
Use tripod homomorphism A natural map to the tripod automorphism group reduces one part to Belyi's injectivity theorem.
Conceptual point The scheme-morphism route looks intractable; the group-theoretic decomposition route supplies the transport.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.10(v) Related: anabelian background current anabelian work

Alien Copies 3.10(vi): Bogomolov Proof as Tripodal Transport

Mochizuki also reads Bogomolov's proof of the geometric Szpiro conjecture over the complex numbers as a generalized tripodal-transport argument. The tripod is viewed as an algebraic version of the circle, and the proof compares holomorphic SL2(Z)-data with real-analytic SL2(R)-data.

Bogomolov sideIUT-side correspondence stated in §3.10(vi)
Canonical lifts of unipotent elements to the universal cover of SL2(Z). Etale theta-function theory.
Aut_+(S^1)-invariant geometry and the conjugacy-invariant displacement map. Mono-analytic log-shells and log-volume estimates.
Additive and multiplicative dimensions confused in the single circle dimension. Indeterminacies Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3.
SL2(R) side IUT III Theorem 3.11.
SL2(Z) side plus character chi The derivation of IUT III Corollary 3.12 from Theorem 3.11.

This page is especially relevant to the formalization project: it states a source-side analogy between a transport estimate and the 3.11-to-3.12 corridor, but it is still an analogy. The Lean corridor must encode the actual IUT structures and inequalities separately.

Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.10(vi) Related: Bogomolov-Szpiro analogy Theorem 3.11 representation Corollary 3.12 estimate

Alien Copies 3.11: Conceptual Discomfort

§3.11 analyzes elementary sources of discomfort that Mochizuki thinks mirror misunderstandings of the log-link, theta-link, and multiradial representation. For this wiki, the section is treated as interpretive source context. Its proof-critical content is the warning not to collapse simultaneous theta-link assignments into an either/or reading.

Logical guardrail for the theta-link Mutually Alien Copies §3.11(iv)
Source itemWiki readingPage
§3.11(i)-(iii)Elementary analogies: induction, logarithm domain/codomain, and ABC as domination by weaker multiplicative data.induction/log/ABC
§3.11(iv)The theta-link requires simultaneous validity of two labeled assignments, not an either/or collapse.AND/OR
§3.11(v)The multiradial algorithm must form a closed loop on one sufficiently strong and weak prime-strip type.closed loops
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.11 Related: theta-links 3.12 estimate Scholze-Stix critique page

Alien Copies 3.11(i-iii): Induction, Logarithm, and ABC

The first three parts of §3.11 use elementary examples to frame why a structure-preserving intuition may be misleading. Mochizuki compares induction, the logarithm's identification of multiplicative and additive monoids, and the ABC inequality's comparison of additive/ring size with multiplicative radical data.

ExampleSource pointIUT-facing analogy
Mathematical induction Some facts about N naturally require the defining structure of N. A proof may need the exact structural machinery rather than a shorter all-at-once argument.
Logarithm Ordinary logarithms identify multiplicative and additive structures without a ring homomorphism. IUT cannot make the same casual identification for the log-link, because the theta-link would cease to be well-defined.
ABC inequality The radical side is weaker-looking multiplicative data, yet the conjecture says it controls additive/ring height up to an exponent. This resembles the claim that Frobenioids, monoids, and Galois actions control ring/arithmetic holomorphic structures up to indeterminacy.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.11(i)-(iii) Related: log-links ABC/Szpiro output

Alien Copies 3.11(iv): AND/OR and Multiradiality

§3.11(iv) is the most important part of the section for the dispute corridor. Mochizuki states that the theta-link is defined so that two assignments, attached to distinct labeled Hodge-theater copies, are simultaneously valid. The logical relation is AND, not OR.

ReadingConsequence in §3.11(iv)
AND with distinct labels The domain and codomain copies remain separate, so the two theta-link assignments can be valid together without contradiction.
Multiradiality The multiradial algorithm is nontrivial because it preserves this simultaneous meaningfulness through the construction.
OR collapse If one reads the assignments as alternatives, distinct copies look unnecessary and the multiradial step appears contentless.
Corridor warning The source explicitly treats this AND/OR confusion as capable of producing deep misunderstanding near Theorem 3.11 and Corollary 3.12.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.11(iv) Related: 3.11 representation 3.12 estimate Mochizuki response page

Alien Copies 3.11(v): Closed Loops and Two-Dimensionality

§3.11(v) describes the multiradial construction as a closed loop: an input prime-strip returns to a single input prime-strip equipped, up to indeterminacy, with both q-intertwining and theta-intertwining. Mochizuki contrasts this with an open path, which would only compare different object types and would not yield intrinsic conclusions.

ConditionSource function
Closed-loop output Allows one to derive nontrivial consequences about the input data rather than only a relation between unrelated input and output types.
Prime-strip strength The input type is strong enough for the multiradial algorithm.
Prime-strip weakness The input type is weak enough that q-pilot and theta-pilot data yield isomorphic collections, making the theta-link definable.
Two dimensions The unit-group and value-group portions remain independent rather than already tied by a ring-structure intertwining.
Holomorphic hull warning Omitting the holomorphic hull in the §3.7(ii) argument is described as replacing the correct prime-strip type by a type that fails the needed strength/weakness balance.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §3.11(v) Related: processions/tensor packets 3.12 estimate single theta-link rule

Alien Copies 4.1: Connections to Classical Theories

§4.1 argues that IUT's novelty is accompanied by many older sources: Grothendieck universes and Galois categories, Hodge-Tate theory, Faltings' p-adic Hodge theory, Tate-curve theta functions, Hodge-Arakelov theory, log/toric monoids, and Grothendieck connections. The point for the wiki is bibliographic orientation, not compression of IUT into any one ancestor.

Source itemMain contentPage
§4.1(i)IUT's central ideas are presented as compact/conceptual despite the long literature chain; theta/log portions are compared with Gaussian/Jacobi themes.compact/Jacobi
§4.1(ii)Nine explicit links to classical theories.classical list
§4.1(iii)Monoids in IUT differ from F1-style monoid geometry because Galois/Kummer actions are central.monoids/Galois
§4.1(iv)Slim categories and poly-morphisms are used to avoid some 2-categorical/stacks overhead.slim/poly
§4.1(v)The notation is treated as a consequence of distinguishing mutually alien copies and nontrivial cyclotomic identifications.notation/copies
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.1 Related: Gaussian comparison tripodal transport Collas category view

Alien Copies 4.1(i): Compact Ideas and Jacobi Comparison

Mochizuki contrasts IUT with a long numerical computation: the length of the required literature chain is not meant to imply that the central idea is merely a page-count exercise. He summarizes the center as comparison of mutually alien miniature scheme theories by mono-anabelian transport and multiradiality.

IUT portionClassical analogy in §4.1(i)
The theta portion, especially the theta-link. The computational problem, compared with the Gaussians in the Gaussian-integral computation.
The log portion, especially the log-link. The solution side, compared with the angular portion after passing to polar coordinates.
Jacobi's identity A function-theoretic version of the Gaussian-integral computation in upper-half-plane geometry.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.1(i) Related: Gaussian comparison theta-links log-links

Alien Copies 4.1(ii): Nine Classical Connections

§4.1(ii) lists nine classical sources or prototypes. The table below records them as a reading map, not as claims that any individual item alone contains the IUT argument.

ItemClassical source named in §4.1(ii)IUT role claimed there
1Universes, Galois categories, and etale topoi.Prototype for multiple universes in one arithmetic setup.
2Inner automorphism indeterminacies in Galois categories.Ancestor of stronger IUT indeterminacies.
3Tate's Hodge-Tate representation theory.Precursor of log-shell theory via Qp Grothendieck-conjecture work.
4Faltings' almost etale p-adic Hodge theory.Technical background for p-adic anabelian geometry and cuspidalization.
5Tate functions on uniformized Tate curves.Foundation for etale theta theory.
6Mumford algebraic theta functions.Scheme-theoretic Hodge-Arakelov theory as natural extension.
7Frobenius invariance of the etale site and Uchida's function-field anabelian geometry.Prototype for mono-anabelian transport and Frobenius-like/etale-like language.
8Abstract monoids in log schemes and toric geometry.Motivates Frobenioid-style monoid use.
9Grothendieck's definition of a connection and the crystalline site.Combinatorial analogue for multiradiality.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.1(ii) Related: Frobenioids/log-shells mono-anabelian transport multiradiality

Alien Copies 4.1(iii): Monoids and Galois Theory

Mochizuki compares IUT's monoid use with geometry over the field with one element, but immediately marks the difference: in IUT, monoids are coupled to anabelian and Kummer-theoretic aspects of Galois or arithmetic fundamental groups.

SettingMonoid role
IUT Monoids appear with Galois actions, Kummer theory, mono-anabelian transport, and coric invariance.
Conventional F1-style geometry, as characterized here Monoids are not usually tied essentially to anabelian or Kummer-theoretic Galois actions.
Conceptual F1 analogue in IUT Not a specific object, but the coricity/core idea: data invariant under links in a unique way.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.1(iii) Related: Frobenioid use Kummer decoupling Collas GMS categorification

Alien Copies 4.1(iv): Slim Categories and Poly-Morphisms

§4.1(iv) explains two bookkeeping techniques used to avoid some 2-categorical overhead: slim categories and poly-morphisms. The point is not to eliminate indeterminacy, but to track it in a category-level language.

TechniqueFunction
Slim categories / slim anabelioids Allow category-of-categories situations to be handled more like ordinary categories when centralizers are trivial.
Poly-morphisms Subsets of arrows whose compositions track how indeterminacies interact.
Basepoint analogy Inner automorphism indeterminacy is compared with the ambiguity of choosing a basepoint in topology.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.1(iv) Related: poly-morphisms anabelian background

Alien Copies 4.1(v): Notation and Mutually Alien Copies

Mochizuki attributes the notational complexity of the IUT papers to two needs: distinguishing objects in different miniature models of conventional scheme theory, and distinguishing nontrivially related objects within one model that ordinary arithmetic geometry often treats tacitly.

Notation pressureWhy §4.1(v) says it matters
Distinct copy labels Required to keep separate lattice points/Hodge theaters and mutually alien miniature scheme theories.
Distinct cyclotomes and rigidity isomorphisms Needed even within a single model when conventional identifications become nontrivial across multiple models.
Inter-universality The notation is presented as a consequence of the theory's central operation, not decoration.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.1(v) Related: alien-copy terminology inter-universality AND/OR guardrail

Alien Copies 4.2: Class Field Theory and Kummer Theory

§4.2 explains why local class field theory is comparatively peripheral in IUT, global class field theory is absent, and Kummer theory is central. The stated reason is structural: Kummer theory is more functorial, works for broader monoid/scheme inputs, supports evaluation of functions at points, and fits the mono-analytic decouplings used in IUT.

AxisClass field theory sideKummer theory side
Input scopeSpecial arithmetic fields and their localizations.General schemes and abstract multiplicative monoids under weak hypotheses.
FunctorialityLimited to field extensions and localization-type operations.Strong functoriality for broad morphism classes.
Anabelian useRestricted range.Well suited to general arithmetic fundamental-group morphisms.
Extra difficultyCyclotomic rigidity is not central.Cyclotomic rigidity is central and delicate.
Analytic estimate styleDirichlet-density/coherent aggregation side.Prime-number-theorem/log-volume side.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.2 Related: Kummer decoupling mono-anabelian transport classical connections

Alien Copies 4.2(i): Kummer Functoriality and Cyclotomic Rigidity

Mochizuki's first contrast is functoriality. Class field theory has a narrow arithmetic-field domain and limited functoriality; Kummer theory applies to much broader scheme and monoid contexts and is therefore better suited to anabelian reconstruction and transport.

PointMeaning in §4.2(i)
Evaluation problem Evaluating functions at closed points is a basic anabelian/IUT operation where class field theory is not immediately applicable, but Kummer theory is functorial.
Cyclotomic rigidity Kummer theory then requires nontrivial identification of cyclotomes built from rings, monoids, Galois groups, or arithmetic fundamental groups.
Kummer-faithful cases The Kummer map on the multiplicative monoid is injective; this is the IUT-relevant regime for cyclotomic rigidity.
Projective Kummer contrast In highly non-Kummer-faithful settings, such as function fields over algebraic closures of finite fields, the image behaves projectively instead.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.2(i) Related: theta/cyclotomic rigidity rational-function monoids

Alien Copies 4.2(ii): Evaluation and Jugendtraum

§4.2(ii) emphasizes evaluation of special functions at special points. In IUT, this is handled through Kummer theory. Classical class field theory relates to such evaluation only in restricted cases, such as exponential functions for Q or elliptic/modular functions for imaginary quadratic fields.

ThemeSource reading
Special-function evaluation Fundamental for the Kummer theory used in §2.13, §2.14, §3.6, and IUT IV.
Class field limitation Known compatibility with such evaluation is confined to classical special cases.
Jugendtraum formulation Mochizuki describes IUT as a kind of Kummer-theoretic replacement version of Kronecker's Jugendtraum/Hilbert's twelfth problem.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.2(ii) Related: Galois evaluation finite harmonic approximation

Alien Copies 4.2(iii): Holomorphicity and Mono-Analyticity

Mochizuki frames global class field theory as tied to arithmetic holomorphic structure, while Kummer theory is suited to the mono-analytic structures of IUT. The section also contrasts class field theory's global reciprocity and density viewpoint with IUT's local unit/value decouplings and prime-by-prime estimates.

ContrastClass field theoryKummer/IUT
Structural mode Delicate arithmetic properties of restricted field types. General multiplicative monoids and mono-analytic structures.
Unit/value relation Global reciprocity intertwines local-unit and value-group behavior. Unit and value group portions are split/decoupled in the IUT Kummer theory.
Hilbert 90 Compatible with vanishing for the full multiplicative group. Nonvanishing for torsion/unit/split subgroups is part of the decoupled picture.
Prime counting Related to coherent aggregations of primes and Dirichlet/Tchebotarev density. Log-volume estimates use Prime Number Theorem style prime-by-prime counting.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.2(iii) Related: unit/value dimensions log-volume estimates

Alien Copies 4.2(iv): Global Reciprocity and Global Cyclotomic Rigidity

The final §4.2 contrast relates the classical use of cyclotomic extensions in verifying the global reciprocity law to IUT's construction of global cyclotomic rigidity isomorphisms for number-field Kummer theory.

Classical sideIUT-side comparison in §4.2(iv)
Cyclotomic extensions help verify that the reciprocity map vanishes on principal ideles. Cyclotomic rigidity for number-field Kummer theory uses the elementary intersection Q_{>0} ∩ Zhat^x = {1}.
Product-formula behavior is lifted to the etale/profinite Galois level through global reciprocity. A product-formula analogue is lifted through global cyclotomic rigidity.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.2(iv) Related: Kummer functoriality cyclotomic rigidity taxonomy

Alien Copies 4.3: Arithmetic and Geometric Mordell Comparisons

§4.3 compares IUT with Faltings' proof of Mordell and with geometric complex-analytic proofs by Parshin and Bogomolov. The comparison is qualitative: it locates IUT as a hyperbolic, mono-analytic/multiradial analogue of arithmetic-holomorphic abelian techniques, not as a restatement of them.

Source itemContentPage
§4.3(i)Seven rough correspondences between Faltings' proof and IUT.Faltings correspondences
§4.3(ii)Arithmetic-holomorphic nonexistence versus mono-analytic/multiradial existence.holomorphic nonexistence
§4.3(iii)Parshin and Bogomolov metric proofs in the complex geometric case.Parshin/Bogomolov
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.3 Related: Bogomolov tripodal reading class field/Kummer contrast ABC/Szpiro output

Alien Copies 4.3(i): Faltings Correspondences

Mochizuki lists seven rough correspondences between Faltings' proof of Mordell and IUT. The list does not assert that IUT follows from Faltings; it records recurring mathematical roles across two very different frameworks.

ItemFaltings-side componentIUT-side analogue in §4.3(i)
1Geometry of numbers, heights, Hermite-Minkowski.Non-interference for F_mod and global realified Frobenioids/degrees.
2Global class field theory and Dirichlet density.Kummer theory and prime-counting/log-volume estimates.
3Hodge-Tate decompositions of p-adic Tate modules.Background to the p-adic/anabelian tools used in IUT.
4Ramification computations for finite flat group schemes.Ramification computations involving log-shells.
5Isogenies and Tate modules of abelian varieties.Hidden endomorphisms, Belyi/elliptic cuspidalization, and noncritical Belyi maps.
6Polarizations of abelian varieties.Commutators of theta groups and mono-theta rigidity.
7Logarithmic geometry of toroidal compactifications.Logarithmic geometry of stable-curve special fibers and tempered combinatorial anabelian geometry.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.3(i) Related: class field/Kummer contrast theta-group rigidity leading-term computation

Alien Copies 4.3(ii): Holomorphic Nonexistence and Mono-Analytic Existence

§4.3(ii) gives one of Mochizuki's compressed slogans for IUT: conventional arithmetic geometry often proves arithmetic-holomorphic nonexistence, while IUT aims at non-arithmetic-holomorphic existence by leaving the conventional scheme-theoretic framework.

SideSource descriptionExamples named in §4.3(ii)
Arithmetic-holomorphic nonexistence Results say that arithmetic tangent-bundle style global sections coincide with a tiny collection of scheme-theoretic auto/endomorphisms. Faltings' Tate-conjecture input, bi-anabelian full faithfulness, nonexistence of conventional Frobenius endomorphisms of number fields, Wiles-style deformation nonexistence.
Mono-analytic/multiradial existence IUT seeks a Frobenius-endomorphism-like operation outside conventional scheme theory, through deformations of arithmetic holomorphic structure. The source writes this suggestively as an H^1-style arithmetic tangent-bundle phenomenon.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.3(ii) Related: mono-anabelian transport multiradiality Frobenius/NF obstruction

Alien Copies 4.3(iii): Parshin and Bogomolov Metric Proofs

§4.3(iii) compares two complex geometric proofs: Parshin's geometric Mordell proof and Bogomolov's geometric Szpiro proof. Mochizuki uses their contrast to mirror the difference between Faltings-style arithmetic holomorphicity and IUT-style mono-analytic/multiradiality.

PointParshin sideBogomolov side / IUT resonance
Metric geometry Holomorphic geometry via Kobayashi distance and the Schwarz lemma. Real-analytic hyperbolic geometry of the upper half-plane.
Estimate type Rough qualitative estimates, fitting the Mordell statement. Effective quantitative estimates, fitting the Szpiro statement.
Belyi/continuation link Schwarz-lemma style analytic continuation. Noncritical Belyi maps in IUT are presented as arithmetic analytic continuation.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.3(iii) Related: Bogomolov tripodal reading IUT leading term ABC/Szpiro output

Alien Copies 4.4: Atavistic Development

§4.4 is Mochizuki's historical interpretation of IUT. He argues against strictly linear narratives of mathematical progress and presents IUT as an atavistic branch: a development drawing from older anabelian, theta, Frobenioid, Kummer, and multiradial ideas rather than from a single modern mainstream line.

Source itemContentPage
§4.4(i)Critique of strictly linear progress models; contrast among mono-anabelian, cohomological/representation-theoretic, and bi-anabelian approaches.linear models
§4.4(ii)Fourteen examples of older techniques that, in Mochizuki's view, could have emerged earlier.examples
§4.4(iii)Strategic warning against evaluating mathematics by narrow deterministic progress models.deterministic models
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.4 Related: classical connections Mordell comparison Collas category view

Alien Copies 4.4(i): Linear Models and IUT's Branch

Mochizuki contrasts IUT with two linear narratives: progress toward motives or Weil cohomology, and progress through Langlands-style representation theory. He also contrasts IUT's mono-anabelian approach with the bi-anabelian section-conjecture approach.

Approach§4.4(i) characterization
IUT / mono-anabelian Non-holomorphic existence; group theory is treated as primary relative to field/representation structures.
Motivic/cohomological and Langlands-style approaches Characterized as abelian and closer to holomorphic nonexistence phenomena.
Bi-anabelian section-conjecture direction Also distinguished from IUT's mono-anabelian route.
Common feature with Wiles-style work Both use Galois groups and upper-half-plane function theory, but IUT uses theta functions where Wiles-style work uses modular forms.
Source: Mutually Alien Copies §4.4(i) Related: holomorphic nonexistence mono-anabelian transport

Alien Copies 4.4(ii): Atavistic Examples

§4.4(ii) lists examples meant to support the atavistic-development view. The table groups the fourteen source examples so the page remains usable as a reading index.

GroupExamples in §4.4(ii)
Belyi and height tools Belyi maps, Belyi cuspidalization, and noncritical Belyi maps for height estimates.
Gaussian/theta interpretations Galois-theoretic interpretation of the Gaussian integral/Jacobi identity; theta functions on Tate curves; theta-group structure.
