Theta monoids Psi_env, Psi_cns, and Frobenioid-theoretic theta monoids. Connect mono-theta environments, Kummer classes, and Frobenioid theta functions. Must remember whether the construction is group-theoretic, Frobenioid-theoretic, or already post-anabelian.
Gaussian monoids Value-profile-indexed monoids Psi_xi and Psi_Fxi. Package theta values by labeled value-profiles and their splittings. Labels and j^2-weighting are not optional metadata.
Frobenioid interpretation F_cns, F_env, F_gau, and related split Frobenioids. Definition 3.8 turns monoids with Galois action into local Frobenioids of monoid type Z. Separates divisor-monoid structure, splittings, and the underlying topological group action.
Post-anabelian forgetting Abstract Gaussian monoids stripped of the construction algorithm. Remark 3.6.2 describes the final "forget" operation after Kummer/Galois evaluation. Useful only after the construction path has been recorded; otherwise it erases source obligations.
IUT II §3 source item Local monoid construction Why it is proof-critical
Proposition 3.1(i) A projective system of mono-theta environments produces split theta monoids Psi_env and infty Psi_env, generated from the constant part and theta subsets inside cohomology limits. These monoids retain the theta data together with splittings up to torsion, so they are the multiradial-friendly input for Gaussian construction.
Proposition 3.1(ii) The same environment also produces a constant monoid Psi_cns, naturally isomorphic to the local base-field monoid and equipped with a local Galois action. Constant monoids are necessary for value-profiles, but their construction uses the base-field cyclotome route that later causes uniradiality.
Example 3.2, Proposition 3.3 When the mono-theta environment comes from a tempered Frobenioid, Kummer classes identify Frobenioid-theoretic theta monoids and constant monoids with the mono-theta-theoretic versions. This ties Hodge-Arakelov theta evaluation back to the Frobenioid objects that live inside Hodge theaters.
Proposition 3.4 Split theta monoids are compatible with the O×mu-version of the theta link, hence multiradial; constant monoids, as constructed, are only uniradial. This is an explicit source-level warning: not every useful local monoid is automatically stable under the multiradial comparison demanded later.
Corollary 3.5(i) The F_l^± symmetry gives symmetrizing isomorphisms between labeled copies of local Galois/constant-monoid data without crushing the label set. Gaussian monoids require distinct values at distinct labels while still using one synchronized basepoint/cyclotome.
Corollary 3.5(ii)-(iii) A value-profile is a label-indexed tuple of theta-value elements. Gaussian monoids are generated by the diagonal unit part and powers or roots of the value-profile; zero-label restriction gives splittings up to torsion. This is the local source of the Gaussian distribution that later becomes a log-volume object.
Remark 3.5.2 Symmetrizing isomorphisms must preserve a bijective relationship with the label set and the single basepoint. Naively quotienting labels to one point fails this requirement. This reinforces the wiki rule that label erasure and label synchronization are different operations.
IUT II's Figure 3.1 can be read as a four-step diagnostic: Frobenioid-theoretic theta monoids pass through Kummer theory to group-theoretic theta monoids, Galois evaluation produces group-theoretic Gaussian monoids, and a final forgetful step yields post-anabelian Gaussian monoids. The wiki keeps these stages explicit because later Lean interfaces must not silently replace one stage by another.
IUT II §4 source item Object or link Formal meaning for the wiki
Definition 4.9(i) ×- and ×μ-Kummer structures on a Frobenioid, expressed as orbits of isomorphisms O×(G) -> O×(A) and O×μ(G) -> O×μ(A). This fixes the unit-quotient interface used later by theta links. ×-Kummer structures are unique; a ×μ-Kummer structure is equivalently the system of invariant submodules I_H^κ(A).
Definition 4.9(ii)-(vi) From an F⊢-prime-strip, construct local F⊢×, F⊢×μ, and F⊢ ×μ data at bad, good nonarchimedean, and archimedean places. The construction is place-sensitive: bad places use the 2l-torsion quotient generated by splittings, good nonarchimedean places use the splitting image, and archimedean places use an inductive system of torsion quotients.
Definition 4.9(vii)-(viii) F⊢×μ-prime-strips and F×μ-prime-strips, including the pilot object of negative arithmetic degree determined by the bad-place generators. This is the precise object class on which the modified theta links act. It is stronger than a bare coric category but weaker than preservation of the full original Frobenioid.
Corollary 4.10(i) Constant prime-strips F_△, naturally identified with Fmod. The target side of the link is the constant/model q-parameter prime-strip, not a new unrelated global object.
Corollary 4.10(ii) Theta and Gaussian prime-strips F_env and F_gau, naturally identified with Ftht and related by evaluation. The Gaussian side is produced from the theta side by the evaluation isomorphism before the F×μ upgrade.
Corollary 4.10(iii) Full poly-isomorphisms F×μ_env -> F×μ_△ and F×μ_gau -> F×μ_△. These are the Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau links. Their definition depends on functorial passage from F-prime-strip isomorphisms to F×μ-prime-strip isomorphisms.
Corollary 4.10(iv)-(v) Coric invariants F⊢×μ_△ and D⊢_△, plus associated global realified Frobenioids modulo R>0-orbits. The preserved part is the unit/coric interface. It is exactly the portion later used when log-wall and volume comparisons require common mono-analytic data.
Corollary 4.10(vi), Remark 4.10.3 Infinite oriented chains of linked theaters and non-preserved divisor/value-group data. Translation by Z is allowed, arbitrary permutation of theaters is not. The value-group distortion is the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation whose log-volume effect is computed later.
IUT II Corollary 4.11 source item Etale-picture construction Why it matters later
Corollary 4.11(i) The chain of D-Theta±ellNF-linked D-Hodge theaters induces a chain of full poly-isomorphisms ... -> nD⊢_△ -> (n+1)D⊢_△ -> .... Thus (-)D⊢_△ is a constant invariant. This supplies the mono-analytic core shared by the otherwise distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures.
Corollary 4.11(ii) Each D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater is a spoke emanating from the common mono-analytic core. The spokes admit arbitrary permutation symmetries. This contrasts with the oriented Frobenius-picture chain, which has translation symmetry but not arbitrary adjacent swaps.
Corollary 4.11(iii) The construction is compatible with the earlier IUT I etale-pictures after identifying D⊢_△ with the appropriate underlying D⊢_> data. The IUT II enhanced picture is not a separate formal universe; it refines the IUT I base-theater picture.
Remark 4.11.1 The Frobenius-picture is order-conscious iteration of Gaussian theta-exponent transformations, while the etale-picture treats the corresponding exponents with permutation-symmetric commutativity. This is the local source of the later distinction between ordered theta-link motion and common mono-analytic comparison data.
Remark 4.11.2 Mochizuki reviews the role of Gaussian distributions, conjugate synchronization, post-anabelian and anabelian Gaussian representations, and unit/value-group splittings. The review explains why the link construction needs both the post-anabelian Gaussian side and the anabelian/log-wall side developed in IUT III.
Frobenius-picture versus etale-picture IUT II Corollary 4.10 to Corollary 4.11

Theta monoids before Gaussian monoids

IUT II Proposition 3.1 constructs theta monoids and constant monoids from projective systems of mono-theta environments. Propositions 3.3 and 3.4 compare the mono-theta, Frobenioid-theoretic, and group-theoretic versions using Kummer classes and rigidity isomorphisms.

This is the local algebraic input for the later Gaussian construction: theta data is first made into monoids that can be acted on, compared, and stripped to unit/value-group components.

IUT II, Proposition 3.1Proposition 3.4

Constant monoids

The constant monoid Psi_cns is reconstructed from the base-field part by using the natural valuation surjection. It is essential for Gaussian value-profiles but is the first local monoid in this chain whose source construction is only uniradial as stated.

IUT III's log-shell passage is therefore not decorative: it is needed to repair this route into a multiradial form with controlled indeterminacy.

Proposition 3.1(ii)uniradial

Symmetrizing isomorphisms

Corollary 3.5 uses the F_l^± symmetry to compare labeled local Galois/constant-monoid data without identifying all labels with one coarse quotient point.

Remark 3.5.2 isolates the rule: keep the bijective relationship with the label set while using one synchronized basepoint.

Corollary 3.5(i)labels

Gaussian monoids and value-profiles

Corollary 3.5 defines value-profiles and Gaussian monoids from symmetrized labeled constant monoids. The construction depends on F_l^±-symmetrizing isomorphisms and on preserving a bijective relationship with the label set; Remark 3.5.2 contrasts this with crushing labels to a single quotient point.

Corollary 3.6 gives the corresponding Frobenioid-theoretic Gaussian monoids when the mono-theta environment comes from a tempered Frobenioid.

Corollary 3.5Corollary 3.6value-profile

Globalization over all places

IUT II §4 extends the Gaussian construction from bad places to good nonarchimedean and archimedean places. Corollary 4.5 then attaches constant monoids, semi-simplifications, local theta/Gaussian monoids, and global realified theta/Gaussian Frobenioids to a D-Theta±ell-Hodge theater.

The global Frobenioids D_env and D_gau carry realified divisor data, so this layer is already preparing the objects whose log-volumes are estimated later.

IUT II §4Corollary 4.5

Kummer Frobenioid structures

Definition 4.9 starts from a Frobenioid whose base category is the category of connected finite etale covers of a local or archimedean field. A universal covering pro-object A has automorphism group G; Kummer structures compare group-theoretically reconstructed unit quotients with the units evaluated at A.

The ×-structure is unique. The ×μ-structure keeps torsion quotients and is recorded by the family I_H^κ(A) as H ranges over open subgroups of G.

Definition 4.9(i)Kummer

F×μ-prime-strips

Definition 4.9 upgrades an F⊢-prime-strip to the F⊢×μ and F×μ interfaces by keeping unit quotients and controlled splitting data while weakening the original Frobenioid.

At bad places the monoid generated by 2l-torsion and splitting images supplies the quotient used in the unit/value monoid O×μ(A). The resulting F×μ-prime-strip also carries a pilot object of negative arithmetic degree.

Definition 4.9(ii)-(viii)link domain

Constant prime-strips

Corollary 4.10(i) turns the constant monoid data Psi_cns(F≻)_t into F-prime-strips and then identifies the zero-label and diagonal-label versions. The resulting F_△ is naturally identified with Fmod.

This is the q-parameter or model side of the modified theta links.

Corollary 4.10(i)Fmod

Theta and Gaussian prime-strips

Corollary 4.10(ii) constructs F_env from theta-environment monoids and F_gau from Gaussian monoids, together with global realified Frobenioids and local comparison isomorphisms.

The evaluation isomorphism F_env -> F_gau is the source of the Gaussian version of the link, not an extra symmetry imposed after the fact.

Corollary 4.10(ii)evaluation

Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau links

Corollary 4.10 constructs F-prime-strips from constant, theta, and Gaussian data, passes them to F⊢×μ-prime-strips, and defines the Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau links between Theta±ellNF theaters.

The coric output is the invariant F⊢×μ_△ and the associated D⊢_△ prime-strip. The divisor or value-group part is not preserved by these links; Remark 4.10.3 identifies that distortion as the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation whose volume effect is later computed.

Corollary 4.10Remark 4.10.3

Value-group distortion

Remark 4.10.3 separates preserved unit/coric data from the divisor or value-group part, which is explicitly not preserved by the Theta×μ and Theta×μ_gau links.

Mochizuki describes this non-preserved part as the arithmetic Teichmuller deformation. Restricting the Gaussian value-group correspondence to the label j = 1 recovers the pivotal distribution, but restriction to proper label subsets generally breaks the relevant F_l^{±} and F_l symmetries.

Remark 4.10.3not preserved

D-theater mono-analytic core

Corollary 4.11(i) states that the D-prime-strip D⊢_△ is constant across the D-Theta±ellNF linked chain. This is the shared mono-analytic core of the etale-picture.

The point is not that all theaters have one holomorphic history, but that their D-theater shadows meet in a common mono-analytic invariant.

Corollary 4.11(i)mono-analytic

Etale-picture

Corollary 4.11(ii) draws D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters as spokes around the common D⊢_△ core. Unlike the oriented Frobenius-picture, this spoke picture admits arbitrary permutation symmetries among the labels.

This distinction is a recurring audit rule: ordered theta motion and common mono-analytic comparison data are different structures.

Corollary 4.11(ii)spokes

Arithmetic holomorphic spokes

Each spoke in the etale-picture is a distinct arithmetic holomorphic structure on the same mono-analytic core. Corollary 4.11 makes this explicit for the D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters obtained from the Theta×μ and Theta×μ-gau chains.

The same construction remains compatible with the earlier IUT I base etale-pictures after passing to the relevant underlying D-theaters.

Corollary 4.11(ii)-(iii)holomorphic structure

Theta-exponent commutativity

Remark 4.11.1 explains the contrast in terms of Gaussian theta exponents: the Frobenius-picture remembers an order of repeated q -> q^{j^2}-type transformations, while the etale-picture packages the associated exponents with permutation symmetry.

Mochizuki compares this to the cartesian versus polar-coordinate representations of the Gaussian integral.

Remark 4.11.1Gaussian analogy
Link to Corollary 3.12: the Gaussian layer explains where the square-weighted theta-value distribution enters the later log-volume inequality. It also clarifies why the wiki keeps unit/coric information distinct from divisor or value-group information.
Sources: IUT II, §§3-4 Related: multiplicative/additive bridges theta/log links log-theta lattice Corollary 3.12 corridor

Frobenioids and Log-Shells

Frobenioids and log-shells are two complementary interfaces in IUT. Frobenioids package divisor, line-bundle, and monoid data categorically; log-shells provide the mono-analytic logarithmic receptacles in which the later multiradial algorithms are expressed.

Multiplicative package to logarithmic receptacle bad-prime Frobenioids, Gaussian monoids, and log-shell versions
IUT I §5 source item Construction Role in the later theory
Remark 5.2.1 F-prime-strips functorially determine D-prime-strips; F⊢-prime-strips determine D⊢-prime-strips. An F-prime-strip also has a mono-analyticization to an F⊢-prime-strip. This justifies treating Frobenioid data as lying over the D-strip layer rather than as an unrelated package.
Remark 5.2.2 F-prime-strips are Kummer-ready, while F⊢-prime-strips are Kummer-blind. The wiki keeps Kummer-ready and mono-analytic data separate because later Kummer transport cannot be recovered from the weakened interface alone.
Definition 5.2(v)-(vi) At nonarchimedean places, the D-side fundamental group reconstructs rational-function pseudo-monoids M^∞κ and M^∞κ×. Kummer classes determine cyclotomic identifications and the uniquely determined coric structures attached to an F-prime-strip. This is one source of the later distinction between Kummer-ready Frobenioid data and data visible only after mono-analytic weakening.
Definition 5.2(vii)-(viii) At archimedean places, Aut-holomorphic reconstruction supplies meromorphic-function pseudo-monoids and Kummer-structure cyclotomic isomorphisms; the ∞κ structure is recovered from ∞κ× by testing torsion at strictly critical points. The archimedean side has its own reconstruction mechanism. Treating all places as if they were nonarchimedean would lose a source distinction in the paper.
Corollary 5.3 Isomorphisms of F-prime-strips are determined by the associated D-prime-strip isomorphisms; for F⊢-prime-strips the corresponding map to D⊢-isomorphisms is surjective. This is a rigidity statement: once the base side is fixed, much of the Frobenioid-side isomorphism data is forced.
Example 5.4(iii)-(iv) A cusp-label class delta in LabCusp(D) determines δ-valuations in V(D); localizing at them constructs an F-prime-strip F|delta, well-defined up to isomorphism. This is where global cusp-label synchronization becomes a Frobenioid localization mechanism.
Example 5.4(v)-(vii) There is a unique NF-side poly-morphism psi^NF : F_J -> F lying over phi^NF. Restriction functors attached to Fmod are immune to the relevant F_l-conjugacy choices; the case j = 1 gives the pivotal distribution. Pivotal distributions are the number-field/Frobenioid precursor to later theta-value and q-parameter comparisons.

Categorical package

IUT I describes a Frobenioid as a categorical abstraction of line bundles or divisor monoids over a base category of topological localizations. This is the source of the F-prime-strip interfaces, where the concrete bad-prime models include O_Fv^▹, O_Fv^× × q_v^N, and their quotient variants.

Bad-prime theta package

In IUT I, Example 3.2, a bad nonarchimedean place produces a tempered Frobenioid F_v, its birationalization, a Frobenioid-theoretic reciprocal theta value Theta_v, and split Frobenioids F_v^| and F_v^Theta. The constructions are asserted to be reconstructible from categorical data.

Delta-valuations

In IUT I, Example 5.4, a class delta in LabCusp(D) selects valuations of D by transporting delta to the distinguished cusp class and then reading off the corresponding image of the D_j component labeled 1.

This definition can also be read intrinsically from the D-NF bridge. The point is that the label-class synchronization of Proposition 4.7 becomes a localization rule for Frobenioid data.

Example 5.4(iii)localization

F-prime-strip F|delta

Localizing at the δ-valuations constructs an F-prime-strip F|delta. At nonarchimedean places this uses p-adic Frobenioids and suitable open subgroups; at bad places the tempered analogue is required; at archimedean places the construction uses Aut-holomorphic reconstruction.

Because the projection from the relevant valuation set to Vmod is not injective, the result is only well-defined up to isomorphism.

Example 5.4(iv)F-prime-strip

Kappa-coric structures

Definition 5.2 attaches ∞κ and ∞κ× pseudo-monoid structures to F-prime-strips. Nonarchimedean components use group-theoretic reconstruction and Kummer cohomology; archimedean components use Aut-holomorphic meromorphic functions.

The ∞κ structure is extracted from ∞κ× by imposing torsion/root-of-unity behavior at strictly critical points.

Definition 5.2(vi),(viii)Kummer

Pivotal distributions

Example 5.4 defines natural restriction functors and realifications for the Frobenioid data over the capsule J. The objects obtained in the label j = 1 case are called pivotal distributions.

These distributions are immune to the relevant F_l-conjugacy indeterminacy because the Fmod object is defined through terminal objects of the global D-side category.

Example 5.4(vii)pivotal

Gaussian Frobenioids

IUT II turns Hodge-Arakelov-theoretic theta evaluations into Gaussian monoids and then interprets those monoids as local Frobenioids. This is where the Frobenioid-theoretic and mono-theta-theoretic versions are compared before the theta-link machinery is assembled.

Log-shell versions

For a topological group G isomorphic to a local Galois group, IUT II recalls the log-shell I(G) subset O×μ(G). Replacing raw O×μ(G) data by the pair (O×μ(G), I(G)) yields log-shell versions of multiradial environments.

LayerSource roleWhy it matters later
D and D⊢ bases Categories of coverings or Galois-category data attached to local curves. Separate holomorphic local structure from mono-analytic structure.
Frobenioids Monoid/divisor/line-bundle package over the base category. Carry theta and q-parameter information in a categorical form.
Gaussian monoids Monoids obtained from theta evaluations at torsion labels. Supply the weighted multiplicative input for later log-volume estimates.
Log-shells Subobjects inside local unit-quotient data, controlled by local logarithms. Make the relevant algorithms multiradial, at the cost of mild indeterminacy.
The formalization burden is not merely to name these objects. A useful Lean model must represent which information is intrinsic to the category, which information is transported through Frobenioid isomorphisms, and which information is visible only after passage to the mono-analytic log-shell layer.
Sources: IUT I IUT II IUT III Related: log-theta lattice Gaussian Frobenioids multiradiality

Theta Links and Log Links

The two principal directions of the log-theta lattice are different in kind. Theta links come from Hodge-Arakelov-theoretic evaluation of theta values; log links come from applying local logarithms and passing through log-shells.

Two link directions horizontal theta motion versus vertical logarithmic motion
LinkSourceFunction in the theoryRisk if misread
Theta link IUT I Full poly-isomorphism from the theta-side F-prime-strip to the model q-parameter F-prime-strip. Reading it as an ordinary ring/scheme morphism remembers too much.
Theta×μ link IUT II Modified theta link that preserves the weakened F⊢×μ prime-strip portion. Treating it as preservation of the whole Frobenioid hides the value-group loss.
Theta×μ_gau link IUT II Gaussian version obtained after replacing theta monoids by Gaussian monoids. Forgetting the Gaussian replacement loses the source of later j^2-weighted data.
Theta×μ_LGP / lgp links IUT III LGP/log-shell versions used in the log-theta lattice and multiradial representation. Collapsing them into the earlier theta link erases the log-shell stage.
Log link IUT III Applies local p-adic logarithmic structures and induces log-shell comparisons along a vertical line. Forgetting calibration and upper-semi behavior can destroy the intended comparison meaning.

Theta and Gaussian prime-strips

IUT II Corollary 4.10 constructs environment and Gaussian F-prime-strips from theta/Gaussian monoids, then compares their associated F⊢×μ-prime-strips by full poly-isomorphisms.

Coric unit portion

The F⊢×μ and associated D⊢ material is coric across the theta and Gaussian links. This is the source for later claims that selected weakened data can be treated independently of a particular holomorphic history.

Vertical obligation

IUT III Remark 1.4.1 says that executing a horizontal theta arrow obligates subsequent vertical log motion if one wants the log-shell tools used in the multiradial representation.

Noncommutativity

Horizontal links are incompatible with local ring structures in a different way from vertical log links. The lattice square is therefore not a commuting square of ordinary schemes or rings.

Translation symmetry

The Frobenius-picture chains admit integer translation symmetry, but not arbitrary permutation symmetry. Adjacent alien copies cannot simply be swapped while leaving the rest fixed.

Formalization pressure

A Lean interface must say whether a map is a theta link, Gaussian theta link, log link, or induced coric prime-strip comparison. Reusing one generic arrow type would hide essential source distinctions.

A useful mnemonic is that theta-type links are horizontal filters between mutually alien Hodge theaters, while log-links are vertical logarithmic operations inside a fixed arithmetic holomorphic structure. The log-theta lattice is built precisely because both operations are needed and they do not commute as ordinary maps.
Sources: IUT II IUT III Related: log-theta lattice Frobenioids/log-shells multiradiality

Log-Theta Lattice

The log-theta lattice is the two-dimensional noncommutative diagram of Hodge theaters studied in IUT III. Horizontal motion is theta-link motion; vertical motion is log-link motion. The noncommutativity is not incidental: it is a source of the comparison constraints, coricity distinctions, and indeterminacies.

Log-theta lattice horizontal theta links, vertical log links
IUT III introduction source item Construction Constraint recorded in the wiki
Abstract and Introduction The log-theta lattice is a noncommutative two-dimensional diagram of Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters. Horizontal arrows are theta-type links; vertical arrows are log-links. The lattice is not a commuting square of schemes. Horizontal and vertical arrows use incompatible pieces of local ring structure.
Introduction, local log-link paragraph At a nonarchimedean valuation, the log-link is modeled by applying log_v to local units: O×μ_Fv ⊗ Q -> F_v. The local group Pi_v is coric for the log-link, while the ring structure used to define the logarithm remains local to one vertical line.
Introduction, log-shell paragraph For bad nonarchimedean places, the log-shell is described as I_v = p_v^{-1} log_v(O×_Kv), a compact additive receptacle containing the Kummer images from both sides of the log-link. The log-shell gives an upper container rather than a precise equality. This is why later volume comparisons produce upper bounds.
Introduction, upper-semi discussion Iterates of the log-link and Kummer isomorphisms form diagrams that are only upper semi-compatible, not genuinely commutative. This is the source-level motivation for treating Ind3 separately in Theorem 3.11 and Corollary 3.12.
Introduction, coricity vocabulary Vertical coricity means invariance under log-links; horizontal coricity means invariance under theta-type links; bi-coricity means both. Later multiradial algorithms must name which direction of invariance they use. A single undifferentiated "invariant" flag would be misleading.
Introduction, Theorem 1.5 preview Compatibility of the log-link with the F_l^{±} symmetry produces vertically coric F⊢×μ prime-strips and bi-coric mono-analytic log-shells. These log-shells become the containers used in the multiradial LGP-monoid and tensor-packet construction of IUT III §3.

Horizontal direction

Horizontal arrows are theta-type links: Theta, Theta×μ, Gaussian Theta×μ_gau, and later LGP/lgp variants. They are not ring or scheme morphisms, and they are incompatible with the local ring structures that define logarithms.

Vertical direction

Vertical arrows are log-links. They apply local logarithmic structures inside a fixed arithmetic holomorphic structure, i.e. inside a single vertical line. This is the direction in which log-shells become available.

Coricity vocabulary

IUT III distinguishes vertical coricity, horizontal coricity, and bi-coricity. A structure is vertically coric if it is invariant along log-links, horizontally coric if invariant along theta-type links, and bi-coric if it survives both directions of the lattice.

Forced vertical step

To use log-shells after a horizontal theta-type link, the theory must move to a node that is a codomain of a vertical log-link. IUT III phrases this as every horizontal execution obligating a subsequent vertical execution.

Local log-link

The local log-link applies the p_v-adic logarithm to the unit quotient side of a local F-prime-strip. It uses the additive and multiplicative structures of the same local ring, so it is defined inside one arithmetic holomorphic structure.

This is why the vertical direction of the lattice is fundamentally different from a theta-type horizontal link.

IUT III Introductionlog-link

Log-shell containment

The bad-place log-shell I_v is designed to contain the images of the Kummer isomorphisms associated to both the domain and codomain of the log-link.

The containment is intentionally one-sided. It prepares an upper-bound mechanism for log-volumes rather than a statement that the two Kummer images are equal.

Remark 1.2.2upper container

Log-link/Kummer incompatibility

IUT III stresses that Kummer isomorphisms are not compatible with the coricity of the group-theoretically reconstructed data at the element level. The relevant compatibility is instead the upper-semi form obtained after passing to log-shells.

This prevents replacing Ind3 by an ordinary quotient-invariance assertion.

Proposition 1.2(iv)Ind3

Bi-coric log-shells

Theorem 1.5 is previewed in the introduction as producing vertically coric F⊢×μ-prime-strips and mono-analytic log-shells that are bi-coric under the lattice arrows.

These are the log-shell objects that later support splitting monoids of LGP-monoids and the tensor-packet comparison.

Theorem 1.5 previewbi-coricity
IUT III §1 source item Precise interface Audit consequence
Definition 1.1(i) For v in Vnon, the p-adic logarithm on the perfected unit monoid defines log(F_v), the tautological log-link F_v -> log(F_v), and the log-shell obtained by scaling the invariant pre-log-shell by (p_v^*)^{-1}. The nonarchimedean log-link is built from local units, group action, and the p-adic logarithm; it is not a map of the original full monoid.
Definition 1.1(ii) For v in Varc, a pointed universal cover of the groupification, quotients by roots of unity, and the complex logarithm construct log(F_v) and an archimedean log-shell. The archimedean case is Kummer/Aut-holomorphic and line-segment based; it should not be copied from the nonarchimedean formula.
Definition 1.1(iii) The local constructions assemble into a tautological log-link F -> log(F). A general or full log-link is obtained by post-composing with a poly-isomorphism log(F) -> F'. The full log-link deliberately replaces the tautological rigidifying path by poly-isomorphism data suitable for etale-like multiradial use.
Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi) The modules I_F⊢×μ ⊆ log(F⊢×μ) are constructed solely from the F⊢×μ-prime-strip, using only the unit quotient data. This is the source for treating log-shells as available after the F⊢×μ weakening used by theta-type links.
Remark 1.1.1 Iterates of log-links are understood only on the local unit groups in their domains. Non-unit/value-group parts are not needed to construct log-shells. The wiki keeps value-group distortion separate from log-shell construction: log-links render unit data additive.
Remark 1.1.2 The tautological log-link identifies the domain and codomain Pi_v's by a rigidifying path built from Frobenius-like monoids. The full log-link uses a tautologically indeterminate isomorphism. Multiradial algorithms need the full-log-link formulation because it can be expressed in etale-like terms without depending on the monoid path.
Proposition 1.2(i)-(iv) A log-link induces poly-isomorphisms of associated D- and D⊢-prime-strips, is compatible with ring structures and log-volumes, but the Kummer isomorphisms fail to be compatible with the D-coric comparison at element level. This is the exact reason that later log-Kummer compatibility must be upper-semi rather than equality-based.
Proposition 1.2(v)-(ix) Holomorphic, mono-analytic, and coric holomorphic log-shells are constructed with compatible log-volumes and poly-isomorphisms between D⊢, F⊢×μ, and F versions. The paper supplies several log-shell representations; they must not be collapsed into one undifferentiated shell object.
Proposition 1.2(x) Full log-links generate an oriented Frobenius-picture chain of F-prime-strips whose D and D⊢ cores are identified up to full poly-isomorphism. This vertical Frobenius-picture has translation symmetry fixing the core, but no arbitrary adjacent-vertex permutation symmetry.

Tautological and full log-links

Definition 1.1 first constructs a tautological log-link F -> log(F). A full log-link post-composes this with the full poly-isomorphism from log(F) to a target F-prime-strip.

Remark 1.1.2 explains the reason: the tautological link carries a specific monoid-built rigidifying path, while the full link is expressed in etale-like indeterminate terms needed later.

Definition 1.1(iii)Remark 1.1.2

Unit-only log-link domain

Remark 1.1.1 says that iterated log-links are to be understood on local units in their domains. The purpose of the log-link is to construct log-shells from units; non-unit value-group portions are not required for this construction.

This is the local mechanism behind the later split between additive log-shell data and multiplicative value-group distortion.

Remark 1.1.1units

F⊢×μ log-shells

Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi) reconstructs log-shell modules from F⊢×μ-prime-strip data alone. The construction uses the unit quotient/perfection interface and is unaffected by quotienting units by torsion.

This gives the log-shell side access to the weakened interface already used by the modified theta links of IUT II.

Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi)F⊢×μ

Mono-analytic log-shells

Proposition 1.2(vi)-(viii) constructs mono-analytic log-shells from D⊢-prime-strip data and relates them by compatible poly-isomorphisms to F⊢×μ and holomorphic F-prime-strip log-shells.

These are the shell objects that can be read without carrying a specific arithmetic holomorphic history.

Proposition 1.2(vi)-(viii)D⊢

Coric holomorphic log-shells

Proposition 1.2(ix) starts from a D-prime-strip, forms the corresponding F-prime-strip F(D), and uses the full log-link to construct coric holomorphic log-shells in the constant monoid data.

This is a separate representation from the mono-analytic shell, connected to it by the natural isomorphisms supplied in the proposition.

Proposition 1.2(ix)coric

Log-link Frobenius-picture

Proposition 1.2(x) organizes full log-links into an oriented integer-indexed chain. The associated D and D⊢ prime-strips form a fixed core under translation by Z.

As in the theta-link Frobenius-picture, arbitrary adjacent swaps are not allowed.

Proposition 1.2(x)Frobenius-picture
IUT III §1 source item Hodge-theater/lattice construction Reason it is proof-critical
Proposition 1.3(i) An isomorphism of associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters determines log-links on each constituent F-prime-strip of the theta and plus-minus bridges; the full case uses the full poly-isomorphism. The theater-level log-link is built over D-theater data. It is not chosen independently for each F-prime-strip without synchronization.
Proposition 1.3(ii)-(iv) A theater log-link lies over a D-theater poly-isomorphism, inherits the Proposition 1.2 compatibility/log-shell/Kummer facts, and generates a vertical Frobenius-picture chain. This lifts the log-link interface from prime-strips to complete Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters.
Remarks 1.3.1-1.3.2 The construction first fixes a single D-theater isomorphism to preserve plus-minus synchronization, and the resulting log-links are compatible with F_l^{±}-symmetrizing isomorphisms. Conjugate synchronization is already a constraint at the log-link stage, before Theorem 3.11.
Definition 1.4 The log-theta lattice is the Z × Z-indexed diagram with full log-links vertically and either Theta×μ or Theta×μ_gau links horizontally. The Gaussian lattice is the §1 object used for Theorem 1.5 and the later multiradial construction.
Remark 1.4.1 Lattice squares are far from 1-commutative. Each horizontal execution obligates a subsequent vertical execution if one wants log-shells. Multiradiality must be built from vertical cores, not by pretending horizontal theta motion preserves local ring histories.
Theorem 1.5(i)-(ii) Vertical arrows induce full poly-isomorphisms between D-Theta±ellNF theaters; horizontal arrows induce full poly-isomorphisms between the associated F⊢×μ_△-prime-strips. The theorem separates vertical coricity from horizontal coricity before composing them into bi-coric objects.
Theorem 1.5(iii)-(iv) F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△) and the corresponding mono-analytic log-shells are bi-coric across arbitrary lattice indices (n,m) and (n',m'). These are the §1 bi-cores used downstream for the multiradial LGP-monoid representation.
Theorem 1.5(v), Remark 1.5.1 The D-prime-strip poly-isomorphisms induce compatible bi-coric global realified Frobenioids, and conjugate synchronization is essential for the subscript-△ constructions. The realified Frobenioid layer is the bridge to later log-volume computation, not just a formal add-on.

Hodge-theater log-link

Proposition 1.3 constructs a log-link between Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters from an isomorphism of their associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters. Each constituent F-prime-strip then receives the corresponding log-link.

The construction is synchronized at the D-theater level; it is not a collection of unrelated local log choices.

Proposition 1.3(i)D-theater

Gaussian log-theta lattice

Definition 1.4 defines two log-theta lattices: non-Gaussian with Theta×μ horizontal arrows and Gaussian with Theta×μ_gau horizontal arrows. The vertical arrows in both are full log-links.

Theorem 1.5 is stated for the Gaussian lattice.

Definition 1.4Gaussian

Vertical and horizontal coricity

Theorem 1.5(i) supplies vertical coricity of associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters along log-links. Theorem 1.5(ii) supplies horizontal coricity of F⊢×μ_△-prime-strips along Gaussian theta-links.

The distinction is structural: one direction controls D-theaters, the other controls the weakened F⊢×μ interface.

Theorem 1.5(i)-(ii)coricity

Bi-coric F⊢×μ-prime-strips

Theorem 1.5(iii) constructs F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△) as an invariant under both lattice directions, up to the specified class of poly-isomorphisms.

The result also records compatibility with Kummer isomorphisms and ×μ-Kummer structures.

Theorem 1.5(iii)bi-coric

Bi-coric mono-analytic log-shells

Theorem 1.5(iv) transports the bi-coricity of F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△) to the corresponding mono-analytic log-shells and their enveloping log modules.

These shells are also related to holomorphic log-shells arising from the constant monoid data used in IUT II Corollary 4.10.

Theorem 1.5(iv)log-shells

Bi-coric realified Frobenioids

Theorem 1.5(v) promotes the bi-coric D-prime-strip comparison to global realified Frobenioid data, compatible with the R>0-orbits already present in IUT II.

This is the §1 preparation for later determinant and log-volume comparisons.

Theorem 1.5(v)realified
FeatureSource claimWiki/formalization consequence
Noncommutativity Squares in the log-theta lattice are far from 1-commutative. Do not model the lattice as a commuting square of ordinary maps.
Vertical cores Multiradiality requires objects invariant with respect to vertical arrows. Distinguish data attached to a vertical line from data transported across theta links.
Z^x effects Horizontal linking data is subject to unit-quotient indeterminacy. Do not force horizontal arrows to identify full arithmetic holomorphic structures.
p-adic analogy Vertical arrows correspond to Frobenius; horizontal arrows to mixed-characteristic extension structure. Use the analogy only as orientation; the formal objects remain the IUT lattice data.
The lattice page is therefore the bridge between the Hodge-theater taxonomy and the proof-critical corridor: it explains why the later comparison must be expressed through multiradial containers and controlled indeterminacies, rather than through an ordinary transport map between two schemes.
Sources: IUT III IUT IV Mutually Alien Copies Related: indeterminacies theta and log links multiradiality Corollary 3.12

Indeterminacies

Indeterminacies are controlled ambiguities that arise because IUT compares weakened data across non-scheme-theoretic links. In the Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12 corridor, the key point is that the three indeterminacies have different mathematical shapes and should not be collapsed into a single quotient.

Indeterminacy channels processions, unit-quotient automorphisms, and upper-semi log-Kummer data

Ind1

The automorphism ambiguity of the processions of D⊢-prime-strips used to form tensor packets. Concretely, this includes permutation automorphisms of the nested label containers S_{j+1}^{±} and automorphisms of the mono-analytic prime-strips in those processions.

Ind2

The non-Ind1 part of the automorphism ambiguity coming from F⊢×μ-prime-strips in the theta, Gaussian, LGP, and lgp links. At nonarchimedean places this includes the Z^x-indeterminacies acting on local O×μ copies.

Ind3

The upper-semi compatibility of tensor-packet log-shell Kummer isomorphisms along a fixed vertical line of the log-theta lattice. IUT III describes this by inclusions at nonarchimedean rational valuations and natural surjections at archimedean rational valuations.

IndeterminacySource locationEffect in estimates
Ind1 Theorem 3.11(i), processions of D⊢-prime-strips. Handled in IUT IV by allowing arbitrary automorphisms in the local estimate input.
Ind2 Theorem 3.11(i),(iii), F⊢×μ prime-strip link automorphisms. Handled together with Ind1 by automorphism-invariant containers and log-volume bounds.
Ind3 Theorem 3.11(ii), upper-semi log-Kummer compatibility. Handled by upper bounds: containers contain possible images rather than identify them by equality.
The most dangerous simplification is to turn one-sided upper-semi data into equality of possible-image regions without proving that this stronger relation is licensed by the source construction. This is precisely the distinction the wiki and the Lean corridor must keep visible.
Sources: IUT III IUT IV Dupuy-Hilado probabilistic Szpiro Higuchi 2026 Related: Corollary 3.12 multiradiality 3.12 diagnostics dispute

IUT III Definition 1.1 and Proposition 1.2: Local Log-Link Mechanism

The local log-link is the vertical move of the log-theta-lattice. At nonarchimedean places it applies the local p_v-adic logarithm to unit quotient data; at archimedean places it uses a universal-cover and torsion-quotient version of the complex logarithm. The output is not a map of full Frobenioid monoids, but a controlled route to log-shell modules.

From units to log-shells Definition 1.1, Proposition 1.2
Source itemConstructionAudit consequence
Definition 1.1(i) At nonarchimedean places, the local logarithm acts on the perfected unit quotient and gives the tautological log-link and scaled log-shell. The log-link is unit-facing; value-group data is not transported by the same formula.
Definition 1.1(ii) At archimedean places, a pointed universal cover and quotients by roots of unity produce an analogous logarithmic object. The archimedean construction has its own topological/Kummer shape.
Definition 1.1(iii) A full log-link is the tautological log-link followed by a poly-isomorphism log(F) -> F'. Multiradial use depends on the full-link formulation, not just the tautological path.
Proposition 1.2 Log-links preserve the D-side core and log-volume compatibility, but Kummer isomorphisms fail to commute elementwise with the coric comparison. This is the local source of upper-semi log-Kummer behavior.
Sources: IUT III, Definition 1.1 and Proposition 1.2 Related: theta/log links log-shell containment upper-semi Log-Kummer

IUT III Introduction: Vertical, Horizontal, and Bi-Coricity

IUT III introduces a directional vocabulary for invariance in the log-theta-lattice. Vertical coricity means invariance under log-links; horizontal coricity means invariance under theta-type links; bi-coricity means compatibility with both directions. The distinction is needed because the two directions use different pieces of arithmetic structure.

Directional invariance in the lattice IUT III Introduction
TermMeaningUse in the wiki
Vertical coricity Invariance under log-links, i.e. vertical arrows of the log-theta-lattice. Used for log-shell and LGP objects constructed along a fixed vertical line.
Horizontal coricity Invariance under theta-type links, including Gaussian, LGP, and lgp variants. Used for objects meant to be read from alien horizontal viewpoints.
Bi-coricity Coricity with respect to all arrows of the relevant log-theta-lattice. Critical for mono-analytic log-shells and containers used in Theorem 3.11.
Sources: IUT III, Introduction Related: log-theta lattice bi-coric log-shells Theorem 3.11 algorithms

IUT III Introduction: Upper-Semi Log-Kummer Compatibility

The log-link and Kummer isomorphisms do not form an elementwise commuting square. IUT III instead uses log-shells as upper containers for Kummer images from both sides of the log-link. This one-sided compatibility is the source of Ind3 in Theorem 3.11 and the later Corollary 3.12 proof route.

Compatibility by containment, not equality IUT III Introduction and Theorem 3.11(ii)
Source pointStatementFormalization consequence
Intro log-shell paragraph I_v contains the Kummer images associated to both the domain and codomain of the log-link. Model the log-shell as an upper container, not as an equality quotient.
Intro upper-semi paragraph Iterated log-links and Kummer isomorphisms give upper semi-compatibility of diagrams. Use direction-sensitive containment or surjection data.
Theorem 3.11(ii) Ind3 is expressed through local inclusion and surjection clauses in the log-Kummer correspondence. The Corollary 3.12 corridor must retain this one-sidedness.
This page is a guardrail for the Scholze-Stix/Mochizuki-sensitive corridor: replacing upper-semi containment by equality would strengthen the source claim and change the formal object being audited.
Sources: IUT III, Introduction and Theorem 3.11(ii) Related: Log-Kummer correspondences Indeterminacies Corollary 3.12 corridor

IUT III Remark 1.1.2: Tautological and Full Log-Links

Definition 1.1 first constructs a tautological log-link by applying the local logarithm inside a fixed Frobenius-like structure. Remark 1.1.2 then explains why the later multiradial theory mainly uses the full log-link: the full link replaces the specific Frobenius-dependent path by a tautologically indeterminate etale-like isomorphism of the local fundamental groups.

Two log-link modes Definition 1.1 and Remark 1.1.2
Source itemStatementWhy it matters
Definition 1.1(i),(ii) The tautological log-link constructs log(F_v) from the local logarithm and carries a specific relationship between the fundamental groups in the domain and codomain. It is the local construction that produces log-shells, but it still uses Frobenius-like monoids in an essential way.
Definition 1.1(iii) A full log-link is obtained by post-composing the tautological log-link with the full poly-isomorphism log(F) ≃ F'. This is the form used when the target must be described without a chosen Frobenius-like rigidifying path.
Remark 1.1.2(i) The tautological log-link gives a specific "rigidifying path"; the full log-link replaces it by the tautologically indeterminate isomorphism needed for etale-like multiradial algorithms. The wiki and formal models must distinguish "constructed by log" from "available up to full poly-isomorphism".
Audit rule: the full log-link is not extra decoration. It is the source device that lets later algorithms speak in etale-like terms rather than in terms of a specific Frobenius-like monoid path.
Sources: IUT III, Definition 1.1 and Remark 1.1.2 Related: local log-link mechanism Log-Kummer correspondences log-theta lattice

IUT III Remark 1.1.1: Unit-Only Domain of Iterated Log-Links

Remark 1.1.1 states that iterated log-links are to be understood only on the local unit groups in their domains. The purpose of the log-link is to construct log-shells from units; the non-unit or value-group portions of the monoids are not needed for this construction.

Domain portionUsed by log-link?Reason
Local units Yes. They feed the local logarithm and determine the log-shell.
Global product of local unit groups Yes, in the idelic setting. Iterates of log-links are read on the product of local units.
Value-group portions No, not for log-shell construction. They remain part of the separate value-group distortion story.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 1.1.1(i) Related: local log-link mechanism value-group distortion F⊢×μ log-shells

IUT III Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi): F⊢×μ Log-Shells

Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi) reconstructs log-shell modules from F⊢×μ-prime-strip data alone. The construction uses the unit quotient/perfection interface and is unaffected by quotienting units by torsion, which is exactly the weakened interface carried by the modified theta links of IUT II.

Place typeConstruction from F⊢×μConstraint
Nonarchimedean Uses the perfected unit portion and the G_v-action. Torsion quotients of units do not affect the log-shell module.
Archimedean Uses the split Frobenioid unit portion plus a pointed universal cover. The same construction may be read after quotienting by N-torsion.
Global collection I_{F⊢×μ} ⊆ log(F⊢×μ). The object is not regarded as constructed from the original full F-prime-strip.
This page is a source-level reason that the wiki separates Kummer-ready Frobenioid data from the weakened F⊢×μ log-shell interface used after modified theta links.
Sources: IUT III, Definition 1.1(iv)-(vi) Related: F×μ Kummer prime-strips Theta×μ links mono-analytic log-shells

IUT III Proposition 1.2(vi)-(viii): Mono-Analytic Log-Shells

Proposition 1.2 constructs log-shells from D⊢ prime-strip data and relates them by compatible poly-isomorphisms to the corresponding F⊢×μ and holomorphic F-prime-strip log-shells. This is the first local form of a log-shell that can be read without carrying a single arithmetic holomorphic history.

RepresentationSource dataUse
Mono-analytic log-shell D⊢-prime-strip. Provides an etale-like container for later multiradial use.
F⊢×μ log-shell Weakened Frobenioid/unit quotient data. Connects to modified theta-link inputs.
Holomorphic F log-shell Full F-prime-strip through the local log-link. Records the arithmetic holomorphic source of the shell.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 1.2(vi)-(viii) Related: log-shell variants D⊢ mono-analytic core bi-coric mono-analytic log-shells

IUT III Proposition 1.2(ix): Coric Holomorphic Log-Shells

Proposition 1.2(ix) starts from a D-prime-strip, forms the corresponding F-prime-strip F(D), and constructs holomorphic log-shells that are coric for the D-side data. These shells are distinct from, but naturally related to, the mono-analytic shells.

StepConstructionInterpretation
D-side input Start from a coric D-prime-strip. The shell is indexed by data invariant in the D-side picture.
F-side formation Construct F(D) and apply the full log-link. The holomorphic shell still has an F-prime-strip realization.
Comparison Relate to mono-analytic log-shells via Proposition 1.2 isomorphisms. The paper keeps multiple shell presentations, not one flattened object.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 1.2(ix) Related: mono-analytic log-shells log-shell containment log-link Frobenius-picture

IUT III Proposition 1.2(x): Log-Link Frobenius-Picture

Proposition 1.2(x) organizes iterated full log-links into an oriented integer-indexed Frobenius-picture of F-prime-strips. The associated D and D⊢ prime-strips form the fixed core of this chain under translation by Z.

Vertical Frobenius-picture full log-links with fixed D and D⊢ core
As with the theta-link Frobenius-picture, this is an oriented chain. The source does not license arbitrary adjacent swaps at this level.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 1.2(x) Related: coric holomorphic log-shells Frobenius/etale order log-theta lattice

IUT III Remark 1.2.4: Log-Wall Ring Incompatibility

Remark 1.2.4 states that the log-link is incompatible with the ring structures on the two sides: it does not arise from a ring homomorphism. This is the log-wall. The only structure manifestly compatible with the log-link is the associated D-prime-strip, while scheme-theoretic basepoints, Kummer structures, and certain birational-to- affine comparisons depend essentially on ring/scheme data.

StructureCompatibility with log-linkReason
D-prime-strip Compatible. It is the abstract group/Aut-holomorphic layer preserved across the log-wall.
Ring structure Not compatible. The log-link is not a ring homomorphism.
Scheme-theoretic basepoints Not compatible a priori. They are defined using geometric points, hence ring-homomorphism data.
Kummer theory Requires special handling. It depends on the multiplicative structure of the ring.
This page is a direct guardrail against modeling the log-link as ordinary transport of scheme structure. The source explicitly denies that interface.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 1.2.4 Related: tautological/full log-link upper-semi Log-Kummer Collas log-theta wandering

IUT III Remark 1.2.3: Root-Unity Indeterminacy

Remark 1.2.3 records a small but structurally important effect of applying a log-link: multiplicative indeterminacy by a subgroup of local units becomes additive indeterminacy by the logarithm of that subgroup. For roots of unity this additive indeterminacy disappears nonarchimedeanly and becomes a finite quotient issue archimedeanly.

Before log

A subgroup H subset O_k^x acts multiplicatively on the local unit-side data at one vertex of the Frobenius-picture.

After log

The next vertex sees additive indeterminacy by log_k(H) on the corresponding additive copy of the local ring.

Root-unity case

If H consists of roots of unity, the nonarchimedean logarithm gives no additive motion.

Remark clausePaper contentWiki consequence
1.2.3(i) Multiplicative unit indeterminacy at one vertex maps to additive indeterminacy by local logarithms at the subsequent vertex. The log-link changes the type of indeterminacy; it is not a plain relabeling of the same group action.
Roots of unity For a root-of-unity subgroup, log_k(H)=0 nonarchimedeanly. This feeds the later constant-multiple rigidity story for theta and Gaussian monoids.
1.2.3(ii) Global arithmetic line bundles amount to multiplicative translates by F_mod^x; overlaps occur only for elements that are units at all places, hence roots of unity. The global quotient must remember the exceptional root-of-unity overlap instead of treating all multiplicative translates as disjoint.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.2.3 Related: log-link Frobenius-picture constant multiple rigidity global degree/log-volume

IUT III Remark 1.2.4: Birational/Affine Basepoint Triangle

Remark 1.2.4 does more than say that the log-link is not a ring homomorphism. It isolates a specific failure mode: after crossing the log-wall, the groups G_v, Delta_v^birat, and Delta_v can still be read as abstract groups with outer actions, but the particular scheme-theoretic surjection Delta_v^birat -> Delta_v is not preserved.

Independent basepoint indeterminacies the dotted relation is not log-wall compatible
LayerWhat survives the log-wallWhat fails
G_v Can be treated as an abstract topological group. Its realization as automorphisms of a fixed ring model is not transported.
Delta_v^birat and Delta_v Each may be read with an outer G_v-action. The particular relation between them depends on scheme structure.
Dotted arrow It is visible before crossing the wall. The surjection Delta_v^birat -> Delta_v is not log-link compatible.
Formalization guardrail: do not bundle all three groups with a permanent commuting triangle unless the source theorem explicitly supplies that compatibility after crossing the log-wall.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.2.4(ii)-(iii) Related: log-wall ring incompatibility basepoints/inter-universality birational decomposition groups

IUT III Remark 1.2.5: Fixed Reference Model Coricity

Remark 1.2.5 connects the log-wall to the general mono-anabelian design rule: construction algorithms should avoid depending on fixed ring or scheme reference models unless that dependence is invariant, or coric, with respect to the action of the log-link.

QuestionSource answerUse in the wiki
What is being avoided? Algorithms depending on a fixed ring/scheme-theoretic reference model. Pages for log-link data should distinguish reconstructed/coric data from chosen model data.
When is dependence allowed? When the dependence is invariant under the action of log on such models. This is the local meaning of coric data in the log-wall context.
Where is it formalized? Through observables, families of homotopies, and telecores in Absolute Anabelian Topics III. The wiki cross-links absolute-anabelian pages instead of pretending IUT III alone defines all prerequisites.
This page explains why the wiki repeatedly separates "source model", "reconstructed object", and "coric observable". In IUT III these are not stylistic distinctions; they control what can pass through the log-wall.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.2.5 Related: cores and telecores absolute anabelian log-shells model-free specimens

IUT III Proposition 1.3: Hodge-Theater Log-Link

Proposition 1.3 lifts the prime-strip log-link of Proposition 1.2 to Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters. An isomorphism of the associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters determines synchronized log-links on the constituent F-prime-strips of the theta and plus-minus bridges.

LayerConstructionWhy synchronized
D-theater input Start from an isomorphism of associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters. The D-side is the coric spine of the theater-level log-link.
F-prime-strip output Each constituent F-prime-strip receives the corresponding log-link. The choices are not independent local decorations.
F_l^{±} compatibility Remarks 1.3.1-1.3.2 preserve plus-minus synchronization. Conjugate synchronization constrains the log-link before Theorem 3.11.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 1.3 and Remarks 1.3.1-1.3.2 Related: local log-link mechanism conjugate synchronization Gaussian log-theta lattice

IUT III Remark 1.3.1: Single-Xi Log-Link Construction

Remark 1.3.1 says that Proposition 1.3 must first construct the log-link from a single isomorphism Xi of associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters, before replacing Xi by a poly-isomorphism. The reason is plus-minus synchronization in the underlying Theta±ell-Hodge theater.

StageSource instructionWiki/formalization consequence
Single isomorphism Build the theater log-link for one fixed Xi. The synchronized plus/minus labels are still controlled by one chosen D-side comparison.
Poly-isomorphism Only after the single-isomorphism construction, range over constituent isomorphisms of a poly-isomorphism. A formal record should not start with an unordered bag of local log-links.
Guardrail The order of construction protects the plus-minus synchronization cited from IUT I and IUT II. This is a source-level reason for representing theater-level log-links as structured data, not merely as independent local links.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.3.1 Related: Hodge-theater log-link Theta±ell rigidity basepoint relay

IUT III Remark 1.3.2: F_l± Compatibility of Log-Links

Remark 1.3.2 records that the constituent F-prime-strips of the capsule F_T are initially treated without using the F_l^{±}-symmetry, but the resulting log-links are compatible with the F_l^{±}-symmetrizing isomorphisms and the conjugate synchronization they determine.

ObjectSource statementWhy it matters
Constituent F_t The construction of Proposition 1.3(i) considers each constituent F-prime-strip first. The local link is not obtained by quotienting away label information in advance.
Labeled monoids The associated labeled monoid collections are compatible with the F_l^{±}-symmetrizing isomorphisms. The source ties the log-link to IUT II conjugate synchronization rather than treating it as free extra structure.
Corollary 3.12 proof Mochizuki explicitly points forward to Step (vi) of the proof of Corollary 3.12. This page is a dependency edge from Section 1 log-link construction into the disputed corridor.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.3.2 Related: IUT II conjugate synchronization unit synchronization Corollary 3.12 proof steps

IUT III Remark 1.3.3: D-Theater Control of Frobenioid Portions

Remark 1.3.3 explains why the Frobenioid-theoretic portions of the Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters in Proposition 1.3 do not introduce the ring/scheme incompatibility described in Remark 1.2.4. The claim is that these portions are fully faithfully controlled by the associated etale-like D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater structures.

Remark 1.3.3 control statement Frobenioid portions are governed through the D-theater spine
Domain/codomain theaters

Frobenioid portions occur on both sides of the theater log-link, but are not connected by scheme-theoretic data of the kind excluded in Remark 1.2.4.

D-theater spine

The associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater gives the etale-like coric structure that fully faithfully controls these portions.

Delicate plus-minus part

The underlying Theta±ell F-prime-strips are handled by intrinsic D-Theta±ell structure, preserving plus-minus synchronization.

PortionSource treatmentAudit consequence
Global Thetaell bridge Its global portion contains no global Frobenioid-theoretic data. Do not invent a global Frobenioid comparison for this bridge.
NF-bridge global data May have independent basepoints under F_l-symmetry. Basepoint independence is tracked, not erased.
Underlying Theta±ell portion The delicate plus-minus synchronization is defined via intrinsic D-Theta±ell structure. The coric D-side control is the reason the log-link avoids the Remark 1.2.4 incompatibility.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.3.3 Related: log-wall ring incompatibility reference-model coricity single-Xi construction

IUT III Definition 1.4: Gaussian Log-Theta Lattice

Definition 1.4 defines the Z × Z-indexed log-theta lattice of Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters. Vertical arrows are full log-links. Horizontal arrows are either Theta×μ links in the non-Gaussian lattice or Theta×μ_gau links in the Gaussian lattice. Theorem 1.5 is stated for the Gaussian version.

AxisArrow typeReading
Vertical Full log-link. Uses local logarithms and produces log-shell access.
Horizontal, non-Gaussian Theta×μ link. Modified theta-link before Gaussian evaluation.
Horizontal, Gaussian Theta×μ_gau link. Gaussian-evaluated lattice used for Theorem 1.5 and later LGP data.
Sources: IUT III, Definition 1.4 Related: Theta×μ links Theta×μ_gau multiradiality bi-cores

IUT III Remark 1.4.1: Horizontal Arrows Obligate Vertical Arrows

Remark 1.4.1 says that log-theta-lattice squares are far from commutative. Horizontal theta motion is incompatible with the local ring structures used by vertical logarithms; after a horizontal arrow, the theory of log-shells requires operating in a theater that is the codomain of a vertical log-link.

MoveSource contentConsequence
Vertical log-link Uses local logarithms defined through local ring structures. Vertical motion is where log-shell access is created.
Horizontal theta-link Identifies unit-type data but is essentially incompatible with those local ring structures. Horizontal motion alone is not enough for the later multiradial representation.
Obligation Each horizontal execution obligates a subsequent vertical execution. The relevant common objects must be vertical cores, not arbitrary objects in a square.
This is one of the local source reasons the wiki treats the log-theta lattice as a noncommutative two-dimensional apparatus rather than a commutative grid.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.4.1 Related: Gaussian log-theta lattice later arrow-dependence page vertical/horizontal coricity

IUT III Remark 1.4.2: Why Forced Horizontal Compatibility Does Not Simplify

Remark 1.4.2 considers a tempting simplification: force horizontal theta arrows to be compatible with arithmetic holomorphic structures by declaring isomorphisms of the corresponding Pi_v's. Mochizuki's answer is that the Z^x-indeterminacy acting on O^{x,mu} immediately reintroduces path bookkeeping.

AttemptFailure modeSource interpretation
Rigidify horizontally by Pi_v One must still track each nontrivial Z^x action on O^{x,mu}. The common holomorphic structure is obliterated whenever the indeterminacy is applied.
Record operation histories The bookkeeping becomes paths alternating between rigidification and indeterminacy operations. The horizontal lines of the log-theta lattice already serve as universal covers of these loops.
Claimed simplification No substantive simplification results. The lattice apparatus is not avoidable by forcing scheme-style compatibility on horizontal arrows.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.4.2 Related: horizontal/vertical obligation IUT IV ring incompatibility closed-loop analogy

IUT III Theorem 1.5(i)-(ii): Vertical and Horizontal Coricity

Theorem 1.5 begins by separating two invariance statements. Vertical arrows identify associated D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters along a log-link direction. Horizontal Gaussian arrows identify associated F⊢×μ_△-prime-strips along a theta-link direction.

CoricityObject controlledDirection
Vertical D-Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater. Log-link direction, fixed horizontal coordinate.
Horizontal F⊢×μ_△-prime-strip. Gaussian theta-link direction, fixed vertical coordinate.
Combined use Bi-coric weakened prime-strip and log-shell data. Only after the two source statements are kept distinct.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 1.5(i)-(ii) Related: vertical/horizontal coricity overview bi-coric F⊢×μ prime-strips log-theta lattice

IUT III Theorem 1.5(iii): Bi-Coric F⊢×μ Prime-Strips

Theorem 1.5(iii) constructs F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△) as an invariant, up to the specified full poly-isomorphisms, under both lattice directions. It records compatibility with Kummer isomorphisms and ×μ Kummer structures.

InputBi-coric outputDownstream role
Common D⊢ data from vertical cores. F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△). Weak prime-strip interface used by LGP constructions.
Horizontal Gaussian coricity. Full poly-isomorphism across theta-link direction. Keeps the weakened link domain stable under Gaussian motion.
Kummer structures. Compatibility with unit quotient data. Prepares the log-Kummer correspondence rather than replacing it.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 1.5(iii) Related: F⊢×μ log-shells bi-coric mono-analytic log-shells LGP/lgp lattice

IUT III Theorem 1.5(iv): Bi-Coric Mono-Analytic Log-Shells

Theorem 1.5(iv) transports the bi-coricity of F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△) to the corresponding mono-analytic log-shells and their ambient log modules. These shells are also compared with holomorphic log-shells coming from the constant monoid data of IUT II.

Shell layerBi-coricity statementWhy it matters
Mono-analytic shell Invariant across arbitrary lattice indices via the specified poly-isomorphisms. Gives a common container for later tensor-packet log-shells.
Ambient log module Moves with the shell under the same comparison. Needed for image containment and log-volume readings.
Holomorphic shell comparison Relates the mono-analytic shell to shells from constant monoid data. Connects IUT II Gaussian constructions to IUT III log-shell methods.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 1.5(iv) Related: mono-analytic log-shells constant F_△ prime-strip Theorem 3.11 algorithms

IUT III Theorem 1.5(v): Bi-Coric Realified Frobenioids

Theorem 1.5(v) promotes the bi-coric D-prime-strip comparison to global realified Frobenioid data, compatible with the R>0-orbits of arithmetic-degree scaling already present in IUT II. This is the Section 1 preparation for later determinant and log-volume comparison.

LayerComparisonLater use
D-prime-strip Bi-coric poly-isomorphism across the Gaussian log-theta lattice. Provides the D-side index/control structure.
Global realified Frobenioid Compatible with arithmetic-degree R>0-orbits. Feeds determinant and log-volume estimates.
Conjugate synchronization Remark 1.5.1 emphasizes its role for subscript-△ constructions. Prevents treating the realified comparison as independent of label synchronization.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 1.5(v) and Remark 1.5.1 Related: coric realified Frobenioids conjugate synchronization tensor/log-volume corridor

IUT III Remark 1.5.1: Diagonal-Conjugacy No-Go

Remark 1.5.1 explains why conjugate synchronization cannot be replaced by the simpler idea of working with the conjugacy class of a diagonal embedding G -> G x G. In a nonabelian Galois group, conjugating one component changes the component by a commutator, which would identify distinct Kummer classes attached to distinct theta values.

Naive diagonal quotient loses component information the obstruction is nonabelian and Kummer-theoretic
Diagonal copy

The tempting replacement starts from a diagonal element (g,g) in G x G.

Conjugate one side

Passing to conjugacy classes identifies it with (g,hgh^-1), equivalently (g,[h,g]g).

Kummer data collapses

The commutator factor in one component is meaningful for distinct Kummer classes, so the quotient is not a valid substitute for synchronization.

Naive replacementSource objectionConsequence
Use a conjugacy class of diagonals. It identifies (g,g) with (g,hgh^-1). The second component changes by [h,g], which is meaningful in a nonabelian group.
Ignore component distinction. Distinct components encode distinct Galois-theoretic Kummer classes attached to distinct theta values. The quotient destroys exactly the data conjugate synchronization is built to preserve.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.5.1(ii) Related: F_l± compatibility conjugate synchronization bi-coric realified Frobenioids

IUT III Remark 1.5.2: No Bi-Coric F_sol and Multiradial Containers

Remark 1.5.2 states a negative boundary. The F_l symmetry on labeled copies of F_mod subset F_sol subset F does not support an analogue of the local conjugate synchronization for G_v. In particular, there is no bi-coric F_sol^x with its ring structure analogous to the bi-coric unit/log-shell objects of Theorem 1.5.

BoundarySource contentReplacement
No full algebraic-closure synchronization F_l-symmetry does not give conjugate synchronization for labeled copies of the full algebraic closure. Only weaker action on F_mod, F_sol, and infinity-kappa-coric pseudo-monoids is available.
No bi-coric F_sol^x Solvable ring reconstruction is incompatible with localization operations in a D-ThetaNF-Hodge theater. Do not model the lattice as transporting a full ring/object unchanged.
Closest approximation Bi-coric global realified Frobenioids, essentially log-volume, are the closest possible approximation. Bi-coric log-shells act as multiradial containers for labeled F_mod copies up to indeterminacy.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.5.2 Related: bi-coric mono-analytic log-shells bi-coric realified Frobenioids F_l without conjugate synchronization

IUT III Remark 1.5.4: Kummer-Detachment and Etale-Transport Indeterminacies

Remark 1.5.4 splits the comparison of one arithmetic holomorphic structure from another into two operations. First, Frobenius-like objects are converted to etale-like objects via Kummer isomorphisms. Second, the etale-like objects are transported between arithmetic holomorphic structures by multiradiality. The corresponding losses of rigidity are named Kummer-detachment and etale-transport indeterminacies.

StepOperationIndeterminacy computed
(a) Pass from Frobenius-like structures to etale-like structures via Kummer isomorphisms. Kummer-detachment indeterminacies, including Frobenius-picture path choices and Z^x effects.
(b) Transport the resulting etale-like structures between arithmetic holomorphic structures by multiradiality. Etale-transport indeterminacies, often from anabelian/group-theoretic algorithms.
Use in the wiki The taxonomy separates damage incurred during detachment from damage incurred during transport. Later Corollary 3.12 pages should not merge these two audit categories.
Source examples: Frobenius-picture diagrams in Propositions 1.2(x) and 1.3(iv) illustrate Kummer-detachment path issues; non-scheme-theoretic automorphisms of local absolute Galois groups illustrate etale-transport indeterminacy.
Source: IUT III, Remark 1.5.4 Related: Kummer detachment indeterminacies Theorem 3.11 algorithms

IUT III Proposition 1.2: Log-Shell Variants

Proposition 1.2 packages the log-link output into several log-shell forms: holomorphic log-shells attached to an F-prime-strip, mono-analytic log-shells attached to D⊢-data, and coric holomorphic log-shells constructed from a D prime-strip. The theorem also records which compatibilities are actual isomorphisms and which are only upper-semi containment phenomena.

Three log-shell roles holomorphic, mono-analytic, and coric holomorphic
ClauseContentFormalization consequence
Proposition 1.2(iii) The log-link is compatible with the relevant nonarchimedean and archimedean log-volume conventions. Volume compatibility may be modeled separately from elementwise commutation.
Proposition 1.2(iv) Kummer isomorphisms fail to be compatible with the coric comparison at the level of the diagrams (*)_non and (*)_arc. This is the precise warning against replacing the log-Kummer interface by equality.
Proposition 1.2(v) Holomorphic log-shells are compact and contain the relevant unit-side images. Remark 1.2.2 makes this concrete as p_v^{-1}log(O_k^*) nonarchimedeanly and a radius-pi archimedean container. The upper container has different local analytic forms at different place types.
Proposition 1.2(vi)-(viii) Mono-analytic log-shells are constructed functorially from D⊢-data and are poly-isomorphic to the associated F⊢×mu and holomorphic log-shells. The etale-like container side is not optional; it is the multiradial interface.
Proposition 1.2(ix),(x) Coric holomorphic log-shells are constructed from a D-prime-strip; iterated full log-links form a Frobenius-picture graph with translation symmetry but no arbitrary adjacent-vertex swaps. Coric invariants are tied to the oriented chain, not to a symmetric quotient.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 1.2 and Remark 1.2.2 Related: upper-semi Log-Kummer Log-Kummer correspondences Theorem 3.11 algorithms

IUT III Theorem 1.5: Bi-Cores of the Log-Theta-Lattice

Theorem 1.5 collects the first major invariance package of IUT III. Vertical log-links identify the associated D-Hodge theaters; horizontal Gaussian links identify the associated F⊢×mu-prime-strips; combining the two produces bi-coric prime-strips, mono-analytic log-shells, and realified global Frobenioids.

Bi-core package vertical, horizontal, and combined coricity
ClauseStatementUse downstream
Theorem 1.5(i) Vertical arrows induce full poly-isomorphisms between the associated D-Theta±ellNF Hodge theaters in a fixed column. Provides the vertical coric core later used for log-shell invariance.
Theorem 1.5(ii) Horizontal arrows induce full poly-isomorphisms between the associated F⊢×mu-prime-strips along a fixed row. Provides the horizontal coric side of the Gaussian log-theta-lattice.
Theorem 1.5(iii) Certain F⊢×mu-prime-strips constructed from D⊢-data are invariant, up to the specified poly-isomorphisms, in both lattice directions. This is the source of the bi-coric prime-strip object used in LGP construction.
Theorem 1.5(iv) The bi-coric prime-strip isomorphisms induce corresponding mono-analytic log-shell isomorphisms, compatible with the holomorphic log-shells produced from the source data. This is the log-shell layer used in tensor packets and log-volume estimates.
Theorem 1.5(v) The same poly-isomorphisms induce compatible isomorphisms of the associated global realified Frobenioid data. Feeds the global LGP/lgp Frobenioid and pilot-object pages.
Theorem 1.5 is the transition from local log-link technology to lattice-level reusable objects. It does not prove Corollary 3.12, but it supplies the bi-coric log-shell and Frobenioid objects that Theorem 3.11 later acts on.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 1.5 Related: vertical/horizontal coricity theta monoid vertical coricity tensor packets vertically coric LGP

Log-Kummer Correspondences

IUT III uses log-Kummer correspondences to compare Frobenius-like objects carried by Hodge-theater Frobenioids with etale-like objects that form multiradial containers. The comparison is deliberately upper-semi: log-shells contain the relevant images, but the source does not assert elementwise equality of all possible images.

From two versions to an upper container Frobenius-like data, etale-like data, and log-shell containment
IUT III source item Content Consequence for the 3.12 corridor
Introduction, log-link discussion Kummer isomorphisms relate the coric version of local Pi_v ↷ O_Fv data to copies appearing on both sides of a log-link, but these isomorphisms are not compatible with coricity at element level. The log-link cannot be audited as an equality-preserving transport of all local ring data.
Definition 1.1 and Proposition 1.2 The log-shell contains images from the domain and codomain of the log-link and carries compatible log-volume data across the permitted poly-isomorphisms. The comparison target is a container with log-volume control, not the original local unit set itself.
Introduction, upper-semi paragraph Iterating log-links and Kummer isomorphisms yields diagrams that are only upper semi-compatible. This is presented as the source of Ind3. The later inequality arises from one-sided containment/interference, not from a symmetric quotient argument.
Remark 3.12.2 Multiradial containers arise from etale-like versions of objects but are applied as containers for Frobenius-like versions. The theory must track the consequences of identifying these two versions. A formal model must mark where Frobenius-like and etale-like objects are distinct labels and where they are deliberately compared.
Theorem 3.11(ii) The log-Kummer correspondence relates tensor-packet log-shells, splitting monoids, and global Frobenioids through Kummer isomorphisms; the local clauses include inclusions at nonarchimedean rational valuations and surjections at archimedean rational valuations. This is the typed source of Ind3; it should not be replaced by a generic equivalence relation.

Frobenius-like label

Frobenius-like objects are the monoidal/Frobenioid objects carried by a Hodge theater: theta monoids, LGP monoids, and global realified Frobenioids before etale readout.

Frobenioidmonoid

Etale-like label

Etale-like objects are read through Galois/Kummer constructions and supply the multiradial containers. They are comparable to Frobenius-like objects only through specified Kummer isomorphisms.

GaloisKummer

Upper container

The log-shell is large enough to contain Kummer images from both sides of a log-link. Its function in the proof corridor is containment and log-volume control.

log-shellupper bound

Nonarchimedean clause

In the source statement of Theorem 3.11(ii), the upper-semi local behavior at nonarchimedean rational valuations is expressed by inclusions of possible images into the relevant log-shell packet.

Ind3inclusion

Archimedean clause

At archimedean rational valuations, the corresponding upper-semi behavior is expressed by natural surjections. This is a different local shape from the nonarchimedean inclusion clause.

Ind3surjection

No-loop discipline

IUT III avoids vicious loops while the Frobenius-like and etale-like versions remain distinct labels. The delicate step is using etale-like containers for Frobenius-like objects without erasing that distinction.

Remark 3.12.2labels
Audit rule: any wiki or Lean formulation that turns the log-Kummer correspondence into equality of all possible images has strengthened the source. The source gives upper-semi container behavior, and that asymmetry is part of the route to Corollary 3.12.
Sources: IUT III, Introduction and Theorem 3.11(ii) Related: indeterminacies multiradiality tensor/log-volume corridor Corollary 3.12 corridor

Theta Monoid Vertical Coricity in IUT III 2.1

Proposition 2.1 globalizes the multiradial local theory of theta monoids to the Gaussian log-theta lattice.
