Arbitrary subgroups of Fmod^× also do not support this additive representation unless they arise as multiplicative groups of subfields.

Formalization guardrail. The additive representation of objects belongs to the Fmod side. Replacing it by an arbitrary realified multiplicative subgroup loses the module-style object representation needed for mono-analytic log-shell comparison.
StructureAdditive representation?Source consequence
Fmod^× ∪ {0}Yes.Supports the fractional-ideal/module representation of objects.
Perfection or realification rational-function monoidsNo.Cannot replace Fmod when additive object-level comparison is needed.
Arbitrary subgroup Γ⊆Fmod^×Generally no.Adding torsor elements is meaningful only when Γ is additively closed in the required sense.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.6.2 Related: two global models mono-analytic log-shells log-shells warning

IUT III Proposition 3.7(i)-(ii): Single Packet Global Frobenioids

Proposition 3.7 constructs global Frobenioids for each packet label α∈A. The rational-function torsor version (FMOD)_α is built from the field image of the global tensor packet. The local fractional-ideal version (Fmod)_α uses the same field together with the Galois invariants of local packet monoids Ψ_log(A,αF_v).

VersionInputOutput
Rational-function torsorImage (MMOD)_α of the global packet localization.(FMOD)_α and an isomorphism from (Fmod)_α.
Local fractional idealField (Mmod)_α plus local Galois-invariant packet monoids.(Fmod)_α and natural isomorphisms to the other models.
RealificationsRealify the two Frobenioid versions.(F^R_MOD)_α and (F^R_mod)_α.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.7(i)-(ii) Related: global packet localization single packet monoids global realified LGP/lgp

IUT III Proposition 3.7(iii)-(iv): Global Realified LGP/lgp

Proposition 3.7(iii)-(iv) constructs the global realified LGP and lgp Frobenioids functorially in a log-link of Θ±ellNF Hodge theaters. The LGP construction uses the realified FMOD route; the lgp construction uses the realified Fmod route. Both produce F-prime-strips tautologically isomorphic to the Gaussian source strip.

Realified Global Targets LGP via FMOD, lgp via Fmod
ConstructionUsesOutput
C_LGPRealifications (F^R_mod)_α ≃ (F^R_MOD)_α applied to the Gaussian global realified Frobenioid.F_LGP and a natural isomorphism F_gau≃F_LGP.
C_lgpNatural realified Fmod isomorphisms instead of the FMOD route.F_lgp and tautological isomorphisms F_gau≃F_LGP≃F_lgp.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.7(iii)-(iv) Related: single packet global Frobenioids bad-prime global objects Theta×mu_LGP link

IUT III Proposition 3.7(v): Bad-Prime Data to Global Objects

Proposition 3.7(v) embeds the global realified LGP and lgp Frobenioids into products of the labeled realified packet Frobenioids. Using the fractional-ideal definition of (Fmod)_j, local fractional ideals generated by elements of bad-prime monoids construct global objects in C_LGP and C_lgp, compatibly with the local maps ρ_{LGP,v} and ρ_{lgp,v}.

InputConstructionOutput
Bad-prime elements of Ψ_Flgp(HT)_vGenerate local fractional ideals in the packet model.Objects in labeled (Fmod)_j factors.
Products of realification functorsEmbed labeled factors into product realified Frobenioids.Global objects in C_lgp and C_LGP.
Local maps ρ_vCompatibility imposed by Proposition 3.7(v).Ensures local-to-global comparison remains typed.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.7(v) Related: bad-prime LGP splittings global realified LGP/lgp theta/q pilot objects

IUT III Definition 3.8(i): Theta/q Pilot Objects

Definition 3.8(i) distinguishes theta-pilot objects from q-pilot objects. A theta-pilot object is determined by generators, up to torsion, of the bad-prime splitting monoids on the LGP/lgp side and is regarded inside C_LGP or C_lgp. A q-pilot object lies in the delta-side global realified Frobenioid C_△ and is determined concretely by the bad-prime Tate parameters q_v.

Pilot Objects theta side versus delta/q side
PilotDetermining dataAmbient Frobenioid
Theta-pilotGenerators up to torsion of Ψ_Flgp^⊥(HT)_v, indexed by bad primes.C_LGP or C_lgp.
q-pilotGenerators up to torsion of the split Frobenioid side, concretely the bad-prime q_v.C_△.
Sources: IUT III, Definition 3.8(i) Related: bad-prime global objects global realified LGP/lgp theta/q pilot overview Corollary 3.12 corridor

LGP and lgp Gaussian Log-Theta Lattices in IUT III 3.8

Definition 3.8(ii)-(iii) defines the horizontal Theta×mu_LGP and Theta×mu_lgp links as full poly-isomorphisms of F×mu-prime-strips, then places them with full vertical log-links into two Gaussian log-theta lattices. The following remarks state which earlier results transfer formally and why unit portions cannot be replaced by log-shells alone.

Definition 3.8(ii)-(iii) horizontal LGP/lgp links plus vertical full log-links
Source itemStatementWiki/formalization consequence
Definition 3.8(ii) From a log-link ‡HT -> †HT and a target Hodge theater *HT, form the associated F×mu-prime-strips *F_delta, †F_LGP, and †F_lgp. The horizontal links are defined relative to the vertical log-link; they are not free-standing identifications of Hodge theaters.
Definition 3.8(ii) The Theta×mu_LGP link and Theta×mu_lgp link are full poly-isomorphisms from †F×mu_LGP or †F×mu_lgp to *F×mu_delta. A page or Lean interface should store which prime-strip variant supplies the horizontal arrow: LGP and lgp are related but distinct.
Definition 3.8(iii) Arrange Theta±ellNF Hodge theaters in a Z×Z-indexed grid. Vertical arrows are full log-links; horizontal arrows are the LGP or lgp links defined relative to the preceding vertical log-link. The diagram is a routed lattice of typed arrows, not merely a rectangular array of copies.
Remark 3.8.1 The LGP and lgp lattices are closely related. The later theory mainly uses the LGP lattice, but computation of the Theta×mu_LGP link uses the Theta×mu_lgp link and related theta-pilot objects. Both link types map theta-pilots to q-pilots. The lgp side is auxiliary but essential; it should not be dropped from the explanatory map.
Remark 3.8.2 Theorem 1.5, Proposition 2.1, Corollary 2.3, and Proposition 3.5 generalize formally to the LGP and lgp Gaussian log-theta lattices. Earlier vertical coricity and upper-semi pages can be reused here, but only because their substantive portions are unaffected by the LGP/lgp transition.
Remark 3.8.3 It is not sufficient to replace the unit portions of the F×mu-prime-strips by the associated log-shells. The nontrivial local Galois action on unit portions is necessary for the Kummer theory of the local and global objects contained by log-shells. This is a source-level anti-simplification rule: log-shells are multiradial containers, not substitutes for the Kummer-theoretic unit data.

Theta×mu_LGP link

The LGP horizontal arrow is the full poly-isomorphism of F×mu-prime-strips from the global realified LGP construction to the delta target, relative to the fixed log-link.

Definition 3.8(ii)LGP

Theta×mu_lgp link

The lgp horizontal arrow is built analogously from the lgp prime-strip. Remark 3.8.1 says this link is needed in the computation of the LGP link.

Definition 3.8(ii)lgp

Horizontal arrows depend on vertical log-links

In Definition 3.8(iii), the horizontal arrow from n,m to n+1,m is defined relative to the full log-link from n,m-1 to n,m.

Definition 3.8(iii)grid

LGP versus lgp

The later theory is mainly organized around the LGP lattice, while the lgp lattice supplies related objects needed for computation. The wiki keeps both names because their source roles differ.

Remark 3.8.1do not merge

Formal transfer

Vertical coricity, theta-monoid Kummer theory, multiradiality, and upper-semi compatibility transfer formally to these lattices according to Remark 3.8.2.

Remark 3.8.2reuse

Log-shells alone are not enough

Remark 3.8.3 blocks a common simplification: trivial Galois action on log-shell containers cannot replace the nontrivial Galois action on unit portions required by Kummer theory.

Remark 3.8.3Kummer data
Audit rule. In the 3.11 to 3.12 corridor, any simplification that keeps log-shell containers but discards the unit portions of the F×mu-prime-strips has lost source data that Mochizuki explicitly calls necessary for Kummer theory.
Sources: IUT III, Definition 3.8 and Remarks 3.8.1-3.8.3 Related: global packet Frobenioids theta/q pilot objects LGP Gaussian log-theta lattice log-shells warning Corollary 3.12 corridor

IUT III Definition 3.8(ii): Theta×mu_LGP Link

Given a log-link ‡HT → †HT and a target Hodge theater *HT, Definition 3.8(ii) defines the Θ×μ_LGP-link as the full poly-isomorphism of F×μ-prime-strips †F^×μ_LGP ≃ *F^×μ_△. The link is explicitly relative to the preceding log-link; it is not a free-standing identification of theaters.

Theta×mu_LGP horizontal link defined relative to a vertical log-link
Sources: IUT III, Definition 3.8(ii) Related: global realified LGP/lgp Theta×mu_lgp link lattice arrow dependence

IUT III Definition 3.8(ii): Theta×mu_lgp Link

The Θ×μ_lgp-link is the analogous full poly-isomorphism †F^×μ_lgp ≃ *F^×μ_△. It uses the lgp prime-strip rather than the LGP prime-strip. Remark 3.8.1 later says this lower-case link is essential in the computation of the Θ×μ_LGP-link.

LinkSource prime-stripTarget
Θ×μ_lgp†F^×μ_lgp*F^×μ_△
Relative datumThe same preceding log-link ‡HT → †HT.
RoleAuxiliary but essential for computing the uppercase LGP link and pilot-object passage.
Sources: IUT III, Definition 3.8(ii) and Remark 3.8.1 Related: Theta×mu_LGP link LGP versus lgp theta/q pilot objects

IUT III Definition 3.8(iii): Lattice Arrow Dependence

Definition 3.8(iii) arranges theaters in a Z×Z grid. Vertical arrows are full log-links. The horizontal arrow from n,mHT to n+1,mHT is the LGP or lgp link defined relative to the preceding vertical full log-link n,m-1HT → n,mHT. Thus horizontal motion depends on vertical log-link context.

Arrow Dependence horizontal links are defined relative to the prior vertical log-link
Sources: IUT III, Definition 3.8(iii) Related: Theta×mu_LGP link Theta×mu_lgp link log-theta lattice

IUT III Remark 3.8.1: LGP Versus lgp

Remark 3.8.1 says the LGP-Gaussian and lgp-Gaussian log-theta-lattices are closely related, but the later theory mainly uses the LGP lattice. Nevertheless, the computation of the Θ×μ_LGP-link uses the Θ×μ_lgp-link and related theta-pilot objects in an essential way. Both link types map theta-pilot objects to q-pilot objects.

Lattice/linkRemark 3.8.1 roleAudit consequence
LGPMain lattice for the subsequent theory.Primary route into later log-volume estimates.
lgpNeeded to compute the LGP link.Cannot be dropped as merely duplicate notation.
Theta/q pilotsBoth link types map theta-pilots to q-pilots.Preserve the pilot-object correspondence in formal models.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.8.1 Related: theta/q pilot objects Theta×mu_LGP link Theta×mu_lgp link

IUT III Remark 3.8.2: Formal Transfer

Remark 3.8.2 states that Theorem 1.5, Proposition 2.1, Corollary 2.3, and Proposition 3.5 generalize formally to LGP- and lgp-Gaussian log-theta lattices. The reason is not that the new lattices are identical to the old Gaussian lattice; rather, the substantive portions used by those results are unaffected by the transition to LGP/lgp.

Transferred resultPreviously established roleLGP/lgp consequence
Theorem 1.5Vertical/horizontal coricity and log-theta lattice structure.Applies formally to LGP/lgp lattices.
Proposition 2.1 and Corollary 2.3Theta-monoid Kummer and multiradiality machinery.Reusable in the LGP/lgp setting.
Proposition 3.5Vertically coric LGP and upper-semi compatibility.Transfers because relevant portions are substantively unchanged.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.8.2 Related: vertical/horizontal coricity theta monoid multiradiality vertically coric LGP

IUT III Remark 3.8.3: Log-Shells Alone Are Not Enough

Remark 3.8.3 blocks a tempting simplification. One cannot replace the unit portions of the F×μ-prime-strips in the horizontal arrows by the associated log-shells alone. Although local Galois groups act trivially on those log-shell containers, the nontrivial Galois action on the local unit portions is necessary for the Kummer theory of the local and global objects contained by the log-shells.

3.12 guardrail. A model that retains only log-shell containers but discards the unit portions of the F×μ-prime-strips has lost source data explicitly required for Frobenius-like/etale-like Kummer comparison.
Candidate simplificationSource verdictReason
Replace unit portions by log-shellsInsufficient.Log-shells have trivial local Galois action at nonarchimedean places.
Keep F×μ unit portionsNecessary.Nontrivial local Galois action is needed for Kummer theory.
Role of log-shellsContainers, not substitutes.They contain objects whose Kummer theory still depends on unit data.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.8.3 Related: F⊢×mu log-shells log-Kummer correspondences total log-Kummer correspondence Corollary 3.12 corridor

Packet and Procession Log-Volumes in IUT III 3.9

Proposition 3.9 defines the log-volume layer for the tensor-packet and LGP/lgp constructions. It fixes local packet normalizations, transfers them to mono-analytic log-shell packets, forms global sums, and records compatibility with vertical log-links. The following remarks explain why the procession average is forced and why the result must remain real-valued, not valued modulo a dilation period.

Proposition 3.9 local packets, mono-analytic transfer, global sum, and log-link compatibility
Source itemDefinition or assertionAudit consequence
3.9(i), nonarch. On nonempty compact open subsets of the direct-sum fields in IQ(alpha F), IQ(AF), and IQ(A,alpha F), the local holomorphic integral structures have log-volume zero, and multiplication by the local prime adds -log(p_v). The sign convention is part of the source definition and must be preserved when later upper-ray inequalities are stated.
3.9(i), arch. On compact closures of nonempty open subsets of the direct-sum complex fields, radial log-volumes are summed over direct summands; local holomorphic integral structures have log-volume zero, and multiplication by e adds 1. The archimedean clause has its own normalization and object class for measurable regions.
3.9(i), processions For processions, log-volumes on products of packet-region spaces are normalized by averaging over the capsules under consideration. The procession-normalized number is not an arbitrary weighted average.
3.9(ii) The packet-normalized log-volumes are transferred to mono-analytic D⊢ packets by applying the relevant p-adic or radial log-volumes to log-shells and adjusting for the holomorphic versus mono-analytic integral-structure discrepancy. The same construction is available for F⊢×mu. A source-faithful interface must store the integral-structure discrepancy, not only the final real number.
3.9(iii) The global log-volume is obtained by summing local log-volumes over v_Q, with all but finitely many terms zero. It is invariant under multiplication by the labeled global field and under permutations of packet labels; on fractional-ideal objects it agrees with the arithmetic line-bundle degree up to normalization. This connects the packet/log-shell numerical layer to the global Frobenioid degree layer.
3.9(iv) In an LGP-Gaussian log-theta lattice, these log-volumes apply to the local/global LGP and lgp monoids/Frobenioids relative to the log-link n,m-1 -> n,m, and are compatible along that log-link at the level of rational log-shell spans. This is the numerical compatibility needed later when 3.5's log-Kummer correspondence is used in Theorem 3.11.
Remark 3.9.1 The discrepancy between holomorphic and mono-analytic integral structures is computable from the initial theta data: p-adically via p_v^*-scaled logarithmic unit lattices, and archimedeanly via Hermitian metric comparisons such as C tensor_R C -> C plus C. The discrepancy is not an unspecified error term; it is source-level data that should eventually be formalized.
Remark 3.9.3 General weighted averages over capsules are disallowed because the same log-shell label can contribute to different capsule index sets. Equal capsule weights are forced by compatibility with procession inclusions. This prevents changing the final numerical estimate by choosing convenient capsule weights.
Remark 3.9.4 Log-link/log-volume compatibility can be formulated using realified semi-simplifications of measure spaces. If a log-link only preserved volumes up to a nontrivial dilation factor, log-volumes would be defined only modulo Z log(lambda), which is unacceptable for the later real-valued estimates. The compatibility requirement is not cosmetic; it rules out circular or periodic ambiguity in the Corollary 3.12 numerical comparison.

Packet normalization

Proposition 3.9(i) defines local packet log-volumes with integral structures normalized to zero. The nonarchimedean sign convention is multiplication by p_v adding -log(p_v).

3.9(i)local packets

Mono-analytic transfer

The same log-volume layer is transported to D⊢ and F⊢×mu log-shell packet objects after correcting the holomorphic versus mono-analytic integral-structure discrepancy.

3.9(ii)log-shells

Global degree link

The global packet log-volume is a finite-support sum over v_Q. For fractional-ideal objects, it recovers the arithmetic line-bundle degree, up to the normalization specified in the source.

3.9(iii)degree

Forced procession average

Remark 3.9.3 gives a specific no-go statement: unequal capsule weights would conflict with the way the same log-shell label appears in different procession capsules.

Remark 3.9.3no weighted choice

Realified semi-simplification

Remark 3.9.4 packages measure spaces into commutative monoids with R_>0-action and then into R_>=0. This is the formal layer used to compare log-volumes across log-links.

Remark 3.9.4Rss

No log-volume period

If log-links introduced a nontrivial volume dilation lambda, the numbers would live in R / Z log(lambda). The source rejects this because Corollary 3.12 requires real-valued estimates.

Remark 3.9.4(ii)real-valued
Formalization consequence. The 3.9 interface should not be just a function from packet regions to real numbers. It should include the local place type, packet normalization, procession averaging rule, holomorphic/mono-analytic discrepancy data, finite-support global summation, and log-link compatibility evidence.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.9 and Remarks 3.9.1-3.9.4 Related: tensor packets LGP/lgp lattice packet log-volume card procession average card Corollary 3.12 corridor

IUT III Proposition 3.9(i): Packet Normalization

Proposition 3.9(i) defines local holomorphic packet log-volumes on packet fields and normalizes each local holomorphic integral structure to have log-volume zero. At nonarchimedean places, multiplication by p_v adds -log(p_v). At archimedean places, multiplication by e adds 1. Packet log-volumes are invariant under permutations of the packet label set A.

Place typeNormalizationIntegral structure
NonarchimedeanMultiplication by p_v adds -log(p_v).Local holomorphic integral structures have log-volume zero.
ArchimedeanMultiplication by e adds 1.Products of complex integral structures have log-volume zero.
ProcessionsAverage over capsules.Defines procession-normalized log-volumes.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.9(i) Related: packet integral structures forced procession average packet log-volume overview

IUT III Proposition 3.9(ii): Mono-Analytic Transfer

Proposition 3.9(ii) transfers the log-volume layer to mono-analytic packet objects by applying the relevant local log-volumes to the log-shells I(D⊢_v), then adjusting for the discrepancy between local holomorphic integral structures and mono-analytic integral structures. The resulting log-volumes are compatible with Proposition 3.2 poly-isomorphisms and have an analogous F⊢×μ version.

SideConstructionCompatibility
D⊢Apply local log-volume to mono-analytic log-shells and adjust discrepancy.Compatible with holomorphic packet log-volumes via Proposition 3.2.
ProcessionsAverage packet-normalized mono-analytic log-volumes.Compatible with holomorphic procession-normalized log-volumes.
F⊢×μReplace D⊢ by F⊢×μ.Compatible via the natural poly-isomorphisms.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.9(ii) and Remark 3.9.1 Related: mono-analytic packets mono-analytic log-shells global degree log-volume

IUT III Proposition 3.9(iii): Global Degree Log-Volume

Proposition 3.9(iii) forms a global log-volume by summing the local packet log-volumes over v_Q∈V_Q, restricted to elements whose components have zero log-volume for all but finitely many v_Q. For fractional-ideal objects of (Fmod)_α, this global log-volume recovers the degree of the associated arithmetic line bundle, with the source normalization.

FeatureSource statementUse
Finite supportOnly finitely many local components have nonzero log-volume.Makes the global sum defined.
InvarianceInvariant under multiplication by (Mmod)_α=(MMOD)_α and permutations of A.Stabilizes the global packet measure under source symmetries.
Degree linkFor fractional-ideal objects, log-volume equals arithmetic line-bundle degree.Connects packet regions to the Frobenioid degree layer.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.9(iii) Related: single packet global Frobenioids bad-prime global objects theta/q pilot objects

IUT III Remark 3.9.3: Forced Procession Average

Remark 3.9.3 says general weighted averages over procession capsules are not available. A log-shell label j' may contribute to multiple capsule index sets S_{j_1+1}^± and S_{j_2+1}^±. Compatibility with the procession inclusion morphisms forces equal weights for the relevant capsules.

No tuning parameter. The procession average cannot be adjusted to obtain a more convenient numerical estimate. Equal capsule weights are a source constraint.
Potential variationSource answerReason
Arbitrary weighted capsule averageNo.The same log-shell label occurs in different procession capsules.
Equal averageForced.Needed for compatibility with inclusion morphisms.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.3 Related: packet normalization procession normalization Corollary 3.12 corridor

IUT III Remark 3.9.4: Realified Semi-Simplification

Remark 3.9.4 formulates log-link/log-volume compatibility through a realified semi-simplification of measure spaces. From a measure space (M, μ_M), finite-valued nonnegative measurable functions with relatively compact support form a monoid with R_{>0}-action. Integration produces a quotient Rss(M) naturally isomorphic to R_{≥0}.

ObjectDefinition roleCompatibility role
Sub(M)Pre-ample relatively compact Borel subsets.Characteristic functions embed into Fn(M).
Fn(M)Finite-valued nonnegative measurable functions with compact positive support.Monoid with R_{>0}-action.
Rss(M)Quotient determined by integration.Realified semi-simplification used to compare volumes.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.4(i) Related: no log-volume period log-shells warning packet log-volume overview

IUT III Remark 3.9.4: No Log-Volume Period

Remark 3.9.4 explains why log-link/log-volume compatibility cannot allow a nontrivial dilation ambiguity. If log-links preserved volumes only up to a factor λ, log-volumes would be defined only modulo Z log(λ). The later Corollary 3.12 comparison requires ordinary real-valued estimates, so this periodic ambiguity is excluded.

No Period Ambiguity Corollary 3.12 needs real-valued log-volume estimates
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.4 Related: realified semi-simplification Corollary 3.12 corridor IUT IV theta-pilot estimate

IUT III Remark 3.9.5: Holomorphic Hull Formalism

Remark 3.9.5 introduces holomorphic hulls for regions in the packet fields of Proposition 3.9. For a relatively compact region, the hull is the smallest region of the form λ · O, with nonzero components of λ, that contains the region. For a non-relatively-compact region, the hull is the full ambient IQ((-)). The resulting operation is a source-level bridge from arbitrary direct-product regions to unit-stable hulls.

Region P

A direct product region in IQ((-)), possibly not stabilized by local units.

Hull φ(P)

The smallest containing region of the form λ · O((-)), or the whole ambient field if compactness fails.

Preorder control

The hull map preserves inclusions and therefore supports later upper-semi log-volume estimates.

Source itemPaper contentWiki consequence
Remark 3.9.5(i) The hull of a relatively compact subset of IQ(αF_{v_Q}), IQ(AF_{v_Q}), or IQ(A,αF_v) is the smallest λ · O region containing it; otherwise the hull is the entire ambient direct-sum field. The hull is not merely a numerical enlargement. It is a new region with arithmetic holomorphic form.
Remark 3.9.5(ii) Let P be the set of direct product regions and H the set of hulls. Then H ⊆ P, and hull formation gives φ : P -> H. The wiki keeps direct-product regions and hulls as separate concept pages because later comparison uses their difference.
Properties P1-P3 φ(H)=H for hulls, P ⊆ φ(P), and P_1 ⊆ P_2 => φ(P_1) ⊆ φ(P_2). Equivalently, φ(P) is the intersection of all hulls containing P. These are the formal constraints any Lean hull interface must expose before it can be used in the 3.12 inequality corridor.
Formalization consequence. A source-faithful model should represent P, H, and φ with the P1-P3 laws. Treating the hull as only a real-valued bound loses the preorder structure used by the paper.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.5(i),(ii) Related: packet log-volume hull approximants hull/determinant closed loop Corollary 3.12 corridor

IUT III Remark 3.9.5: Hull Approximants

After defining the hull map, Remark 3.9.5 defines log-volume hull approximants. For a region P, the sets Φ(P) and Ξ(P) collect hulls contained in φ(P) whose log-volume lies between μlog(P) and μlog(φ(P)), with equality to μlog(P) in the Ξ case. This is an indeterminacy of hull choices, but the paper stresses that the indeterminacy is compact because all choices remain inside φ(P).

ObjectPaper contentAudit consequence
Φ(P) Hulls H ⊆ φ(P) with μlog(φ(P)) ≥ μlog(H) ≥ μlog(P). The containment H ⊆ φ(P) is essential; omitting it destroys compactness of the choice set.
Ξ(P) Hulls H ⊆ φ(P) with μlog(H)=μlog(P). The paper records useful nonempty cases, but does not assert nonemptiness in complete generality.
HPhi(P) The union over Φ(P) satisfies HPhi(P)=φ(P). The final hull can be recovered from the approximant family.
Strictness example The source gives a Q_p x Q_p example where μ(P) is strictly smaller than the volume of the union of exact-volume approximants. Formal pages must not silently replace a possible region by its hull without recording that the log-volume can increase.
No exact-approximant shortcut. A model that assumes Ξ(P) is always nonempty, or that μlog(P)=μlog(φ(P)) generally, is stronger than the paper and would distort the Corollary 3.12 audit.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.5(iii),(iv) Related: holomorphic hull formalism nabla hull formalism hull/determinant closed loop

IUT III Remark 3.9.5: Nabla Hull Formalism

Remark 3.9.5(v),(vi) introduces an abstract set-theoretic quotient formalism, denoted here by the paper's nabla-style operation. If S ⊆ E, passing to the quotient that identifies all elements of S makes maps between nonempty subsets of S become identity maps on the image of S. Mochizuki presents this as an abstract formulation of upper semi-commutativity/semi-compatibility.

Subset S in E

All elements of S are identified in the quotient.

Maps inside S

Maps between nonempty subsets of S descend to the identity on the quotient image.

Hull application

Take S=φ(P) or a bounded-family hull φ(P_B).

Indeterminacy removed

Possible regions and their hull approximants become identified at the quotient level.

Source itemPaper contentFormalization consequence
Set quotient For a proper subset S of a set E, identify all elements of S and leave E - S unaffected. The quotient is a controlled collapse of a specified subset, not a global collapse of all regions.
Formal empty sets Formal empty intersections inside S may also map to the quotient image of S. This records the role of domains of partial logarithm maps in upper-semi diagrams.
Apply to hulls Taking S=φ(P) identifies P with its hull approximants in Φ(P) or Ξ(P). This is the formal mechanism by which the hull-choice indeterminacy is suppressed for later comparison.
Bounded families For bounded possible-region families PB={Pβ}, use φ(P_B), the intersection of all hulls containing every Pβ. The Corollary 3.12 route must work with the family of possible outputs, not a secretly selected output region.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.5(v),(vi) Related: upper semi-compatibility hull approximants 3.12 diagnostics

IUT III Remark 3.9.5: Hull, Determinant, and Closed Loop

Remark 3.9.5(vii)-(ix) explains why hulls are not decorative. The possible images produced by Theorem 3.11 cannot be directly compared to the q-pilot arithmetic line-bundle objects in the codomain F×mu-prime-strip. The paper passes from possible regions to hulls, then to determinant/tensor-power line-bundle data, then through log-Kummer vertical-shift adjustment, and only then to log-volume.

Possible regions

Theorem 3.11 outputs regions in tensor packets of log-shells.

Hulls

Passing to φ(P_B) gives unit-stable arithmetic vector-bundle regions.

det⊗M

Determinants and positive tensor powers produce arithmetic line-bundle objects comparable to q-pilot data.

Log-Kummer shift

Vertical shifts are adjusted by log-Kummer correspondences, with the expected indeterminacy.

Log-volume

Only after the comparable-object stage does the realified log-volume quotient give the numerical comparison.

OperationPaper contentWhy it matters
(Ob1)-(Ob2) Arbitrary log-shell regions need not be stabilized by local units, while q-pilot line-bundle data is only generated up to unit indeterminacy. Hulls give unit-stable regions that can be read as local pieces of arithmetic vector bundles. The hull step is the first source bridge from possible-image regions to objects compatible with arithmetic line-bundle comparison.
(Ob3)-(Ob4) Determinants, weighted positive tensor powers, and normalization by the structure sheaf convert hull vector-bundle data into arithmetic line-bundle objects; tensor-power twists do not affect later log-volume computations. A 3.12 model should distinguish region, hull, determinant/tensor, and final numerical layers.
(Ob5)-(Ob6) Independence from choosing one possible output is obtained by using the bounded-family hull φ(P_B) and the quotient formalism. Exact-volume approximants may be unavailable, so Φ is more robust than Ξ. Formal interfaces must prevent selecting a convenient possible region as if it were canonical.
(Ob7)-(Ob9) Local Galois groups and unit portions cannot be removed from F×mu-prime-strips. Log-Kummer correspondences adjust vertical shifts; realified semi-simplification then gives a log-volume bijection for hulls across the log-link. The loop closes only if comparable objects are built before the log-volume quotient forgets Galois/unit data.
(sQ3),(sQ4),(sQ5) Hull/determinant/quotient, log-Kummer vertical-shift adjustment, and log-volume are all subquotient operations. The paper emphasizes the qualitative difference: (sQ3),(sQ4) preserve arithmetic holomorphic compatibility, while (sQ5) forgets it. Skipping directly to log-volume breaks the closed loop and leaves no nontrivial 3.12 conclusion according to the source.
3.12 guardrail. Do not formalize the disputed passage as "possible region -> real number" alone. The source route is possible regions -> hulls -> determinant/tensor line-bundle objects -> log-Kummer vertical adjustment -> log-volume.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.5(vii)-(ix) Related: Theorem 3.11 algorithms Corollary 3.12 proof steps product formula compatibility no region-category shortcut

IUT III Remark 3.9.5: No Region-Category Shortcut

Remark 3.9.5(x) rejects a natural shortcut: replacing the local and global Frobenioids of arithmetic line bundles by more general categories of regions. Such categories would have to use the additive rank-greater-than-one module structure of IQ((-)), while the Theta×mu_LGP-link is not compatible with the additive structures in its domain and codomain. The glued categories must remain purely multiplicative at the relevant stage.

Candidate shortcutSource obstructionAudit consequence
Use categories of arbitrary regions Regions live in a multidimensional additive module; comparing lines inside it naturally invokes addition. This conflicts with the multiplicative nature of the Theta×mu_LGP gluing.
Use higher-rank arithmetic vector bundles directly Rank-greater-than-one vector-bundle data is not the purely multiplicative line-bundle data used in the Frobenioid prime-strip. The determinant step is not optional bookkeeping; it restores the line bundle level.
Norm/determinant down to Q or Q_p The relevant decomposition group need not split in a way that makes such a norm operation Galois-equivariant on algebraic closures. This would be incompatible with the local Galois action central to log-Kummer and log-shell theory.
Formalization consequence. The wiki should not replace the hull/determinant/Frobenioid route with a convenient category of measured regions. That would model a different comparison than the one used by the source.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.5(x) Related: hull/determinant closed loop log-shells alone not enough Theta×mu_LGP link

IUT III Remark 3.9.6: Product Formula Compatibility

Remark 3.9.6 clarifies the scope of log-link/log-volume compatibility. The compatibility is not asserted for arbitrary regions in the domain and codomain of the log-link. It concerns certain sufficiently small regions. This is still enough for product formulas, because the ratios of conversion between log-volumes at distinct valuations may be formulated using such sufficiently small regions.

PointPaper contentUse in the wiki
Scope Log-link compatibility applies to certain sufficiently small regions, not arbitrary regions. Prevents overstating Proposition 3.9(iv) as a universal region equality.
Product formula Ratios of conversion between log-volumes at distinct places can be expressed using sufficiently small regions. Connects local log-link compatibility to global arithmetic degree comparison.
Global degrees Proposition 3.9(iv) implies compatibility between global arithmetic degrees in the log-link domain and codomain. Explains why the local compatibility still feeds into Corollary 3.12's global numerical estimate.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.6 Related: realified semi-simplification global degree log-volume closed-loop hull passage

IUT III Remark 3.9.7: General-Region Log-Volumes

Remark 3.9.7 records elementary extensions of the log-volume formalism. The finite-support condition in the global product can be satisfied by taking the standard integral regions at all but finitely many unramified nonarchimedean places; these standard regions have zero log-volume, are mono-analytic invariants, and equal their own holomorphic hulls. More general relatively compact subsets are handled by infima over finite-log-volume covering regions, equivalently by inverse systems.

CasePaper contentFormalization consequence
Almost all places At all but finitely many unramified nonarchimedean places, one may use I((-)) ⊆ IQ((-)); it has zero log-volume, is mono-analytic invariant, and equals its own hull. Global products need a finite-support condition, but the source provides a canonical almost-everywhere zero-volume choice.
Single local region For a relatively compact subset E, define its log-volume as the infimum of log-volumes of finite-log-volume regions E* containing E; allow -∞. A formal definition must permit extended values or use inverse systems of finite-log-volume approximants.
Direct product pre-regions For direct product pre-regions, use inverse systems of direct product regions covering the pre-region. The direct-product shape is preserved in the approximating system.
Global collections For collections {E_{v_Q}}, define log-volume by the infimum over covering global collections satisfying the finite support condition, or equivalently by inverse systems of such collections. The global layer should not be modeled only for exact product regions; it needs the infimum/inverse-system extension.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.9.7 Related: packet normalization holomorphic hull formalism IUT IV normalized log-volume

Global Kummer Theory and Non-Interference in IUT III 3.10

Proposition 3.10 globalizes the log-Kummer correspondence from local LGP monoids to number fields, Frobenioids, and F-prime-strips. Its central non-interference statement says that global multiplicative elements that lie in all local packet integer monoids are exactly torsion elements, i.e. roots of unity. The following remarks explain why this gives precise compatibility for FMOD/LGP but only upper-semi-compatible behavior for the additive Fmod/lgp side.

Proposition 3.10 vertical global objects, roots-of-unity non-interference, and FMOD/Fmod bias
Source itemConstruction or statementAudit consequence
Proposition 3.10 setup Work in an LGP-Gaussian log-theta lattice and, for each column n, use vertical coricity to obtain n,◦HT_D-Theta±ellNF. Global comparisons are made against a columnwise vertically coric target, not against a single ordinary theater selected ad hoc.
Proposition 3.10(i) Applying the global Frobenioid constructions of Proposition 3.7 to the F(n,◦D≻)_t prime-strips constructs vertically coric number fields, monoids, Fmod/FMOD Frobenioids, and Gaussian/LGP/lgp F-prime-strips. The 3.7 global packet objects now exist in a vertically coric form suitable for comparison across all horizontal indices.
Proposition 3.10(i), Kummer displays For every n,m, Kummer isomorphisms compare the ordinary global fields, monoids, Frobenioids, and F-prime-strips in n,m with the vertically coric n,◦ target. These are the global counterparts of the local Kummer comparisons of Proposition 3.5.
Proposition 3.10(ii) The intersection of the global multiplicative group MMOD_alpha with the product of local packet integer monoids Psi_log(A,alpha F_v) is the finite torsion subgroup M_mu_MOD, i.e. roots of unity. This is the non-interference fact: any overlap between global multiplicative action and all local integer constraints is harmless torsion.
Proposition 3.10(ii), final paragraph The global groups act on the vertically coric IQ(S_{j+1}^±F(n,◦D≻)_{V_Q}) not by one Kummer isomorphism but via all Kummer pre-composites with log-link iterates, producing a global log-Kummer correspondence. The totality principle from Proposition 3.5 remains mandatory at the global number-field level.
Proposition 3.10(iii) The global log-Kummer correspondence induces log-link-compatible isomorphisms of FMOD Frobenioids and realified FMOD Frobenioids, and also compatible isomorphisms of associated F⊥-prime-strips. This is the precise Kummer-detachment side used later in Theorem 3.11.
Remark 3.10.1(i) The precise Frobenioid isomorphism works for FMOD because its construction uses the global multiplicative group and valuation subquotients. It does not work for Fmod because Fmod also depends on local packet monoids subject to upper-semi compatibility. Do not state a log-link-compatible global Frobenioid isomorphism for Fmod where the source only gives one for FMOD.
Remark 3.10.1(ii),(iii) FMOD/LGP is rigid and suited to Kummer-detachment indeterminacies. Fmod/lgp is distorted by upper-semi and holomorphic/mono-analytic discrepancies, but its additive log-shell relation makes it suited to explicit log-volume estimates. The two sides have different proof roles; merging them would lose the reason for using LGP in the main lattice while still consulting lgp/Fmod for estimates.
Remark 3.10.2 The F×mu-prime-strips used in the Theta×mu_LGP and Theta×mu_lgp links are constructed from the environmental prime-strip; lower horizontal arrows are obtained by conjugating upper etale-like arrows by Kummer isomorphisms. Horizontal LGP/lgp arrows are not primitive labels; they are built from environmental data and Kummer conjugation.

Vertically coric global objects

Proposition 3.10(i) constructs vertically coric versions of the global fields, monoids, Frobenioids, and LGP/lgp prime-strips introduced in Proposition 3.7.

Open the vertically coric global objects page

3.10(i)global

Roots-of-unity non-interference

The intersection statement in 3.10(ii) reduces simultaneous global/local interference to torsion. This is why adjacent log-link ambiguity is described as harmless identity indeterminacy.

Open the non-interference page

3.10(ii)torsion

Global totality

As in 3.5, the action is not through a single Kummer isomorphism. It uses the totality of Kummer maps pre-composed with vertical log-link iterates.

Open the global totality page

3.10(ii)totality

FMOD/Fmod bias

FMOD is multiplicative and admits precise log-Kummer compatibility; Fmod is additive and estimate-friendly but only upper-semi-compatible at the relevant local monoids.

Open the FMOD/Fmod bias page

Remark 3.10.1Fig. 3.2

Kummer detachment

Remark 3.10.1 says the precise FMOD/LGP compatibility is what makes Kummer-detachment indeterminacies disappear for the global portion of the Theta×mu_LGP link.

Open the Kummer-detachment page

Remark 3.10.1(ii)LGP

Kummer-conjugated arrows

Remark 3.10.2 reviews the commutative diagram in which the lower horizontal LGP/lgp arrows are obtained by conjugating corresponding upper etale-like arrows by the relevant Kummer isomorphisms.

Open the conjugated-arrows page

Remark 3.10.2F×mu
Audit rule. Do not upgrade the Fmod side to the precise log-link-compatible behavior of FMOD. The paper explicitly separates the two: FMOD/LGP is rigid and Kummer-detachment-friendly, while Fmod/lgp is distorted but suited to log-volume estimates.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.10 and Remarks 3.10.1-3.10.2 Related: packet log-volume global packet Frobenioids LGP/lgp lattice global Kummer card FMOD/Fmod bias card Theorem 3.11

IUT III Proposition 3.10(i): Vertically Coric Global Objects

Proposition 3.10(i) applies the global Frobenioid constructions of Proposition 3.7 to the columnwise vertically coric D-Theta±ellNF theater n,◦HT_D-Theta±ellNF. The output is a family of fields, monoids, Frobenioids, and Gaussian/LGP/lgp prime-strips that are vertically coric in the fixed column n.

3.10(i) global 3.7 packet objects rebuilt over the columnwise vertical target
InputConstructionConstraint
n,mHT_{Theta±ellNF} for all m Vertical coricity of Theorem 1.5(i) determines n,◦HT_D. The construction is columnwise; it is not a global collapse of all horizontal indices.
Proposition 3.7 global packet objects Rebuild fields, monoids, Fmod/FMOD, and prime-strips over n,◦. The source lists both uppercase and lowercase variants because they have different roles later.
Kummer displays For each n,m, ordinary objects map to the vertically coric n,◦ target. The displays are compatibility data for later log-Kummer correspondences.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.10(i) Related: 3.10 overview Proposition 3.7 global objects Proposition 3.5 vertical target

IUT III Proposition 3.10(ii): Roots-of-Unity Non-Interference

Proposition 3.10(ii) identifies the overlap between the global multiplicative group MMOD_alpha and all local packet integer monoids. The intersection is exactly the finite torsion subgroup, i.e. roots of unity. This is the paper's global non-interference statement for local integers.

PieceSource meaningWhy it matters
Global side (dagger MMOD)_alpha is the global group of nonzero elements of the number field. It is the group that acts in the global Kummer comparison.
Local integer side The product of Psi_log(A,alpha F_v) gives the local packet integer constraints. These are the local regions in which unwanted global action could interfere.
Intersection The simultaneous global/local overlap is (dagger M_MOD^mu)_alpha. The proof cites the classical fact that global elements integral at all places are roots of unity.
Adjacent ambiguity Only roots of unity survive at adjacent horizontal indices. These become harmless identity indeterminacies in the log-Kummer action.
The statement is not that all local ambiguity vanishes. It is that the global multiplicative part cannot nontrivially interfere with all local integer constraints except through torsion.
Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.10(ii) Related: global log-Kummer totality root-of-unity zero effect in 3.5

IUT III Proposition 3.10(ii): Global Log-Kummer Totality

The final paragraph of Proposition 3.10(ii) repeats the discipline already visible in Proposition 3.5: the action is not through one chosen Kummer isomorphism. The groups labelled n,m act through the totality of Kummer maps pre-composed with iterates of the log-links, on n,◦-labelled IQ containers.

Totality principle all horizontal labels and log-link iterates are part of the action

For formalization and exposition, this page is an anti-shortcut rule. A model that selects one representative Kummer map may accidentally erase exactly the horizontal/log-link dependence that Proposition 3.10(ii) keeps visible.

Sources: IUT III, Proposition 3.10(ii), final paragraph Related: single Kummer map not enough in 3.5 roots-of-unity non-interference

IUT III Remark 3.10.1: FMOD/Fmod Bias

Remark 3.10.1 separates two roles that must not be conflated. FMOD/LGP is multiplicative, rigid, and compatible with precise log-Kummer Frobenioid isomorphisms. Fmod/lgp is additive and log-shell-adjacent, hence suited to explicit estimates, but only after upper-semi and holomorphic/mono-analytic distortions are accounted for.

StructureBiasAllowed use
FMOD/LGP Multiplicative; constructed from the global group and valuation subquotients. Precise log-link-compatible Frobenioid isomorphisms; Kummer-detachment analysis.
Fmod/lgp Additive; depends essentially on local packet monoids inside logarithmic modules. Explicit estimate computation through log-shells and log-volumes.
Upper-semi compatibility One-sided local inclusions rather than equalities. Permits estimates, but blocks a precise Fmod Frobenioid isomorphism parallel to FMOD.
A source-faithful wiki or Lean model must not give Fmod the exact log-Kummer compatibility asserted for FMOD. The paper explicitly says this is unavailable.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.10.1 and Fig. 3.2 Related: Kummer detachment global degree log-volume LGP versus lgp

IUT III Remark 3.10.1(ii): Kummer-Detachment Role

The precise compatibility of the FMOD/LGP side is used to control Kummer-detachment indeterminacies when moving from the Frobenius-like global portion of the domain of a Theta×mu_LGP link to the corresponding etale-like structures.

Two-role split rigidity for detachment, distortion-tolerant additivity for estimates

This explains why the LGP-Gaussian log-theta lattice is preferred for the main global Kummer-detachment route, even though the additive lgp side remains necessary for concrete log-volume estimates.

Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.10.1(ii)-(iii) Related: FMOD/Fmod bias packet log-volume Corollary 3.12 proof steps

IUT III Remark 3.10.2: Kummer-Conjugated Horizontal Arrows

Remark 3.10.2 reviews the F×mu-prime-strip diagram behind the Theta×mu_LGP and Theta×mu_lgp links. The lower horizontal environmental-to-Gaussian-to-LGP/lgp arrows are obtained by conjugating the corresponding upper etale-like arrows by the relevant Kummer isomorphisms.

Remark 3.10.2 lower horizontal arrows are built by Kummer conjugation

The diagram matters because the horizontal links used by the Theta×mu construction are not free-standing labels: their lower-row construction is tied to the upper-row etale-like arrows and the vertical Kummer isomorphisms.

Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.10.2 Related: Theta×mu_LGP link Theta×mu_lgp link global log-Kummer totality

Theorem 3.11: Multiradial Algorithms via LGP-Monoids/Frobenioids

Theorem 3.11 is the main construction theorem of IUT III. It starts from initial theta data and an LGP-Gaussian log-theta lattice, fixes the vertically coric D-theater n,◦HT_D-Theta±ellNF for each column, constructs the multiradial representation n,◦R_LGP, and then records how this representation is related to every n,m Hodge theater by log-Kummer correspondences and horizontal Theta×mu_LGP-link compatibility.

Theorem 3.11 vertical coric input, multiradial output, log-Kummer comparison, and horizontal compatibility
Source itemConstruction or statementWhat the wiki must preserve
Theorem 3.11 setup Fix initial theta data and a family of distinct Theta±ellNF-Hodge theaters n,mHT. For each column n, use vertical coricity to obtain n,◦HT_D-Theta±ellNF. The theorem is columnwise: the vertically coric object is determined only up to isomorphism from all theaters in that column.
Theorem 3.11(i), procession Apply the natural functor of IUT I Proposition 6.9(ii) to obtain the procession of D⊢-prime-strips Prc(n,◦D⊢_T). The multiradial representation is built from the procession, not from a free list of labels.
Theorem 3.11(i)(a) Use the mono-analytic tensor packets and Q-spans of Proposition 3.2(ii), equipped with the procession-normalized mono-analytic log-volumes of Proposition 3.9(ii). The local log-shell packet layer and its log-volume are part of the same theorem interface.
Theorem 3.11(i)(b) At bad primes, include the LGP splitting monoid of Proposition 3.5(ii)(c) as a subset of the relevant packet Q-spans and with its multiplicative action on the product of those packet modules. Bad-prime splitting monoids are active operators on the packet containers, not merely labels attached to them.
Theorem 3.11(i)(c) For each j∈F_l, include the global number field/monoid data and the associated FMOD/Fmod and realified Frobenioids whose global degrees are computed by the log-volumes from (a). The global Frobenioid layer is tied back to the packet log-volume layer.
Ind1 and Ind2 The representation n,◦R_LGP is taken up to automorphisms of the D⊢-prime-strip procession and independent local automorphism actions on tensor-product summands. These indeterminacies act before the log-Kummer comparison with ordinary n,m theaters.
Theorem 3.11(i), final paragraph n,◦R_LGP is constructed by an algorithm functorial for isomorphisms of processions, and etale-picture permutation symmetries induce compatible poly-isomorphisms between columns. The construction is algorithmic and functorial; cross-column comparison uses etale-picture symmetries.
Theorem 3.11(ii)(a) Kummer isomorphisms compare the vertically coric local mono-analytic tensor packets and Q-spans with the corresponding local packets in each n,m Hodge theater, compatibly with log-volumes. This is the local packet comparison; it is the place where Ind3 later appears.
Theorem 3.11(ii)(b),(c) The same log-Kummer framework compares bad-prime LGP splitting monoids, number fields, global FMOD/Fmod Frobenioids, and realified Frobenioids. The comparison has three layers: local packets, bad-prime splitting monoids, and global Frobenioids.
Theorem 3.11(ii), final paragraph As m varies, splitting monoids and MOD-subscripted global Frobenioids are mutually compatible up to roots of unity; the local packet isomorphisms are only upper-semi-compatible, by inclusions at nonarchimedean places and surjections at archimedean places. This is the precise split between harmless torsion, exact MOD behavior, and Ind3 upper-semi packet behavior.
Theorem 3.11(iii) Kummer isomorphisms satisfy compatibility with the horizontal Theta×mu_LGP links: for F⊢×mu prime-strips, environment prime-strips, mono-theta projective systems, poly-isomorphisms, and evaluation maps. Horizontal compatibility is stated as stabilization/equivariance/ functoriality under arbitrary automorphisms of the horizontal-arrow domains and codomains, modulo the named indeterminacies where applicable.

Data (a),(b),(c)

The theorem's multiradial representation has three visible layers: local mono-analytic tensor packets with log-volumes, bad-prime LGP splitting monoids acting on packet modules, and global number-field/ Frobenioid data with degrees measured by the packet log-volumes.

Open the 3.11 data page

3.11(i)source interface

Ind1, Ind2, Ind3

Ind1 comes from automorphisms of the procession. Ind2 comes from independent automorphism actions on tensor-product summands. Ind3 is not another quotient of global Frobenioids; it is the upper-semi compatibility of local packet Kummer isomorphisms as the horizontal index varies.

Open the indeterminacy page

3.11(i),(ii)indeterminacy

MOD versus mod

Theorem 3.11(ii) explicitly transfers exact mutual compatibility from roots-of-unity non-interference to the MOD global Frobenioids, while warning that this does not apply in the same way to mod-subscripted isomorphisms.

Open the MOD/mod page

3.11(ii)FMOD/Fmod

Horizontal compatibility

Part (iii) says that the Kummer constructions, projective systems, and evaluation maps are compatible with horizontal Theta×mu_LGP arrows in the stabilized, equivariant, functorial sense specified by the source.

Open the horizontal compatibility page

3.11(iii)horizontal arrows

IPL, SHE, APT

Remark 3.11.1 recasts the theorem as an algorithm whose input is an F×mu-prime-strip, whose output is linked to that input by full poly-isomorphisms, and whose construction is simultaneously executable relative to the domain and codomain holomorphic structures. Its transport is algorithmic, not a direct map of set-theoretic regions.

Open the IPL/SHE/APT source page

Remark 3.11.1IPL/SHE/APT

Label discipline

Remark 3.11.2 emphasizes that the label transitions require all labels in the relevant F_l and |F_l| systems. The procession index sets used in the multiradial representation are naked finite sets; they are not arbitrary omissions of arithmetic labels.

Open the label discipline page

Remark 3.11.2labels
The source theorem does not identify adjacent Hodge theaters by a shared ring structure. Its comparison route is: vertically coric target, multiradial representation modulo Ind1/Ind2, log-Kummer correspondence with Ind3 for local packets, and horizontal link compatibility in the stabilized/equivariant/ functorial sense.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 3.11 and Remarks 3.11.1-3.11.4 Related: global Kummer 3.10 multiradiality overview tensor/log-volume corridor 3.11 representation card 3.11 log-Kummer card Theta-link compatibility card Corollary 3.12

IUT III Theorem 3.11(i): Data (a), (b), (c)

The multiradial representation n,◦R_LGP is the collection of three visible data layers, taken modulo Ind1 and Ind2. These layers tie local mono-analytic tensor packets, bad-prime LGP splitting monoids, and global Frobenioid degree data to the same vertically coric column.

LayerSource contentWhy it matters
Data (a) Local mono-analytic tensor packets I(S_{j+1}^{±}; n,◦D⊢_{v_Q}) and Q-spans, including labelled local variants, equipped with procession-normalized log-volumes from Proposition 3.9(ii). This is the log-shell packet container layer in which local possible images and their log-volumes are read.
Data (b) For v∈V_bad, the LGP splitting monoid Psi^⊥_{LGP}(n,◦HT_D)_v, regarded through the Proposition 3.2 poly-isomorphisms as a subset of packet Q-spans and acting multiplicatively on packet modules. The bad-prime splitting monoid is an operator on the packet container, not a passive label.
Data (c) Global number fields/monoids and non-realified/realified FMOD/Fmod Frobenioids, with global degrees computable by the log-volumes from data (a). This is where global arithmetic line-bundle data is made comparable with local packet log-volumes.
The source statement binds the three layers together. A wiki or Lean model should not split them into unrelated packet, monoid, and Frobenioid oracles.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 3.11(i)(a)-(c) Related: Theorem 3.11 overview packet log-volume 3.9 global Kummer 3.10

IUT III Theorem 3.11: Ind1, Ind2, Ind3

Theorem 3.11 names three indeterminacy mechanisms. Ind1 and Ind2 are part of the multiradial representation of Theorem 3.11(i). Ind3 appears in Theorem 3.11(ii), where local packet Kummer isomorphisms vary along the horizontal index and are only upper-semi-compatible with log-links.

3.11 indeterminacies two quotienting mechanisms plus one upper-semi compatibility mechanism
NameSource locationCorrect reading
Ind1 Theorem 3.11(i), after data (a)-(c). Automorphisms of the procession of D⊢-prime-strips.
Ind2 Theorem 3.11(i), after data (a)-(c). Independent local automorphism actions on direct summands of tensor-product packets.
Ind3 Theorem 3.11(ii), after the log-Kummer comparison. Upper-semi compatibility for local packet isomorphisms: nonarchimedean inclusions and archimedean surjections, not exact equality.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 3.11(i)-(ii) Related: indeterminacies overview nonarch containment 3.5 arch ball containment 3.5

IUT III Theorem 3.11(ii): MOD versus mod

Theorem 3.11(ii) transfers exact mutual log-link compatibility to the MOD-subscripted global Frobenioid isomorphisms, but explicitly does not give the same exact compatibility for the mod-subscripted isomorphisms. This is the theorem version of the FMOD/Fmod asymmetry of Remark 3.10.1.

SideCompatibility statusReason
MMOD/FMOD/F^R_MOD Mutually compatible with log-links as m varies. Roots-of-unity non-interference makes adjacent ambiguity harmless.
Mmod/Fmod/F^R_mod Not granted the same exact mutual compatibility. The local monoid layer is subject to upper-semi compatibility and distortion.
Local packet data (a) Precisely compatible with log-volumes, but only upper-semi-compatible as Kummer data. This is the route from Ind3 to numerical upper estimates.
This page is an audit guardrail: exact compatibility may be used on the uppercase MOD route, but the lowercase mod route must remain estimate-oriented.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 3.11(ii) Related: FMOD/Fmod bias 3.10.1 roots-of-unity non-interference

IUT III Theorem 3.11(iii): Horizontal Link Compatibility

Part (iii) states compatibility of the Kummer constructions with the horizontal Theta×mu_LGP arrows. The compatibility is not a commutative-square simplification. It is formulated through F⊢×mu prime-strips, environmental prime-strips, mono-theta projective systems, evaluation maps, and stabilization under automorphisms of the horizontal-arrow domain and codomain.

PartObject comparedCompatibility form
3.11(iii)(a) F⊢×mu_△ prime-strips. Kummer isomorphisms commute with full horizontal poly-isomorphisms after applying the relevant symmetry.
3.11(iii)(b) Environmental F⊢×mu prime-strips. Natural isomorphisms to the environmental side preserve the horizontal comparison.
3.11(iii)(c) Mono-theta environments, n,◦R, and evaluation maps. Algorithmic constructions are stabilized/equivariant/functorial under arbitrary automorphisms, modulo Ind1/Ind2/Ind3 where stated.
3.11(iii)(d) Kummer maps and poly-isomorphisms involving M^∞κ data. The same horizontal compatibility condition is imposed on the global Kummer/evaluation route.
Sources: IUT III, Theorem 3.11(iii) Related: Kummer-conjugated arrows 3.10.2 Theta×mu_LGP link 3.8 3.12 proof steps

IUT III Remark 3.11.1: IPL, SHE, APT, HIS

Remark 3.11.1 recasts Theorem 3.11 as a functorial construction algorithm: input is an F×mu-prime-strip up to isomorphism; output is linked to that input by full poly-isomorphisms and is simultaneously expressible in the domain and codomain arithmetic holomorphic structures. The transport is algorithmic, not a direct map of regions.

NameSource meaningCorollary 3.12 relevance
IPL Output data is linked to the fixed input q-pilot prime-strip through full poly-isomorphisms of F×mu-prime-strips. The q-pilot remains the fixed input object for the later comparison.
SHE The construction and output are expressible relative to both theta-side and q-side arithmetic holomorphic structures. This is the simultaneous-executability claim used before the final qualitative comparison.
APT Parallel transport is an algorithm, not a set-theoretic function carrying one region to another. Possible output regions still require hull/log-volume processing before comparison.
HIS Theta functions and theta values are hidden internal structures for qualitative uses of the algorithm. IUT IV cannot ignore them, because explicit log-volume estimates depend on how theta values occur.
The source explicitly warns that possible regions produced by the algorithm cannot necessarily be directly compared with structures in the codomain of the theta link. This is why the later hull and log-volume corridor is essential.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.1 Related: multiradiality overview tensor/log-volume corridor Corollary 3.12 proof steps

IUT III Remark 3.11.1: Naive q-Power Model

Remark 3.11.1(vi) tests IPL and SHE against a deliberately naive model: inside one fixed arithmetic holomorphic structure, write a theta-like correspondence q -> qlambda for an integer lambda > 1. The construction of qlambda by taking the lambda-th power of q is simultaneously executable in that fixed ring context. Thus it has the analogue of SHE, but it does not identify q with qlambda as IPL would require.

FeatureNaive model behaviorSource lesson
Single ring structure Both q and qlambda are computed inside one arithmetic holomorphic structure R. This gives simultaneous executability by construction.
SHE analogue The algorithm "take the lambda-th power" is tautologically valid for both inputs in the fixed structure. The SHE-like part is easy only because no alien holomorphic structures are being compared.
IPL analogue The fixed container does not itself induce an identification between q and qlambda. SHE alone is not the source mechanism used by Theorem 3.11.
Audit point. A model that keeps a single ring history can make the power construction look harmless, but it has not reproduced the input prime-strip link property.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.1(vi) Related: IPL/SHE/APT source distinct-labels tradeoff Remark 3.12.2 intertwining

IUT III Remark 3.11.1: Distinct-Labels Tradeoff

Remark 3.11.1(vii) makes the q-power toy model diagnostic. To force an IPL-like simultaneous relationship, introduce a formal symbol standing for the abstract prime-strip and map it to two labelled realizations. With distinct labels, the IPL analogue is tautologically consistent, but the SHE analogue is not automatic. If the two labels are forcibly identified, SHE becomes tautological, but IPL degenerates into confusion between q and powers qlambda n.

Distinct labels

Use separate dagger-like labels for the structures producing q and qlambda.

IPL holds

The formal symbol can map to both labelled realizations without contradiction.

SHE unclear

The relationship between the two arithmetic holomorphic structures is not computed.

Forced identification

Deleting labels restores a single-ring power algorithm but loses the meaningful IPL relation.

ApproachWhat it gainsWhat it loses
Distinct labels Both assignments from the formal prime-strip symbol are simultaneous and consistent. There is no a priori computation of the relation between the two labelled holomorphic structures.
Forced identification The tautological power algorithm becomes available again. To keep simultaneous assignments, q becomes confused with qlambda n for indeterminate n.
IUT solution route Keep distinct Frobenius-like labels but compare via etale-like permutation symmetries. This requires the full multiradial machinery; it is not a single-ring simplification.
Do not collapse labels. The source uses this toy model to show why deleting distinct arithmetic-holomorphic labels gives a trivial multiradial representation, not the IUT comparison.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.1(vii) Related: naive q-power model trivial multiradial model label discipline

IUT III Remark 3.11.1: Trivial Multiradial Model

Remark 3.11.1(viii) explains why the forced-identification toy model is only a qualitative toy. The representation by the set of powers {qlambda n} is useful for seeing the logical shape of IPL/SHE, but it is useless for Corollary 3.12 log-volume estimates. It puts indeterminacy in the value-group part while keeping unit groups rigid, whereas IUT does the opposite: value groups remain rigid and unit-group portions carry Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3 through log-Kummer theory.

AspectTrivial modelIUT 3.11/3.12 route
Value group Indeterminacy is allowed in the value group qZ. Value-group portions of the relevant F-prime-strips are held rigid.
Unit group Unit groups are held rigid and do not carry the relevant indeterminacies. Unit portions of F⊢×mu-prime-strips carry Ind1, Ind2, and Ind3.
Ring structure Only the multiplicative value-group structure is used; additive issues are ignored. Log-Kummer uses the intertwining between additive and multiplicative structures.
Labels Label deletion creates a trivial family of powers. Distinct Frobenius-like Hodge-theater labels are retained; etale-like permutation symmetries mediate comparison.
Formalization consequence. The toy model is useful only as a negative control. It should not be used as the semantic content of Theorem 3.11 or Corollary 3.12.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.1(viii) Related: distinct-labels tradeoff Ind1/Ind2/Ind3 Remark 3.12.2 intertwining

IUT III Remark 3.11.2: Label Discipline

Remark 3.11.2 explains why the theorem uses all labels in the finite multiplicative/additive Teichmuller systems, while the mono-analytic procession index sets are "naked" finite sets determined only by cardinality. This distinction is what makes the procession layer suitable for a multiradial representation.

Remark 3.11.2 symmetries to labels to processions
PointSource warningWiki consequence
All labels The transitions require all labels in F_l or |F_l|; arbitrary omissions are not allowed. Do not simplify the representation by dropping inconvenient labels.
Arithmetic labels The labels depend on local and global arithmetic fundamental groups. They are arithmetic holomorphic data, not anonymous finite indices.
Procession indices The mono-analytic procession index sets are naked finite sets determined up to isomorphism by cardinality. This loss of arithmetic label history is precisely why they can serve in a multiradial representation.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.2 and Fig. 3.3 Related: 3.11 data etale-picture symmetries multiradiality

IUT III Remark 3.11.3: Single-Link Rule

Remark 3.11.3 starts from a negative fact: the squares of the LGP-Gaussian log-theta lattice are far from commutative. Therefore the unit and value group portions of adjacent vertical lines cannot be compared by two vertically shifted theta-link positions. They must be related through one Theta×mu_LGP link.

Remark 3.11.3(ii) unit and value portions must use one horizontal link

Bi-coric F⊢×mu prime-strips and mono-analytic log-shells are the controlled exception: the lattice squares become effectively commutative for those pieces. The rest of the proof cannot assume ordinary commutativity.

Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.3(i)-(ii) Related: 3.12 single theta-link rule 3.11 horizontal compatibility

IUT III Remark 3.11.3: Log-Kummer Reconciliation

Remark 3.11.3 explains why the final log-Kummer correspondence of Theorem 3.11(ii) is needed. To treat units in the vertically once-shifted way required by LGP monoids, one must relate Frobenius-like units to etale-like structures both by ordinary Kummer isomorphisms and by Kummer isomorphisms pre-composed with a log-link iterate.

NeedMechanismSource consequence
Use one theta link for units and values. Theorem 3.11(iii) horizontal compatibility. Cyclotomes in etale theta/Kummer theory are insulated from Aut(F⊢×mu) indeterminacy.
Treat units via bi-coric log-shells. Kummer maps pre-composed with a log-link iterate. Frobenius-like data must be related to etale-like data invariantly under log-link pre-composition.
Relate adjacent LGP monoids. The total log-Kummer correspondence of Theorem 3.11(ii). This reconciles the single-link constraint with the vertical shift demanded by the LGP construction.
This is a central 3.12 guardrail: the log-Kummer correspondence is not decorative infrastructure. It is the mechanism that makes the single-link theta comparison compatible with bi-coric log-shell treatment of LGP monoids.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.3(iii)-(iv) Related: total log-Kummer correspondence 3.5 global totality 3.10 3.12 proof steps

IUT III Remark 3.11.4: Invisible Indeterminacies

Remark 3.11.4 explains the compatibility in Theorem 3.11(iii)(c),(d) after forgetting Frobenius-like labels. Roots-of-unity multiplication and certain cyclotomic-rigidity ambiguities are "invisible" only because the objects under consideration are formulated up to the relevant orbits or whole sets stabilized by these actions.

ObjectA priori ambiguityWhy it is invisible
Theta values in data (b) Multiplication by 2l-th roots of unity. The theory is already invariant under this root-of-unity action.
Number-field copies in data (c) Root-of-unity multiplication, and possible ±1 cyclotomic ambiguity. The associated Frobenioids use local data and the entire stabilized underlying set, not chosen elements.
Local nonarchimedean log-shell containers Cyclotomic-rigidity ambiguity. Geometric and arithmetic cyclotomes are never confused, so no vicious circle/loop arises.
"Invisible" does not mean absent. It means the source construction has chosen objects on which these ambiguities have no substantive effect.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.4(i) and Figs. 3.4-3.5 Related: roots-of-unity non-interference 3.11 indeterminacies

IUT III Remark 3.11.4: No Further Indeterminacies

Remark 3.11.4(ii) claims completeness of the listed log-Kummer indeterminacies in the final compatibility statement of Theorem 3.11(iii)(c),(d). The reason given is the general Kummer fact that such isomorphisms are determined by cyclotome isomorphisms and the Galois action on roots of monoid elements.

Determining dataStatus in Remark 3.11.4Consequence
Cyclotome isomorphisms The possible effects are exactly those analyzed in Remark 3.11.4(i). No unlisted cyclotomic ambiguity remains.
Galois action on roots of monoid elements Compatibility with the relevant Galois or arithmetic fundamental groups is tautological. No additional indeterminacy can arise from the root-action side.
Ind1 and Ind2 versus data (b),(c) Multiradiality/radial-coric decoupling makes Ind1 and Ind2 ineffective on the geometric containers underlying data (b),(c). Ind3 remains the explicit upper-semi issue for local packet data (a); invisible analogues for (b),(c) are already accounted for.
Sources: IUT III, Remark 3.11.4(ii)-(iii) Related: invisible indeterminacies 3.12 proof steps 3.12 Step (x)

Multiradiality, SHE, IPL, APT

Multiradial algorithms are meant to make sense from the viewpoint of an alien arithmetic holomorphic structure. In IUT III this is achieved by replacing holomorphic prime-strip data with mono-analytic processions, tensor packets of log-shells, and controlled indeterminacies.

From labeled theta data to multiradial output processions, tensor packets, LGP monoids, and source obligations
IUT III §2 source item Multiradial theta-monoid content Formal consequence
Proposition 2.1(i) From the D- and D⊢-prime-strips of a Theta±ellNF-Hodge theater, functorial algorithms construct Psi_env(D>), infty Psi_env(D>), and D_env(D⊢_>). Vertical log-link poly-isomorphisms transport these data. Theta-environment monoids are vertically coric before the §3 tensor-packet construction starts.
Proposition 2.1(ii) Kummer isomorphisms identify the Frobenioid-theoretic Psi_Fenv(HT_Theta), infty Psi_Fenv(HT_Theta), and C_env(HT_Theta) with the D-theoretic environment data. This is the Frobenius-like to etale-like passage for theta-environment monoids.
Proposition 2.1(iii)-(vi) At bad primes, mono-theta Kummer classes and exterior cyclotome isomorphisms yield a composite map to Psi(F⊢_△)×μ that is zero; good and archimedean places use labeled Kummer semi-simplification isomorphisms. These are compatible with constant-monoid identifications. The zero-map/torsion insulation is the local mechanism that keeps mono-theta rigidity compatible with F⊢×μ automorphisms.
Remark 2.1.1 The proof-critical mono-theta properties are cyclotomic rigidity, discrete rigidity, constant multiple rigidity, isomorphism class compatibility, and Frobenioid structure compatibility. The wiki treats "rigidity" as a five-part source package, not a single informal stability assertion.
Theorem 2.2(i) Automorphism groups of F-prime-strips map to automorphisms of associated F×μ and then F⊢×μ prime-strips, compatibly with the Kummer isomorphisms of Proposition 2.1 and Theorem 1.5. Multiradiality must account for the exact automorphism route through the weakened prime-strip interfaces.
Theorem 2.2(ii) At bad primes, the identity automorphism on the theta-splitting objects, exterior cyclotome torsion, mono-theta environments, and zero-labeled splittings is compatible with arbitrary Aut(F⊢×μ_△) actions. This is the local Kummer-compatible multiradiality statement for theta monoids.
Corollary 2.3 A radial environment packages D-Theta±ellNF data, the associated F_env(D>), bad-prime rigidity data, F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△), and the full poly-isomorphism from environment to constant side. The radial algorithm maps this to coric data and is full and essentially surjective. This is the §2 etale-picture of radial data: spokes are radial data and the center is the common coric F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△) output.

Vertical coric theta monoids

Proposition 2.1(i) reconstructs theta-environment monoids and global realified Frobenioids from D-theater data and shows that vertical log-link poly-isomorphisms transport them compatibly.

This is the §2 reason the theta monoid layer can be read along a vertical line of the log-theta lattice.

Proposition 2.1(i)vertical coric

Theta-monoid Kummer isomorphisms

Proposition 2.1(ii) supplies Kummer isomorphisms from the Frobenioid-theoretic theta-environment monoids to the D-theoretic environment monoids, including the associated global realified Frobenioids.

These isomorphisms are the controlled Frobenius-like to etale-like passage for the theta environment.

Proposition 2.1(ii)Kummer

Bad-prime zero map

Proposition 2.1(iii) and Theorem 2.2(ii) isolate the bad-prime mechanism: torsion/exterior-cyclotome data maps trivially into the relevant ×μ quotient, so the identity on mono-theta rigidity data remains compatible with arbitrary F⊢×μ_△ automorphisms.

This is the source-level insulation of cyclotomic rigidity from the Z^×-type indeterminacies.

Proposition 2.1(iii)Theorem 2.2(ii)

Mono-theta rigidity package

Remark 2.1.1 names five inputs from mono-theta theory: cyclotomic rigidity, discrete rigidity, constant multiple rigidity, isomorphism class compatibility, and Frobenioid structure compatibility.

The §2 multiradial argument depends on this package, especially on cyclotomic rigidity and isomorphism class compatibility with log-link basepoints.

Remark 2.1.1rigidity

Radial environment

Corollary 2.3 packages D-Theta±ellNF data, theta-environment F-prime-strips, bad-prime rigidity data, and the F⊢×μ_△ output into a radial environment.

The radial algorithm forgets to the coric pair (D⊢_△, F⊢×μ_△(D⊢_△)).

Corollary 2.3radial data

Etale-picture of radial data

Corollary 2.3 identifies radial data along vertical lines, then uses horizontal poly-isomorphisms to form a common coric center. The resulting etale-picture admits arbitrary permutation symmetries among spokes.

This extends IUT II's D-theater etale-picture to the theta-monoid multiradial setting.

Corollary 2.3(ii)etale-picture

Procession reduction

IUT III replaces the full label set by nested containers S_{j+1}^{±} = {0,...,j}. This reduces the label ambiguity from all independent label choices to procession automorphisms and keeps the core label 0 insulated from later label indeterminacy.

Tensor packets

For each valuation of Q, the construction forms direct sums of log-shells over valuations above it, then tensor products over the elements of S_{j+1}^{±}. These packets are the mono-analytic containers for LGP monoids and labeled copies of F_mod^x.

LGP monoids

Logarithmic Gaussian procession monoids are the log-shell-theoretic versions of the Gaussian monoids from IUT II. Their splitting monoids act on tensor packets and later feed the log-volume estimates in Corollary 3.12.

Alien viewpoint

The final representation forgets the arithmetic holomorphic structure of a specific vertical line and is formulated in terms meaningful from a horizontally related, non-ring/scheme-theoretic Hodge theater.

TermSource meaningFormalization burden
Multiradial Constructed from mono-analytic data so it is meaningful from alien holomorphic viewpoints. Represent the processions, log-shell tensor packets, and permitted indeterminacies.
IPL Output data remains linked to the fixed input q-pilot prime-strip through full poly-isomorphisms of F-prime-strips. Prove the output/input relation, not merely a shared label or postulated comparison.
SHE The construction and its output are simultaneously valid relative to the domain and codomain holomorphic structures of the theta link. Track which operations are executable in both holomorphic structures while the q-pilot value is held fixed.
APT Algorithmic parallel transport: not a direct set-map on regions, but an algorithm valid on both sides up to controlled indeterminacy. Model the algorithmic transport and its allowable hull/Ind1/Ind2/Ind3 losses explicitly.
The source text warns against reading APT as ordinary transport of a set-theoretic region. The output may be a family of possible regions; comparison with the q-pilot requires further passage through holomorphic hulls, determinants, log-volumes, and the log-Kummer correspondence.
Sources: IUT III IUT IV 2026 formalization report Higuchi 2026 Related: RIMS Lean effort indeterminacies tensor/log-volume corridor LANA

Tensor Packets and Log-Volume Corridor

IUT III §3 converts the bridge and Gaussian machinery into containers on which log-volumes can be computed. The corridor runs through tensor packets, splitting monoids of LGP-monoids, the log-Kummer correspondence, holomorphic hulls, and finally the procession-normalized log-volume comparison of Corollary 3.12.

Theorem 3.11 interface containers, correspondences, hulls, and log-volume
IUT III sourceObject introducedPrecise role in the corridor
Proposition 3.1 Local holomorphic tensor packets from F-prime-strip capsules. Creates the local tensor containers and the holomorphic integral structures used before mono-analytic comparison.
Remark 3.1.1 Normalized weighted log-volume formalism for tensor packets. Fixes the weight conventions and warns that the weighted sum is not generally the logarithm of an ordinary measure.
Proposition 3.2 Local mono-analytic tensor packets from D⊢-prime-strip capsules. Transfers the packet construction to the log-shell side and records compatibility with holomorphic packets via poly-isomorphisms.
Proposition 3.3 Global tensor packet (†M_mod)_A. Connects global labeled fields to products of local packets through log-shifted localization maps.
Proposition 3.4 Local packet-theoretic Frobenioids and logarithmic Gaussian procession monoids. Builds the LGP monoids that act on tensor-packet containers and feed the later log-Kummer correspondence.
Proposition 3.5 Vertically coric local LGP monoids and upper semi-compatibility. Replaces a single incompatible Kummer isomorphism by the totality of Kummer isomorphisms pre-composed with vertical log-link iterates.
Example 3.6 Concrete models FMOD and Fmod for the global Frobenioid. Separates the multiplicative rational-function torsor model from the additive local fractional-ideal model.
