Brief report, August 2023 Mochizuki distinguishes nonmathematical refusal to accept IUT from mathematically formulated objections, and cites the Lepage discussions as an example of objections becoming precise enough to evaluate. The wiki's neutral stance is to preserve exact source claims and exact objections without turning either into authority-by-consensus.
Report, October 2025 §2.1 The report rejects the idea that IUT contains intrinsic contradictions in its setup, and says that Lean should be able to formalize the falsity of such claims when they are made as precise statements. This supports the wiki's formalization-first framing: a contradiction claim should become a typed theorem or countertheorem.
Report, October 2025 §2.2 The report treats set-theoretic copies and universes as ordinary mathematical phenomena, comparing them with SGA1 profinite fundamental groups and derived-functor constructions. This page links that discussion back to Hodge-theater/copy pages and to Lean universe management, while keeping IUT-specific definitions in IUT I-III.
Report, October 2025 §2.4 The report argues that anabelian geometry, the multiradial representation, and the Diophantine abc output are mathematically linked, not separable layers of rhetoric. This is context for the Collas, LANA, and GT pages. The actual proof dependencies remain tracked through the IUT source pages.
Report, October 2025 §3 Lean is presented as an ideal universal colleague and as the natural next step for communicating IUT across narrow specializations. The report cites Lean4 formalization of SGA1-style constructions as evidence that nested universes are not a fundamental obstruction. This complements the RIMS Lean page: the wiki should make each concept precise enough to become a Lean-facing interface later.

Accountability framing

The 2023 report asks that objections to IUT be formulated as explicit mathematical documents with precise statements and complete proofs. The wiki follows the same principle by distinguishing source claims, critiques, and context reports.

2023 reportcontext

Set-theoretic copies

The 2025 report treats labelled copies and universes as familiar from classical constructions such as Galois-category fundamental groups and derived functors. IUT's novelty is the type of weakened data compared, not the mere existence of copies.

2025 §2.2universes

Lean no-obstruction signal

Mochizuki reports that Lean4's universe apparatus and recent SGA1-related formalizations suggest no fundamental Lean obstruction to formalizing IUT at the level of set-theoretic copies and universes.

2025 §3.2Lean

Anabelian and Diophantine link

The 2025 report argues that the anabelian and multiradial aspects of IUT are not detachable from the Diophantine abc/Szpiro output. The wiki treats this as motivation for cross-linking anabelian pages with the IUT IV output route.

2025 §2.4context

Universal colleague

The 2025 report describes Lean-style formalization as a way to address an ideal universal colleague when ordinary exposition depends on scarce specialist overlap.

2025 §3communication

Context boundary

These reports do not replace IUT I-IV, the explicit-estimates paper, or formal proof obligations. Their role in the wiki is to orient readers and identify contested interface questions.

wiki boundarynot theorem source
Reading rule: use the situation reports to understand Mochizuki's framing of accountability, copies, universes, and Lean. Use IUT I-IV and the supporting technical papers for mathematical definitions and theorem dependencies.
Sources: 2023 brief report 2025 current situation report Related: RIMS Lean effort Scholze-Stix and responses basepoints/inter-universality Collas category view IUT IV output route

Scholze-Stix and Responses

The Scholze-Stix note focuses on the proof-critical comparison near IUT III, Corollary 3.12. The wiki treats this as an important page, not as the main theme of IUT. The theory itself remains the primary object.

Scholze-Stix objection

Their simplified model argues that the comparison needed for the abc application cannot be obtained as claimed; in their reading, the attempted passage loses the numerical content required for the inequality.

critiqueCorollary 3.12

Mochizuki-side response

Mochizuki and Hoshi maintain that the simplification remembers or identifies structures in a way that violates the intended IUT boundary: Hodge-theater histories are weakened before comparison, and this is essential.

responseHodge theaters
IssueScholze-Stix emphasisMochizuki-side emphasisWiki treatment
Real-line copies All identifications of ordered one-dimensional real vector spaces must be explicit and consistent. Different arithmetic holomorphic structures must not be collapsed before the permitted weakening. Track real-line charts, source histories, and charted inequalities as separate interfaces.
j^2 weights With consistent identifications, the weights that would give the nontrivial inequality disappear or create monodromy. The relevant weighted theta data is handled through the IUT label and indeterminacy machinery. Do not model weights as ordinary scalar equalities unless the source route supplies that chart.
Indeterminacy Blurring by indeterminacy is not enough if it does not produce a numerical inequality. Indeterminacies are part of the legitimate comparison apparatus, especially after weakening history. Separate equality-like invariance from one-sided upper-semi data and final numerical membership.

March 2018 discussions

Mochizuki's 2019 report records the Kyoto discussions of 15-20 March 2018 among Scholze, Stix, Hoshi, and Mochizuki. It frames the disagreement as centered on proposed simplifications that identify objects Mochizuki says must remain distinct.

2018 reportsource context

A/B model

The 2019 report gives a rough model with positive real numbers A and B: the theta-link imposes a relation like -2B=-A, while the multiradial theorem gives a separate inequality. Mochizuki says setting A=B is an extraneous simplification.

2019 report §1model

2025 situation report

Mochizuki's October 2025 report returns to set-theoretic copies, universes, and identification issues, and again argues that Lean is important because it can record the intended logical structure independently of social disagreement.

2025 reportLean context
Formalization reframes the dispute into exact interfaces: what source data is present, which histories are erased, which transports are allowed, and which numerical bridge is actually proved.
Sources: Scholze-Stix note 2018 discussion report 2025 current situation report Related: Scholze-Stix critique page Mochizuki response page Corollary 3.12 corridor

Scholze-Stix Critique

Scholze and Stix isolate the comparison near IUT III Corollary 3.12 as the decisive point for the abc application. Their note argues that after a simplification to the relevant real-vector-space and labeling data, the proposed comparison no longer yields the nontrivial numerical inequality required downstream.

Critique route consistent charts, weights, and loss of nontrivial comparison
Critique pointContentWiki/formalization consequence
Explicit charts The comparison of one-dimensional ordered real vector spaces must specify the identifications used to compare quantities. Keep chart data separate from the ordered-real inequality target.
Weights In the simplified reading, the factors intended to produce a nontrivial inequality either disappear under consistent identification or produce incompatible monodromy. Do not hide weight transport inside a generic indeterminacy label.
Numerical bridge Blurring or quotienting alone is not enough unless it supplies the final numerical membership or inequality. Separate source construction, common container, and final inequality proof.
Neutral reading. This page records the critique as a precise formalization challenge. It does not assume the simplification is legitimate; that question is exactly what the source-faithful 3.11 to 3.12 route must test.
Sources: Scholze-Stix note Related: Mochizuki response page single theta link 3.12 membership step (xi-f)

Mochizuki Response

Mochizuki's response, including the 2019 report on the March 2018 discussions and later situation reports, says that the simplification used by Scholze and Stix identifies structures that IUT deliberately keeps separate. The response centers on weakened Hodge-theater histories, log-Kummer transport, and the controlled indeterminacies used before making the final numerical comparison.

Response route do not collapse histories before the IUT comparison permits it
Response pointContentWiki/formalization consequence
Non-identification Hodge-theater and set-theoretic histories should not be identified merely because later weakened data can be compared. Keep labels, histories, and weakened outputs as distinct fields.
A/B model Mochizuki's report uses a simplified A/B model to argue that imposing equality of the two quantities is an extraneous simplification. Represent equality assumptions separately from IUT-supplied comparison data.
Lean role Later reports argue that formalization can record the intended logical structure and avoid conversational ambiguity. Use Lean and the wiki to expose exact source obligations, not to settle by paraphrase.
Neutral reading. This page records the response as a source-discipline requirement: if the Scholze-Stix simplification collapses data that IUT says must remain separated, the formalization should make that collapse visible as an additional assumption.
Sources: 2018 discussion report 2025 current situation report Related: Scholze-Stix critique page Collas audit guide coarse space step (x)

RIMS Lean Effort

Mochizuki's April 2026 progress report frames Lean formalization partly as a communication tool. It proposes a staged path toward a LeanForm of IUT, with early attention on the logical structure around the route from Theorem 3.11, through a reorganized "3.11.5", to Corollary 3.12.

Formalization target source route decomposed into Lean-facing obligations

For this repository, the practical lesson is to keep every bridge explicit: if a common container is supplied, Lean should still ask what numerical chart, transport, calibration, or bound converts it into the final signed inequality.

Communication role

Mochizuki's report emphasizes LeanForm not only as verification but also as a precise communication medium. The stated aim is to expose the logical structure of IUT in a form that can be checked without relying on informal agreement about hidden identifications.

LeanFormcommunication

Reported status

The report says the project is in the early skeletal part of Stage 1. It also says the code discussed there is not yet public-release-ready: its communicative success still depends on the recipient already knowing the basic IUT setup and the main issues.

early Stage 1skeletal

Species without full ZFC

The report treats species/mutations as the conceptual language of functorial algorithms, but concludes that a full Lean formalization of ZFC as a first-order theory is probably unnecessary for Stages 1-2. Standard Lean records and componentwise data can express the needed early logic.

Stages 1-2species

ZFC as later infrastructure

Mochizuki notes that a LeanForm of ZFC as a first-order theory would make abstract species/mutations more direct, but records the working conclusion that standard Lean data structures should suffice for the early Stage 1-2 logical formalization.

ZFClater stage

3.11.5 convention

The label "3.11.5" is introduced as a Lean-facing decomposition, not as a theorem number in IUT I-IV. It moves the hull-and-determinant work before the final 3.12 comparison so the last step can focus on simultaneous comparison in a common container.

3.11.5hull + det

APT and IPL

The report identifies algorithmic parallel transport and input prime-strip linkage as central obligations before the final signed inequality. The wiki therefore treats APT/IPL as source obligations, not as cosmetic labels on an already available ordered-real proof.

APTIPL

Formal blackbox decomposition

The report says the technical strategy is to reorganize IUT into purely formal or combinatorial blackboxes. For the wiki this means each page should distinguish an assumed blackbox interface from the source theorem that is eventually supposed to construct it.

blackboxesLeanForm

N-th power model

The report reviews the elementary model where the N-th power map is an isomorphism of multiplicative monoids but not a ring homomorphism. This models why a common multiplicative/group-theoretic input does not automatically identify the two ring structures being compared.

§3 reviewmonoid not ring

Common container

In the report's review, the multiradial representation is a common container built from common monoid and abstract-group data. It is meant to express selected portions of distinct arithmetic holomorphic structures without treating those ring structures as identical.

multiradialcontainer

Four-triangle route

Section 4 decomposes the route from Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12 into four commuting triangles: multiradial algorithm, descent, hull plus determinant, and the SHE-restricted comparison of q-pilot and theta-pilot data.

§43.11 to 3.12

Stage 2 work signal

The report says the RIMS group has skeletal Lean code for the final comparison triangle and is making progress on code for the second and third triangles, where APT is central. This places the public RIMS effort at early Stage 1 with active movement toward Stage 2 obligations.

APTStage 2
StageMochizuki's stated targetWiki/formalization reading
1 IUT III Theorem 3.11 implies Corollary 3.12. Primary public dispute corridor; our Lean work separates source obligations from ordered-real algebra.
2 Proof of Theorem 3.11 modulo IUT I-II. Construct the multiradial output, APT/IPL behavior, and source side of the corridor.
3 IUT I-II modulo earlier anabelian, Frobenioid, and theta-function results. Replace record-level Hodge-theater and theta-link interfaces by constructions from the source machinery.
4 Earlier work from 1995-2015. Long-range mathlib-scale formalization of p-adic/anabelian/Frobenioid background.
5 Numerical aspects from IUT IV and Explicit Estimates. Formalize the effective estimate layer after the source corridor is mathematically pinned down.
Sources: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report Essential Logical Structure Related: RIMS Lean stages four-triangle route LANA species and mutations Corollary 3.12

LeanForm Communication

Mochizuki's 2026 report frames LeanForm not only as machine verification, but as a communication tool. The stated aim is to make the logical structure of the final IUT corridor precise, machine-checkable, and resistant to false interpretations. The wiki records this as a source claim about the role of formalization, not as a mathematical proof by itself.

Verification plus communication formalization report §1
Status. The report says the available Lean code is skeletal and not yet public-release quality. The wiki should therefore describe the communication strategy and staged obligations without implying that a complete formal proof has already been released.
Sources: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §1 Related: RIMS Lean effort RIMS Lean stages four-triangle route

LeanForm Species and Mutations

The 2026 report says that a faithful formalization should begin with the basic definitions of the theory. For IUT this means species and mutations, viewed as the formal version of functorial algorithms. The report also says a full LeanForm of ZFC as a first-order theory would make this abstract treatment natural, but is probably unnecessary for early Stages 1 and 2.

Species/mutations as formalization discipline formalization report §2
Report itemMeaningWiki consequence
Species Type of object defined by a set-theoretic formula, independent of a chosen ZFC model. Link to IUT IV §3 and keep type information explicit.
Mutation Formula-defined construction from one species to another. Model formalization interfaces as structured transformations, not labels.
ZFC LeanForm Useful for arbitrary formulas, but not required for early Stage 1-2 work. Do not block the wiki or Lean project on full set-theory formalization.
Sources: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §2 Related: species vs categories mutation histories/cores RIMS Lean stages

LeanForm Vicious Cycles

In §1 of the 2026 report, Mochizuki frames LeanForm as a way to break a communication cycle: readers want confidence before investing in IUT, but gaining rigorous confidence requires studying IUT in detail. The report's claim is that a machine-checkable logical record can provide a precise synopsis without relying on informal social trust.

Cycle componentReport contentWiki reading
Simple logic claim Mochizuki says the logic of the disputed final portions is simple and elementary when understood correctly. The wiki should make the exact logical interfaces inspectable instead of relying on prose reassurance.
Motivation barrier Mathematicians may be reluctant to study all of IUT without prior confidence in correctness. A source map should let readers enter the theory gradually without pretending the proof is already settled.
Detail requirement The report says genuine rigorous understanding still requires detailed study of IUT I-IV. The wiki goal remains full source coverage, not only a high-level synopsis.
Machine-checkable record LeanForm is proposed as a precise, fast, nonnegatable record of logical structure. The wiki should separate checked Lean claims from still-supplied source assumptions.
This page records a communication claim from the report. It is not evidence that the mathematical proof is complete; it explains why the RIMS project prioritizes the 3.11 to 3.12 corridor.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §1 Related: LeanForm communication four-triangle route Scholze-Stix critique

LeanForm Blackbox Decomposition

The 2026 report says the technical essence of LeanForm for IUT is a reorganization of the theory into suitable purely formal or combinatorial blackboxes. This is a staging device: a blackbox interface can make the route logic precise while still marking the source theorem or construction that must eventually fill the blackbox.

Source mathematics

IUT I-IV and prerequisite papers provide the intended mathematical constructions.

Blackbox interface

Lean-facing records expose the inputs, outputs, and compatibility laws needed by a route.

Checked route logic

The formal proof can verify what follows once the blackbox obligations are supplied.

Report pointMeaningAudit consequence
Formal/combinatorial blackboxes Repackage parts of IUT into interfaces suited to Lean checking. Every wiki page should distinguish the interface from the source construction.
Final portion focus The report says §4 details this reorganization for the final public-dispute corridor. Stage 1 pages should not claim to formalize all of IUT.
Skeletal status The report says the code remains skeletal and depends on recipient background. Mark RIMS code status as early and communication-oriented, not public complete.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §§1,4 Related: four-triangle route RIMS Lean effort 3.12 membership step

LeanForm ZFC/Mathlib Gap

In §2, Mochizuki explains why abstract species/mutations would be natural after formalizing ZFC as a first-order theory: one could then quantify over an indeterminate set-theoretic formula. He reports learning that Shogo Saito had achieved such a Lean formalization, while Mathlib did not yet contain one.

LayerReport statementWiki consequence
Species/mutations They may be viewed as a refinement of categories/functors, or as internal categories/functors in the syntactic category of ZFC. This ties the 2026 report directly to IUT IV §3 species pages.
Indeterminate formulas A first-order ZFC formalization is needed to work with arbitrary formulas, not only formulas natively available in Lean. Abstract species support is a later foundational target.
Shogo Saito The report says such a LeanForm had recently been achieved by Shogo Saito. Record this as a report claim; the wiki should later link source code if it becomes available.
Mathlib gap The report says Mathlib did not yet have this first-order ZFC LeanForm. Do not assume standard Mathlib already provides abstract species machinery.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §2 Related: IUT IV ZFCG universes LeanForm species/mutations LANA

LeanForm Stage 1-2 Interface Decision

After consulting Lean experts, Mochizuki reports that a full abstract formalization of species/mutations via first-order ZFC is probably not needed for Stages 1-2. Instead, standard Lean mechanisms for types of mathematical objects and relationships between component data should suffice for the initial logical formalization.

DecisionReport contentProject consequence
Do not wait for full ZFC Full ZFC-as-first-order-theory support is theoretically desirable but not required for early stages. The wiki and Lean project can continue with structured interfaces.
Use standard Lean data Types, records, and componentwise relationships can express the early IUT logic. This matches our current approach of explicit source obligations and typed corridor records.
Scope limitation The claim is made for at least Stages 1-2, especially the code discussed in §4. Do not generalize this to all of IUT or all prerequisite foundations.
Communication lesson The absence of Mathlib ZFC support is read as reflecting how algorithmic construction viewpoints are underrepresented in existing formalized mathematics. The wiki should keep algorithmic reconstruction as a named concept, not an implicit background habit.
This page explains why the RIMS-style Stage 1 interface can be record-based without contradicting the source's species/mutation foundations. It is a pragmatic staging claim, not a denial that full species foundations matter.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §2 Related: RIMS Lean stages four-triangle route IUT IV mutations

Mochizuki 2026 LeanForm Stage Plan

Mochizuki's April 2026 report proposes a five-stage formalization path. The stated immediate focus is Stage 1 and Stage 2: first the implication from IUT III Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12, then the proof of Theorem 3.11 modulo IUT I-II. The later stages move backward into the anabelian, Frobenioid, theta-function, and numerical source layers.

Five-stage LeanForm plan formalization report, §2
StageReport targetWiki reading
Stage 1 IUT III Theorem 3.11 implies Corollary 3.12. Most public dispute focus; should remain a typed corridor, not a proof-completion claim.
Stage 2 Proof of Theorem 3.11 modulo IUT I-II. Where APT, IPL, multiradial representation, and log-Kummer source obligations become central.
Stage 3 IUT I-II from earlier anabelian, Frobenioid, and theta-function results. Replace blackbox Hodge-theater/theta-link interfaces by source constructions.
Stage 4 Formalize earlier background papers from roughly 1995-2015. Long-range library work, close to LANA's anabelian-library orientation.
Stage 5 Numerical aspects of IUT IV and Explicit Estimates. Downstream effective estimates once the disputed comparison corridor is pinned down.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report, §2 Related: RIMS Lean effort four-triangle route LANA species versus categories

Mochizuki 2026: Four-Triangle Route from 3.11 to 3.12

The report reorganizes the implication from Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12 into four Lean-facing triangles. The final triangle is described as "3.11.5 => 3.12", where "3.11.5" is a bookkeeping name for Theorem 3.11 plus the hull-and-determinant operation moved before the final simultaneous comparison.

Four-triangle decomposition formalization report, §4
Report componentMeaningWiki consequence
Descent arrow (dsc) Part of the route from Theorem 3.11 output toward the comparison stage. Do not fold descent into the final inequality without naming it.
APT Algorithmic parallel transport for expressing gluings up to indeterminacies. Central to the first/second/third triangles and to Stage 2 work.
IPL Input prime-strip link property connecting input BPS data and multiradial output data. Needed both before and at the final comparison interface.
Hull + determinant Moved into the intermediate "3.11.5" bookkeeping target. Separates construction of the comparison object from the final q/Theta comparison.
SHE Simultaneous holomorphic expressibility restricts the final comparison to the q-pilot input case. The final triangle is not a generic ordered-real inequality; it is a specialized container comparison.
The report's "3.11.5" label is not an additional theorem in the IUT papers. It is a Lean-facing decomposition tool. The wiki keeps this convention visible so readers do not mistake reorganization for a new source result.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report, §4 Related: RIMS Lean stages Theorem 3.11 Corollary 3.12 proof steps RIMS Lean effort

LeanForm N-Power Model

In §3 of the 2026 report, Mochizuki uses an elementary N-th power model as a controlled analogue for the IUT comparison problem. For an integral domain with group action and no nontrivial N-th roots of unity, the N-th power map identifies multiplicative monoids with group action, but it is not a ring homomorphism and does not preserve addition.

N-th power model formalization report §3
Model itemFormal readingIUT warning
N-th power map Isomorphism on suitable multiplicative monoid data with group action. Do not read it as a transport of ring structure.
†/‡ copies Distinct ring structures can share multiplicative and abstract group data. The common part is a logical AND layer, not a scheme-theoretic identification.
Modulo p and Faltings examples The report gives these as elementary and height-theoretic guide cases. They orient the final comparison but do not replace the IUT constructions.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §3 Related: GT N-power analogy theta link log link

LeanForm Common Container

The N-power model is used to explain the role of a common container: the multiplicative monoid and abstract group structures are shared, while the arithmetic holomorphic ring structures remain distinct. In IUT the analogous container is the multiradial representation for the Theta-pilot.

Common input

Multiplicative monoid data and abstract group data survive the passage between alien copies.

Algorithmic construction

Anabelian reconstruction, logarithms, theta evaluation, and Kummer theory produce the multiradial representation.

Separated rings

The two arithmetic holomorphic structures are compared through the container, not identified as one ring.

Source phraseWiki interpretationFormalization consequence
Common multiplicative monoid and abstract group structures The shared layer is weaker than ring or scheme data. Lean interfaces must type the common layer separately from ring histories.
Multiradial representation for the Theta-pilot A container built so the Theta-pilot can be expressed from multiple alien viewpoints. Do not replace multiradiality by ordinary equality in one ambient category.
q-pilot versus Theta-pilot The final comparison reads domain-like and image-like data in one comparison environment. This page is the conceptual bridge to the fourth triangle and SHE/IPL pages.
The container is not a hidden global ring. Its point is to make a controlled comparison possible without restoring the ring/scheme history that the IUT links deliberately leave behind.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §3 Related: common-container chain Collas category view fourth triangle

Infinite H Descent

The report explains the multiradial representation as arising from an analytic-continuation-like process along an infinite H in the log-theta lattice. Descent operations gradually introduce fuzzifications corresponding to the named indeterminacies, while log-link invariants juggle additive and multiplicative parts of ring structures.

Descent along the log-theta lattice formalization report §3; Essential Logical Structure §3.3, §3.10, §3.11
OperationReport roleAudit consequence
Log-link invariance Constructs invariants with respect to the log-link. The log-link cannot be treated as incidental to the Theta-link comparison.
Fuzzification Introduces indeterminacies through descent. Indeterminacies should be typed operations, not vague error labels.
Miraculous rotation Relates the log-volume-dilating Theta-link gap to a log-volume-preserving log-link gap. This is a core page for the disputed corridor and for Collas's log-theta wandering view.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §3 Related: Collas log-theta wandering CAT miraculous rotation IUT III Theorem 3.11

APT and IPL in the LeanForm Route

The 2026 report separates the route from Theorem 3.11 to the final comparison into source obligations. The first, second, and third triangles use APT and IPL to pass from descent data to the intermediate "3.11.5" object, while IPL remains relevant at the final comparison boundary.

AbbreviationReport roleWhere it enters
APT Algorithmic parallel transport: expresses gluings up to indeterminacies such as Ind1/2/3 plus hull. Central in the second and third triangles, especially the route to HDD.
IPL Input prime-strip link: relates input BPS data and output data of the multiradial algorithm. Used in the 3.11 to 3.11.5 part and again in the final comparison.
HDD The composite of descent with hull+det. Defines the intermediate object compared with the SHE/q-pilot side.
This page is a staging map, not a proof of APT or IPL. The report says the RIMS group is making progress on skeletal Lean code for APT in the second/third triangles; the source constructions still point back to IUT III.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §4 Related: IPL/SHE/APT source multiradiality four-triangle route

LeanForm Fourth Triangle

The report identifies the fourth triangle as the skeletal-code focus discussed in §1: simultaneous comparison, relative to a single comparison container, of the Theta-pilot side and the q-pilot side. The Theta-pilot corresponds to HDD, or the image side of the N-th power analogy; the q-pilot corresponds to SHE, or the domain side.

Fourth triangle: 3.11.5 to 3.12 SHE + IPL comparison
SideReport identification3.12 relevance
q-pilot Data constructed from etHT by applying SHE; analogous to the domain of the N-th power map. Supplies the comparison side tied to the original q-pilot input.
Theta-pilot Corresponds to HDD, or the image of the N-th power map after descent and hull+det. Supplies the multiradial output side.
Final content Height is equal to N times height up to a small discrepancy from Ind1/2/3 plus hull. This is the public-dispute corridor that LeanForm Stage 1 isolates.
Source: Mochizuki 2026 Lean report §4 Related: Corollary 3.12 proof steps SHE common container

LANA

LANA stands for Lean for ANAbelian geometry. ZEN Mathematics Center announced it publicly on 31 March 2026 as an international project involving ZEN University, Utrecht University, and the University of Alberta. Its first aim is Lean formalization of anabelian geometry; its second aim is formal verification of IUT.

LANA route public status as checked 2026-06-01
Public sourceReported contentWiki consequence
Project homepage The homepage gives the acronym and states the primary goal as Lean digitization of definitions and results from anabelian geometry. Treat LANA first as an anabelian-library project, not only as a direct IUT checker.
ZEN announcement The 31 March 2026 announcement presents the project as an international collaboration aiming at anabelian formalization and IUT verification. The wiki records the institutional scope and keeps links to the public source rather than inferring unpublished technical details.
ZMC remarks Kato, Commelin, Topaz, Kedlaya, and Hoshi describe a neutral stance, bootcamp-style study, Lean foundations, and a repeatedly encountered point around Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12. This is directly relevant to the wiki's 3.12 corridor pages, but it should be reported as public project status rather than as a mathematical result.

Core members reported by ZEN

Fumiharu Kato, Johan Commelin, Kiran Kedlaya, Yuichiro Hoshi, and Adam Topaz, with additional junior participants.

Status as of public announcement

ZEN reports work since autumn 2023, a neutral stance, substantial clarity about understood and unresolved points, and an interim progress report planned for 17 July 2026.

Anabelian library first

The public remarks emphasize formalizing principal theorems of anabelian geometry and building a Lean library, including foundations such as Galois categories and fundamental groups, before using that base for IUT.

Unresolved point policy

The LANA remarks state that the team has repeatedly encountered a specific IUT point, can articulate it with enough precision to target Lean, but has not concluded whether the remaining obstacle is a mathematical gap or a limit of current understanding.

Neutral verification stance

Kato's public remarks describe LANA as aiming neither to defend nor reject IUT in advance. The stated task is to formalize enough of the theory to make the unresolved points precise and then continue the dialogue with Mochizuki.

2026 announcementneutral

Human understanding first

The announcement stresses that IUT cannot be treated as a mechanical translation task. The team reports bootcamps in Japan, Canada, the Netherlands, and the United States, aimed at building shared mathematical understanding before and during formalization.

bootcampsIUT study

Lean is not generative AI

Commelin's remarks distinguish Lean verification from generative AI prediction: Lean checks formal proofs against logical rules, while humans remain responsible for choosing definitions and ensuring that statements match the intended mathematics.

Leanverification

Repeated wall near 3.11 to 3.12

Topaz and Hoshi both describe a repeatedly encountered point in the logic around Theorem 3.11 to Corollary 3.12. Publicly, LANA has not classified this point as either a proof gap or a limitation of present understanding.

3.11 to 3.12unresolved
LANA is independent from the RIMS Lean effort, but public materials indicate dialogue with Mochizuki and a focus on making points of contention precise enough for Lean. The public status recorded here was rechecked against the ZEN/ZMC online pages on 1 June 2026.
Online sources: Project LANA homepage ZEN announcement Kato/ZMC remarks Related: RIMS Lean effort

Source Library

Local PDF links open from the repository, so the wiki remains usable offline. The ten official Mochizuki IUT-category papers and the four preparatory papers from the IUT Summit source list are listed separately in the corpus audits; this library also includes auxiliary surveys, critiques, and formalization-adjacent papers.

IUT I: Construction of Hodge Theaters

Shinichi Mochizuki, May 2020, 186 pp.

Initial theta data, hyperbolic orbicurves, Frobenioids, etale theta functions, log-shell background, and Hodge theaters.

PDF

IUT II: Hodge-Arakelov-theoretic Evaluation

Shinichi Mochizuki, December 2020, 174 pp.

Kummer theory around Hodge-Arakelov theta evaluation and construction of theta links between theaters.

PDF

IUT III: Canonical Splittings of the Log-Theta-Lattice

Shinichi Mochizuki, May 2020, 199 pp.

Log-theta lattice, multiradial algorithms, Theorem 3.11, and the proof-critical Corollary 3.12 corridor.

PDF

IUT IV: Log-Volume Computations and Set-Theoretic Foundations

Shinichi Mochizuki, April 2020, 87 pp.

Log-volume estimates and their application to Diophantine consequences.

PDF

The Geometry of Frobenioids I

Shinichi Mochizuki, June 2008, 126 pp.

General Frobenioid theory: categorical abstraction of divisors, line bundles, Frobenius degree, divisor monoids, and model Frobenioids.

PDF

The Geometry of Frobenioids II

Shinichi Mochizuki, 72 pp.

Continuation of the Frobenioid framework, including poly-Frobenioid infrastructure used around later prime-strip and Frobenius-like constructions.

PDF

The Etale Theta Function

Shinichi Mochizuki, 112 pp.

Etale theta functions and Frobenioid-theoretic manifestations; core input for theta evaluation and later μ6 modifications.

PDF

Topics in Absolute Anabelian Geometry III

Shinichi Mochizuki, 164 pp.

Absolute anabelian reconstruction input used by the theta-approach and by the group-theoretic reconstruction side of IUT.

PDF

Panoramic Overview

Shinichi Mochizuki, survey, 45 pp.

Accessible survey of the motivation, non-scheme-theoretic deformation, and components of the log-theta lattice.

PDF

IUT Progress Report 2010

Shinichi Mochizuki, RIMS Kyoto 2010, 12 pp.

Early progress-report slides describing the comparison with earlier Teichmuller theories, the log-theta lattice, inter-universality, and expected main results.

Dilation page · expected-results page

PDF

Invitation to IUT, 2012 Version

Shinichi Mochizuki, RIMS Kyoto 2012, 11 pp.

Original invitation lecture notes preceding the expanded and later lecture variants.

PDF

Invitation to IUT, Expanded Version

Shinichi Mochizuki, lecture notes, 15 pp.

Compact pedagogical route through Hodge-Arakelov motivation, Teichmuller deformation, the log-theta lattice, and anabelian inter-universality.

HA obstruction · distortion page · HT bookkeeping · Kummer bridge

PDF

Invitation to IUT, Three-Hour Version

Shinichi Mochizuki, RIMS Kyoto 2014, 19 pp.

Earlier lecture-note variant of the invitation route; kept as an offline reference alongside the expanded version.

PDF

Invitation to IUT, 2+2 Hour Version

Shinichi Mochizuki, Kumamoto 2014, 19 pp.

Longer lecture-note variant of the invitation route, useful for comparing presentation choices across the invitation series.

PDF

Invitation to IUT, 2015 Version

Shinichi Mochizuki, RIMS Kyoto 2015, 19 pp.

Later invitation lecture-note variant from the pre-publication verification period.

PDF

Mutually Alien Copies

Shinichi Mochizuki, 170 pp.

Survey organized around analogies with Gaussian integrals and the role of mutually alien copies.

PDF

Bogomolov's Szpiro Proof from the IUT Viewpoint

Shinichi Mochizuki, January 2016, 24 pp.

Compares IUT with Bogomolov's proof of the geometric Szpiro conjecture, emphasizing shared hyperbolic geometry and differences involving theta functions and Gaussian distributions.

PDF

Classical Roots of IUT

Shinichi Mochizuki, marked-up lecture notes, December 2021, 27 pp.

Four classical entry points into IUT: isogeny-height invariance, crystals/Hodge filtrations, complex Teichmuller theory, and the Jacobi theta identity.

PDF

IUT as an Anabelian Gateway

Shinichi Mochizuki, MFO-RIMS report, 7 pp.

Short report framing IUT as an anabelian gateway from Diophantine geometry toward analytic number theory.

PDF

Anabelian Gateway, MFO-RIMS 2023

Shinichi Mochizuki, September 2023, 12 pp.

MFO-RIMS lecture-note version of the anabelian-gateway route.

PDF

Anabelian Gateway, IUGC 2024

Shinichi Mochizuki, April 2024, 14 pp.

IUGC lecture-note version of the anabelian-gateway route, between the MFO-RIMS and IUT Summit variants.

PDF

Anabelian Gateway, IUT Summit 2025

Shinichi Mochizuki, March 2025, 15 pp.

Expanded talk version emphasizing the N-th power map model, projective-line analogy, and Galois-orbit IUT preview.

PDF

Tripodal Transport

Shinichi Mochizuki, June 2021, 26 pp.

Lecture notes on tripodal transport in arithmetic geometry, adapted from the mutually-alien-copies exposition.

PDF

Overview of Combinatorial Anabelian Geometry

Shinichi Mochizuki, July 2021, 10 pp.

Short lecture-note overview of combinatorial anabelian geometry, relevant to the algorithmic reconstruction background of IUT and LANA.

overview page · tripod synchronization

PDF

Essential Logical Structure

Shinichi Mochizuki, March 2024, 167 pp.

Logical decomposition of IUT in terms of AND, OR, and exclusive alternatives.

PDF

Essential Logical Structure I-V

Shinichi Mochizuki, December 2021, 42 pp.

Earlier marked-up logical-structure notes from global multiplicative subspaces through closed loops, multiradial representations, and holomorphic hulls.

PDF

On the Formalization of IUT

Shinichi Mochizuki, April 2026, 14 pp.

Preliminary LeanForm progress report and staged route around 3.11.5 to 3.12.

PDF

Why abc is still a conjecture

Peter Scholze and Jakob Stix, 10 pp.

The central published critique of the IUT proof of abc, focused near Corollary 3.12.

PDF

Report on Discussions, March 2018

Shinichi Mochizuki, February 2019, 45 pp.

Mochizuki's report on the Kyoto discussions with Scholze, Stix, and Hoshi.

PDF

Brief Current Situation Report

Shinichi Mochizuki, August 2023, 4 pp.

Short situation report emphasizing publication status, continuing misunderstandings, and the request for precise written mathematical objections.

current-situation page

PDF

Current Situation Surrounding IUT

Shinichi Mochizuki, October 2025, 30 pp.

Recent report addressing mathematical misunderstandings, set-theoretic copies, universes, and anabelian developments.

current-situation page

PDF

GT as a Species of Teichmuller Theory

Shinichi Mochizuki, joint work in progress with Tsujimura, October 2025, 14 pp.

Compares classical Teichmuller theory, IUT, and Grothendieck-Teichmuller theory through common-container and anabelian reconstruction patterns.

GT genus-zero quotient · decomposition route

PDF

Teichmuller Dilations of Varying Hues

Shinichi Mochizuki, ICMS 2025 marked-up version, 14 pp.

Compares complex Teichmuller theory, IUT, and GT via common containers, N-th power gluing, CAT idempotent function theory, and GT-side rotation.

CAT rotation · Chebotarev/Hilbertian tools

PDF

Collas: Anabelian Arithmetic Geometry and IUT

Benjamin Collas, revised overview, 24 pp.

Conceptual overview of IUT through Grothendieckian anabelian geometry and the geometry of monoids.

PDF

Collas: Anabelian Perspectives in Galois-Teichmuller Theory

Benjamin Collas, 20 pp.

Context for anabelian reconstruction, Galois-Teichmuller theory, and the algorithmic viewpoint surrounding IUT-adjacent geometry.

PDF

Collas-Murotani-Yamaguchi: Symmetries and Anabelian Geometry

Benjamin Collas, Takahiro Murotani, Naganori Yamaguchi, arXiv 2025, 15 pp.

Compact survey of reconstruction from symmetries, from Galois theory to etale fundamental groups and current anabelian trends.

PDF

Collas-Philip-Yamaguchi: Deligne-Mumford Curves

Benjamin Collas, Severin Philip, Naganori Yamaguchi, May 2026, 36 pp.

Recent anabelian reconstruction paper for Deligne-Mumford curves and their etale fundamental groups; included as current background for the anabelian side of LANA/IUT formalization.

PDF

Hoshi: Introduction to IUT

Yuichiro Hoshi, Japanese survey, 105 pp.

Survey covering cyclotomes, anabelian geometry, core objects, multiradial algorithms, log-shells, and log links.

PDF

Dupuy-Hilado: Initial Theta Data

Taylor Dupuy and Anton Hilado, arXiv v2 June 2025, 20 pp.

Explicit treatment of initial theta data and the hypothesis side of Corollary 3.12.

PDF

Dupuy-Hilado: Probabilistic Szpiro

Taylor Dupuy and Anton Hilado, April 2020, 36 pp.

Derives transparent Szpiro-type inequalities from a probabilistic formulation of Mochizuki's Corollary 3.12, conditional on that input.

PDF

Explicit Estimates in IUT

Mochizuki, Fesenko, Hoshi, Minamide, Porowski, 58 pp.

Official source copy of the explicit estimates paper listed in Mochizuki's IUT corpus.

PDF

Explicit Estimates, WRAP Copy

Fesenko, Hoshi, Minamide, Mochizuki, Porowski, 59 pp.

Repository copy of the accepted-manuscript version of explicit estimates in IUT.

PDF

Pajo: Scale-Free Common Containers

Paul Pajo, April 2026, 14 pp.

Typed no-go theorem showing that a scale-free common container alone does not entail a numerical inequality.

PDF

Higuchi: Formal Negative Result

Joaquim Reizi Higuchi, March 2026, 9 pp.

Countermodel-style analysis of the passage from Step (xi-e) to Step (xi-f) without a numerical bridge axiom.

PDF

Glossary

Short definitions for navigation. Each entry is intentionally compact; follow the internal links for context and source references.

Alien copy
A typed copy of arithmetic data whose history is not identified with the original ring/scheme history.
Anabelian geometry
Reconstruction of arithmetic/geometric objects from fundamental-group-like data; a major prerequisite background for IUT.
APT
Algorithmic parallel transport through IUT gluings, up to specified indeterminacy.
Corollary 3.12
The proof-critical IUT III comparison that supplies the signed log-volume inequality used downstream.
Frobenioid
Categorical packaging of arithmetic line-bundle/divisor/monoid data used in Mochizuki's earlier infrastructure.
Hodge theater
A miniature arithmetic world associated to initial theta data, designed to separate additive and multiplicative structures.
Indeterminacy
Controlled ambiguity allowed by the IUT comparison; in the key corridor, Ind3 is one-sided upper-semi rather than equality.
IPL
Input prime-strip link property tying output possibilities to input prime-strip data at the comparison boundary.
Kummer detachment
The IUT III passage from Frobenius-like objects in the Theta×μ-LGP link to etale-like objects in the multiradial representation, using the full log-Kummer correspondence.
LANA
Lean for ANAbelian geometry, a ZEN Mathematics Center project to formalize anabelian geometry and verify IUT.
Log link
Vertical link in the log-theta lattice, formed via local logarithms and log-shells.
Log-shell
Local logarithmic object associated to units; used to manage additive log data.
Log-theta lattice
Two-dimensional diagram of Hodge theaters whose horizontal arrows are theta links and vertical arrows are log links.
Multiradial
Meaningful from multiple radial or alien holomorphic viewpoints, rather than tied to one ordinary scheme history.
Prime strip
A local/global strip of prime-indexed data used in the IUT comparison machinery.
SHE
Simultaneous holomorphic expressibility, a condition needed to express output data relative to the target holomorphic structure.
Theta link
Horizontal link between Hodge theaters produced from Hodge-Arakelov theta evaluation.
IUT Wiki. Local offline edition updated 1 June 2026. Source PDFs are mirrored in docs/papers/; online project links remain external.
